Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Position Summary

The Senior Data Analyst is responsible for delivering highly visible inbound and outbound EDI projects for the data operations team at Allied. This includes playing a key role in the implementation of new trading partners; configuration of inbound and outbound ANSI X12-834 eligibility files and standard and custom data extracts, maintenance of existing trading partner files, and resolving existing trading partner file issues.

Technology platforms for project delivery: QicLink (system or record), new data integrations tools which include support client eligibility (ANSI X12-834 file format). This includes EDI mapping using internal testing and validation tools, etc. This role requires in-depth knowledge of eligibility data exchange, expert knowledge of ANSI X12-834 file format and reading/understanding file spec/requirement documentation

Essential Functions


Lead outbound file implementation projects end to end, including project initiation, defining timelines, coordinating internal resources, leading or participating in project meetings, and managing ongoing communications with vendors and internal Allied stakeholders.
Own ANSI X12 834 eligibility file implementations, ensuring accurate configuration, testing, and smooth transitions to production.
Lead additional EDI or data extract implementations as required.
Serve as a primary point of contact for eligibility (834) questions, implementations, production issues, and project related updates for both internal teams and external partners.
Independently navigate, interpret, and troubleshoot 834 formatted files, including completing detailed data mapping, supporting file testing, and resolving complex data and formatting issues.
Leverage internal testing, validation, and monitoring tools to execute file testing, document configurations, and maintain a single source of truth for eligibility and EDI files across teams and vendor partners.
Monitor file processing status and performance through eligibility audit reports and other reporting mechanisms, proactively identifying risks, discrepancies, or trends.
Review, investigate, and support resolution of data discrepancies related to eligibility, accumulators, or downstream impacts, coordinating closely with customer service, account management, IT, and vendor/client contacts.
Coordinate with internal teams to ensure implementation milestones and delivery timelines are met, escalating risks or blockers as needed.
Troubleshoot and escalate complex data or file processing issues with internal and external partners, providing clear documentation and recommended next steps.
Support data quality initiatives by following established controls and contributing to the improvement of testing, documentation, and operational processes.
Act as a trusted partner to Data Operations leadership by owning assigned initiatives, providing status updates, and contributing subject matter expertise to broader data management efforts.
Other duties as assigned.


EDUCATION


Bachelor’s degree or equivalent work experience required.


Experience And Skills


3-5+ years of experience working with inbound and outbound ANSI X12 834 EDI files, including expert level proficiency with the X12 834 format
Working knowledge of data management, data warehousing, and coding languages
Required experience with SQL and Microsoft Power BI, along with strong computer skills, including proficiency in the Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, etc.)
Excellent knowledge of data integration programs involving all components including source systems, ETL, data quality/integrity, database, and delivery mechanisms and EDI business processes, practices, and procedures within healthcare environment
Strong work-ethic and positive attitude and exceptional ability to manage multiple projects across functional teams in a fast-paced environment
Strong communication skills (both verbal and written) and an ability to provide recommendations on data integrations and applications
Strategic, conceptual analytical thinking and decision-making skills.
Ability to advise on complex technical matters to all stakeholders; ability to communicate effectively with management.


POSITION COMPETENCIES


Accountability
Analytical Thinking
Collaboration
Communication
Customer Focus
Functional Expertise
Initiative


PHYSICAL DEMANDS


This is a standard desk role requiring extended sitting and computer work.


WORK ENVIROMENT


Remote


Here at Allied, we believe that great talent can thrive from anywhere. Our remote friendly culture offers flexibility and the comfort of working from home, while also ensuring you are set up for success. To support a smooth and efficient remote work experience, the internet connection must be obtained through a cable broadband or fiber optic internet service provider with speeds of at least 100Mbps download/25Mbps upload. Reliable internet service is essential for staying connected and productive.

The company has reviewed this job description to ensure that essential functions and basic duties have been included. It is not intended to be construed as an exhaustive list of all functions, responsibilities, skills, and abilities. Additional functions and requirements may be assigned by supervisors as deemed appropriate.

Compensation is not limited to base salary. Allied values our Total Rewards, and offers a competitive Benefit Package including, but not limited to, Medical, Dental, Vision, Life and Disability Insurance, Generous Paid Time Off, Tuition Reimbursement, EAP, and a Technology Stipend.

Allied reserves the right to amend, change, alter, and revise, pay ranges and benefits offerings at any time. All applicants acknowledge that by applying to the position you understand that the specific pay range is contingent upon meeting the qualification and requirements of the role, and for the successful completion of the interview selection and process. It is at the Company's discretion to determine what pay is provided to a candidate within the range associated with the role.

Title: Junior Database Administrator
Description:
Responsibilities

Responsibilities & Qualifications

The Junior Database Administrator is expected to be able to accomplish the following:


Implements and maintains smooth operation and physical design of databases.
Conducts performance tuning of indexes and databases in general.
Reviews database design and integration of systems, provides backup recovery and makes recommendations regarding enhancements and/or improvements.
Maintains security and integrity controls.
Formulates policies, procedures, and standards relating to database management, and monitors transaction activity and utilization .
May develop stored procedures and/or triggers.
Administers a variety of database systems, such as Microsoft SQL Server, and other Database Management Systems (DBMS).
Develops and enforces database administration and user standards and procedures; audits, modifies , and amends data in systems using SQL commands.
Oversees the scheduling of database projects, database and transaction log backups, notifications, and database replication between multiple SQL Servers and remote sites.
Assists Database Designers/Information System Analysts in the development and creation of SQL and Web-based databases and database systems.
Reviews technical designs, reports, documentation, and other materials produced by staff; creates and maintains high-level reporting systems using products such as Crystal Reports; researches and implements new database and online technology.


Required Qualifications


Active TS/SCI + CI Poly clearance
BS, 5-7 years of relevant experience; MS, 3-5 years; PhD, 0-2 years
One or more of the following DoD 8570 c ertifications :
ComTIA Security+
CompTIA CySA +
Systems Security Certified Practitioner (SSCP)
Certified Authorization Professional (CAP) – foundational RMF exposure

Desired Qualifications


Microsoft SQL Server Database Certification (e.g., Azure Database Administrator Associate – DP-300)
MySQL Database Administrator Certification
AWS Certified Database – Specialty
AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Associate
Microsoft Azure Database Administrator Associate


Overview

We are seeking a Junior Database Administrator to join our Task Order proposal on the Defense Intelligence Agency’s Solutions for the Information Technology Enterprise III (SITE III). The Junior Database Administrator i mplements and optimizes the computerized database systems that support the Company's or client's ability to function on a daily basis .

TekSynap is a fast-growing high-tech company that understands both the pace of technology today and the need to have a comprehensive well planned information management environment. “Technology moving at the speed of thought” embodies these principles – the need to nimbly utilize the best that information technology offers to meet the business needs of our Federal Government customers.

We offer our full-time employees a competitive benefits package to include health, dental, vision, 401K, life insurance, short-term and long-term disability plans, vacation time and holidays.

Visit us a t www.TekSynap.com .

Apply now to explore jobs with us!

Title: Consultant - Data Science / Data Lake
Description:
Are you looking to apply cyber analytics, artificial intelligence, and security operations experience in a client-facing role? At Deloitte, you'll help organizations modernize security operations, improve cyber resilience, and use data-driven insights to strengthen threat detection and response. In this Consultant, Cyber Defense & Resilience, Security Operations role, you will support cyber data, analytics, and transformation initiatives across leading organizations.

Recruiting for this role ends on 06/30/2026.

Work you'll do

As a Consultant, Cyber Defense & Resilience, Security Operations on the Cyber Defense & Resilience Security Operations team, you will be responsible for...


Supporting the design and modernization of cyber data, analytics, and security operations capabilities for client environments
Applying artificial intelligence, machine learning, and data engineering methods to cybersecurity use cases such as detection engineering, threat hunting, and response support
Working with cyber data platforms, cloud technologies, and telemetry sources to improve visibility, analysis, and operational decision-making
Contributing to strategy, design, and implementation workstreams across cyber transformation engagements
Preparing analyses, client deliverables, and recommendations while collaborating with cross-functional stakeholders and project team members


A successful candidate would possess these skills:


Ability to work independently and collaborate as part of a team
Effective written and verbal communication skills
Meticulous attention to detail and quality of work product
Ability to build and sustain professional relationships
Ability to lead projects or workstreams
Ability to manage and prioritize multiple tasks in a fast-paced and dynamic environment
Strong interpersonal skills and professional demeanor
Ability to meet deadlines
Ability to provide clear guidance to others


The team

Cyber Defense & Resilience is an integrated team of security and data technologists working at the intersection of cybersecurity, cyber data engineering, and artificial intelligence and machine learning for cyber defense and operations. The team helps clients modernize security data environments, improve data operations, and apply scalable analytics and artificial intelligence capabilities to cybersecurity challenges. Through a combination of advisory, implementation, and managed services support, Deloitte helps organizations strengthen cyber operations and advance business performance.

Qualifications

Required:


2+ years of experience in analytics consulting, cybersecurity analytics, security operations, or a combination of these (including internships and in-school experience)
1+ years of experience with artificial intelligence development tools or frameworks such as vector databases, LangChain, or CrewAI
1+ years of experience using Python, Structured Query Language (SQL), R, or SAS to prepare data for analysis, engineer features, visualize data, or support machine learning workflows
Experience working with cybersecurity cloud platforms such as Google SecOps, Amazon Web Services (AWS), or Microsoft Azure, and exposure to security operations center (SOC) threat hunting or incident response
Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or a field aligned to the role; or 4 years of equivalent professional experience
Ability to travel 50%, on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sectors you serve.
Limited immigration sponsorship may be available.


Preferred:


Experience supporting the design, development, or deployment of enterprise data science or artificial intelligence solutions
Experience applying artificial intelligence, machine learning, or advanced data engineering to cybersecurity use cases such as detection engineering or threat response support
Experience parsing and normalizing cyber or information technology telemetry datasets
Experience with PyTorch, Keras, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn, NumPy, or SciPy
Experience with Apache Kafka, Storm, or Spark
Experience creating client-ready materials using Microsoft PowerPoint or Microsoft Visio


The wage range for this role takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to skill sets; experience and training; licensure and certifications; and other business and organizational needs. The disclosed range estimate has not been adjusted for the applicable geographic differential associated with the location at which the position may be filled. At Deloitte, it is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range for their role and compensation decisions are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case. A reasonable estimate of the current range is .

You may also be eligible to participate in a discretionary annual incentive program, subject to the rules governing the program, whereby an award, if any, depends on various factors, including, without limitation, individual and organizational performance.

Title: Lead Data Engineer
Description:
🚀 Senior Data Engineer – Snowflake Migration Lead

📍 Location: Philadelphia, PA (Onsite)

💼 Experience: 8+ Years (4+ in US)

📌 Employment Type: Full-Time




About the Role

We're looking for a Senior Data Engineer to lead a large-scale Teradata to Snowflake migration program. This is a high-impact, onshore tech lead role where you'll own the full migration lifecycle — from SQL conversion to production cutover — for 750+ scripts.




You'll lead a team of 6–8 engineers, work closely with the client and SI partner, and drive delivery end to end. If you enjoy solving complex data problems, leading teams, and delivering under pressure — this role is for you.




What You'll Do

Lead the Teradata to Snowflake migration — script conversion, repointing, and optimization
Own end-to-end delivery: conversion → validation → cutover
Manage and update Airflow pipelines (DAGs, configurations, deployments)
Run parallel validation and data reconciliation to ensure accuracy
Handle production cutover planning, release governance, and rollback strategies
Mentor and manage a team of 6–8 engineers — code reviews, task tracking, and daily standups
Serve as the primary technical point of contact for the client and SI partner
Communicate risks, progress, blockers, and escalations to stakeholders clearly and on time




What You Bring (Must Have)

--Experience

8+ years in data engineering with 3+ years of hands-on Snowflake experience
Proven track record in Teradata to Snowflake migration programs
Experience leading teams in fast-paced, deadline-driven delivery environments




--Technical Skills

Strong expertise in Snowflake — SQL, performance tuning, RBAC, and architecture design
Deep knowledge of Teradata — BTEQ, SQL conversion, and stored procedures
Solid skills in PySpark and Python; hands-on experience with Databricks
Working experience with Apache Airflow — DAGs, configs, connections, and scheduling
Familiarity with ETL tools such as IICS or SSIS in a migration context
Experience with data lakes and object storage — S3, ADLS, or MinIO
Comfortable with Git, version control, and CI/CD pipelines




--Leadership & Communication

Strong stakeholder communication skills — comfortable with both business and technical audiences
Ability to manage delivery under tight SLAs and navigate complex project dependencies
Prior experience in client-facing consulting or managed services environments




Nice to Have

Telecom domain experience — CDR, subscriber data, revenue datasets
Snowflake certifications — SnowPro Core or SnowPro Advanced
Exposure to data observability and validation tools like Great Expectations or Monte Carlo
Hands-on with Snowpark, Streams & Tasks, or Dynamic Tables
Experience with Databricks Unity Catalog or MLOps frameworks
Background in large-scale migration programs within consulting or SI setups




Why Join Us?

Work on a high-visibility migration program with real impact
Lead a talented team and shape the technical direction
Collaborate with top-tier clients and partners
Show more

Title: Database Administrator - I
Description:
Job Requirements

As a Database Administrator-I at Phenom, you will support the maintenance, monitoring, and optimization of database systems across multiple environments. You will work closely with others DBAs, DevOps engineers, and development teams to ensure database reliability, availability, and performance.

This role is ideal for candidates who are passionate about databases, cloud technologies, and solving technical problems while learning modern database administration practices.

Key Responsibilities


Assist in maintaining and monitoring database systems including MongoDB, PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis, and Elasticsearch.
Support database backup, recovery, and routine maintenance activities.
Help troubleshoot basic database performance and connectivity issues.
Learn and apply database optimization techniques such as indexing and query tuning.
Work with cross-functional teams including Engineering, DevOps, and Product teams.
Assist in automating routine database tasks using scripts and tools.
Monitor database health, uptime, and alerts under guidance from senior team members.
Follow database security, compliance, and data governance best practices.
Stay updated with emerging database and cloud technologies.


What We’re Looking For


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or related field.
Basic understanding of SQL and database concepts.
Familiarity with at least one database technology such as PostgreSQL, MongoDB, or MySQL.
Knowledge of database fundamentals including backup/recovery and performance basics.
Exposure to scripting or programming languages such as Python, Bash, or Shell scripting is a plus.
Understanding of Linux/Unix fundamentals is preferred.
Familiarity with cloud platforms like AWS or Azure is an advantage.
Good analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Eagerness to learn and grow in a fast-paced environment.
Internship, academic project, or certification related to databases/cloud technologies is a plus.


Preferred Skills


Basic understanding of Docker or Kubernetes.
Exposure to Agile/Scrum development environments.
Familiarity with CI/CD concepts and DevOps practices.
Interest in big data, AI, or distributed systems technologies.


Additional Requirements

Applicants selected may be subjected to a background security investigation and may need to meet eligibility requirements for access to classified information.

Salary


Expected salary range: $80,000 – $90,000


Please note the Salary range is subject to change in the future in accordance with Phenom’s policies

Benefits


We want you to be your best self and to pursue your passions!
Health and wellness benefits/programs to support holistic employee health
Flexible hours and working schedules, as well as parental leave for new parents
Growing organization with career pathing and development opportunities
Tons of perks and extras in every location for all Phenoms!

Title: New Home Starts Payment Specialist
Description:
About The Role

The New Home Starts Payment Specialist is responsible for processing payment requests and reconciling vendor invoices and statements related to New Home Starts. This role involves close collaboration with Project Planners, Project Managers, Accounts Payable personnel, Vendors, and other departments as needed. Additionally, the New Home Starts Payment Specialist will participate in department-wide projects and provide general support as required.

What You'll Do


Review, analyze, reconcile, and submit invoices to Accounts Payable for timely processing
Process and review various payment requests from New Home Starts and Construction, ensuring prompt and accurate payments.
Reconcile monthly credit card transactions, meeting Finance department deadlines
Review Vendor and Developer statements to ensure accounts remain current and in good standing
Support New Home Starts by analyzing and researching various projects and assisting with administrative tasks (e.g., memo distribution, travel arrangements)



Requirements


What We're Looking For


High school diploma or equivalent required; college degree preferred.
Requires at least 2-3 years of relevant invoice processing & payment experience.
Demonstrated experience managing tasks that require a high level of confidentiality
Demonstrated computer skills including a working knowledge of MS Office products, including Outlook, Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.



Benefits


Why You Will Love Working Here

Medical, dental, vision coverage

Title: Consultant - Data Science / Data Lake
Description:
Are you looking to apply cyber analytics, artificial intelligence, and security operations experience in a client-facing role? At Deloitte, you'll help organizations modernize security operations, improve cyber resilience, and use data-driven insights to strengthen threat detection and response. In this Consultant, Cyber Defense & Resilience, Security Operations role, you will support cyber data, analytics, and transformation initiatives across leading organizations.

Recruiting for this role ends on 06/30/2026.

Work you'll do

As a Consultant, Cyber Defense & Resilience, Security Operations on the Cyber Defense & Resilience Security Operations team, you will be responsible for...


Supporting the design and modernization of cyber data, analytics, and security operations capabilities for client environments
Applying artificial intelligence, machine learning, and data engineering methods to cybersecurity use cases such as detection engineering, threat hunting, and response support
Working with cyber data platforms, cloud technologies, and telemetry sources to improve visibility, analysis, and operational decision-making
Contributing to strategy, design, and implementation workstreams across cyber transformation engagements
Preparing analyses, client deliverables, and recommendations while collaborating with cross-functional stakeholders and project team members


A successful candidate would possess these skills:


Ability to work independently and collaborate as part of a team
Effective written and verbal communication skills
Meticulous attention to detail and quality of work product
Ability to build and sustain professional relationships
Ability to lead projects or workstreams
Ability to manage and prioritize multiple tasks in a fast-paced and dynamic environment
Strong interpersonal skills and professional demeanor
Ability to meet deadlines
Ability to provide clear guidance to others


The team

Cyber Defense & Resilience is an integrated team of security and data technologists working at the intersection of cybersecurity, cyber data engineering, and artificial intelligence and machine learning for cyber defense and operations. The team helps clients modernize security data environments, improve data operations, and apply scalable analytics and artificial intelligence capabilities to cybersecurity challenges. Through a combination of advisory, implementation, and managed services support, Deloitte helps organizations strengthen cyber operations and advance business performance.

Qualifications

Required:


2+ years of experience in analytics consulting, cybersecurity analytics, security operations, or a combination of these (including internships and in-school experience)
1+ years of experience with artificial intelligence development tools or frameworks such as vector databases, LangChain, or CrewAI
1+ years of experience using Python, Structured Query Language (SQL), R, or SAS to prepare data for analysis, engineer features, visualize data, or support machine learning workflows
Experience working with cybersecurity cloud platforms such as Google SecOps, Amazon Web Services (AWS), or Microsoft Azure, and exposure to security operations center (SOC) threat hunting or incident response
Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or a field aligned to the role; or 4 years of equivalent professional experience
Ability to travel 50%, on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sectors you serve.
Limited immigration sponsorship may be available.


Preferred:


Experience supporting the design, development, or deployment of enterprise data science or artificial intelligence solutions
Experience applying artificial intelligence, machine learning, or advanced data engineering to cybersecurity use cases such as detection engineering or threat response support
Experience parsing and normalizing cyber or information technology telemetry datasets
Experience with PyTorch, Keras, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn, NumPy, or SciPy
Experience with Apache Kafka, Storm, or Spark
Experience creating client-ready materials using Microsoft PowerPoint or Microsoft Visio


The wage range for this role takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to skill sets; experience and training; licensure and certifications; and other business and organizational needs. The disclosed range estimate has not been adjusted for the applicable geographic differential associated with the location at which the position may be filled. At Deloitte, it is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range for their role and compensation decisions are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case. A reasonable estimate of the current range is .

You may also be eligible to participate in a discretionary annual incentive program, subject to the rules governing the program, whereby an award, if any, depends on various factors, including, without limitation, individual and organizational performance.

Title: IT Support Specialist
Description:
Mapbox is the leading real-time location platform for a new generation of location-aware businesses. Mapbox is the only platform that equips organizations with the full set of tools to power the navigation of people, packages, and vehicles everywhere. More than 4 million registered developers have chosen Mapbox because of the platform’s flexibility, security and privacy compliance. Organizations use Mapbox applications, data, SDKs and APIs to create customized and immersive experiences that delight their customers.

The IT team has the twin responsibilities of making the technology that every Mapboxer uses as effective and frictionless as possible while also optimizing the performance of our corporate technology stack. We do that by promoting secure, repeatable processes, supporting data wherever it lives, and delivering self-service solutions whenever possible.

Everyone at Mapbox is a customer, a stakeholder, and a partner, often all at the same time. IT has a diverse range of functions, including incident response, laptop support, office networking, audio/visual administration, SaaS application integration, new hire orientation, and end-user training.

What You'll Do

Mapbox is a global organization with offices in North America, Europe, and Asia, and our IT team provides excellent support and services for our offices worldwide. You will join the local IT team to support a high-growth employee base in the region, as well as help maintain a follow-the-sun support model with your IT colleagues in other offices and develop solutions that contribute to the high velocity of Mapbox as a whole. Reporting to the IT Manager, your primary responsibilities lie in the areas of IT and Security Operations, helping ensure the optimal health of systems and services—both on premises and in the cloud—while also assisting our employees with maintaining consistent, effective processes that optimize for usability and security.

In This Role, You Can Expect To


Collaborate with the rest of the IT team to manage inbound support requests and incidents, and resolve them according to our service level agreements.
Provide superior customer support in-person, in our ticketing system, and via chat. Provide the skills for our employees to effectively use our corporate tools and become IT advocates.
Support the smooth onboarding of new hires and manage the full employee IT lifecycle.
Setup, maintain, and strengthen the in-office LAN, WiFi, audio-visual, conferencing, and computing systems.
Identify trends in request tickets and help build automation and process improvements to make the system work better over time and intelligently reduce risk.
Secure configuration and access management of our operational production endpoints in AWS, GitHub, Google Cloud, G Suite, Okta, and other systems of record.
Participate in weekly sprints and IT projects, both as a contributor and as a leader. Write documentation for IT team solutions and end user knowledge.
This role is located in Washington, D.C.


What We Believe Are Important Traits For This Role


Experience with our IT tools and platforms of choice, including macOS and Windows 10/11, Okta SAML, GitHub, Slack, Google G Suite, Jamf, Intune, Zoom, and ticket management systems such as Jira.
Experience in a multi-input, fast-paced IT support organization that prioritizes customer service, security, and innovation.
The ability to thrive as a regional operator as part of a geographically diverse team. While Mapbox deeply values team support and interaction, most forms of team communication will not be in real time.
Comfortable working in spoken and written English. Knowledge of Japanese is a plus.
A love for problem solving, and a commitment to frictionless risk reduction. You dig into the root causes of issues, think outside the box, and strive for “secure by default” whenever possible.
Experience configuring and maintaining common small office networking products.
Experience automating with scripting languages like bash. Knowledge of Node.js, or Python is a plus.
Familiarity with Agile frameworks to keep projects on track and delivered at velocity.


What We Value

In addition to our core values, which are not unique to this position and are necessary for Mapbox leaders:


We value high-performing creative individuals who dig into problems and opportunities.
We believe in individuals being their whole selves at work. We commit to this through supportive health care, parental leave, flexibility for the things that come up in life, and innovating on how we think about supporting our people.
We emphasize an environment of teaching and learning to equip employees with the tools needed to be successful in their function and the company.
We strongly believe in the value of growing a diverse team and encourage people of all backgrounds, genders, ethnicities, abilities, and sexual orientations to apply.


Our annual base compensation for this role ranges from $72,250 - $97,750 for most US locations and 5% to 10% higher for US locations with a higher cost of labor. Job level and actual compensation will be decided based on factors including, but not limited to, individual qualifications objectively assessed during the interview process (including skills and prior relevant experience, potential impact, and scope of role), market demands, and specific work location. Please discuss your specific work location with your recruiter for more information.

By applying for this position, you acknowledge that you agree to the Mapbox Privacy Policy which is linked here.

Mapbox participates in E-Verify to confirm employee work authorization. Please refer to the Notice of E-Verify Participation and Right to Work posters for more information.

We are committed to a fair and equitable hiring process. We do not discriminate against any protected class.

Title: Paralegal Aide, Level II
Description:
** Candidates must be permanent in the Paralegal Aide title or be reachable on the list for exam no.1140 or no. 4070 **

The mission of the New York City Police Department is to enhance the quality of life in New York City by working in partnership with the community to enforce the law, preserve peace, protect the people, reduce fear, and maintain order. The New York City Police Department strives to foster a safe and fair city through the strategic deployment of resources, focusing on both crime prevention and addressing quality-of-life concerns while building lasting community relationships.

The Early Intervention Program analyzes relevant events and information sources to identify members who may be at-risk for poor performance/adverse consequences. Confers with member’s commanding officer and relevant stakeholders. Designs a remedial action plan to provide guidance and support and enhance performance. Coordinates with the Health and Wellness Section to ensure a holistic approach to the member’s well-being. Coordinates the Early Intervention Committee.

The duties of the Paralegal Aide II include, but are not limited to:


Locates, assembles, organizes and monitors data, records, documents, and information related to the Department which are necessary in order to inquiries, regarding Stop, Question, and Frisk, Racial Profiling Complaints, Adverse Credibility Findings, Suppression Decisions, Declined to Prosecute Data and develop an Early Intervention System (EIS) to include: certain types of arrests, excessive force, and ongoing disciplinary proceedings;
Assists in reviewing background information to be used in preparation for Department responses;
Performs routine legal research and summaries, drafting legal communications for review by higher level staff; and
Assists with case studies/projects into identified patterns and trends necessitating additional data analysis.


To Apply

Please click the 'Apply' button, or visit: https://cityjobs.nyc.gov and search for Job ID #781520.

Hours/Shift

9x5, 10x6, Saturdays and Sundays RDOs

Additional Information

In compliance with Federal Law, all persons hired will be required to verify identity and eligibility to work in the United States and to complete the required employment eligibility verification document form upon hire.

The City of New York offers a comprehensive benefits package including health insurance for the employee and his or her spouse or domestic partner and unemancipated children under age 26, union benefits such as dental and vision coverage, paid annual leave and sick leave, paid holidays, a pension, and optional savings and pre-tax programs such as Deferred Compensation, IRA, and a flexible spending account.

PARALEGAL AIDE - 30080

Minimum Qualifications


A baccalaureate degree from an accredited college or university, accredited by regional, national, professional or specialized agencies recognized as accrediting bodies by the U.S. Secretary of Education and by the Council for Higher Education Accreditation (CHEA); or
An associate degree or completion of 60 semester credits from an accredited college, accredited by regional, national, professional or specialized agencies recognized as accrediting bodies by the U.S. Secretary of Education and by the Council for Higher Education Accreditation (CHEA) and two years of full-time satisfactory experience, acquired in the United States, in the performance of paralegal (legal assistant) services; or
A four-year high school diploma or its educational equivalent approved by a State’s Department of Education or a recognized accrediting organization and four years of full-time satisfactory experience, acquired in the United States, in the performance of paralegal (legal assistant) services; or
A satisfactory combination of education and/or experience which is equivalent to “1”, “2” or “3” above. Paralegal Certification obtained in the United States from an accredited program or from a program approved by the American Bar Association can be substituted for 12 months of experience. Undergraduate credit can be substituted for experience on the basis of 30 semester credits from an accredited college for 12 months of experience. However, all candidates must have at least a four-year high school diploma or its educational equivalent approved by a State’s Department of Education or a recognized accrediting organization.


To be acceptable, experience in paralegal (legal assistant) services must have involved the American Legal System.

Experience which is primarily legal secretarial or includes only incidental paralegal (legal assistant) services is not acceptable.

Special Note

Individuals must have one additional year of pertinent paralegal experience or have a baccalaureate degree in addition to the requirements listed above to be eligible for placement in Assignment Level II duties and pay of Paralegal Aide.

Preferred Skills


Excellent verbal, interpersonal, research and writing skills. - Proficiency in utilizing spreadsheets and Department databases.


55a Program

NOTE: This position is open to qualified persons with a disability who are eligible for the 55-a program. Please indicate in your cover letter that you would like to be considered for the position under the 55-a program.

Residency Requirement

New York City residency is generally required within 90 days of appointment. However, City Employees in certain titles who have worked for the City for 2 continuous years may also be eligible to reside in Nassau, Suffolk, Putnam, Westchester, Rockland, or Orange County. To determine if the residency requirement applies to you, please discuss with the agency representative at the time of interview.

Additional Information

The City of New York is an inclusive equal opportunity employer committed to recruiting and retaining a diverse workforce and providing a work environment that is free from discrimination and harassment based upon any legally protected status or protected characteristic, including but not limited to an individual's sex, race, color, ethnicity, national origin, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, veteran status, gender identity, or pregnancy.

Title: Data Insights Analyst
Description:
At bet365, we're one of the world's leading online gambling companies, revolutionising the industry since 2000. Founded by Denise Coates CBE, we now employ over 9,000 people and serve over 100 million customers in 27 languages. Our focus on In-Play betting has solidified our market-leading position, offering an unmatched experience across 96 sports and 700,000 streaming events. With over 750 concurrent sporting fixtures at peak and more live sports streamed than anyone else in Europe, we handle over 6 billion HTTP requests daily and process more than 2 million bets per hour at peak.




We empower our employees to push boundaries and explore new ideas, cultivating a culture that celebrates and rewards creativity. This offers employees a wealth of opportunities for growth, giving them the opportunity to make a real impact in the world of online gambling. As a forward-thinking company, we’re breaking new ground in software innovation too, redefining what’s possible for our customers worldwide.




Job Description




We are seeking a highly analytical and commercially minded Gaming Insights Analyst to join our dynamic Insights team. In this role, you will be a crucial partner to the Casino Gaming and Marketing divisions, transforming raw data into the strategic intelligence that drives our business forward.




You will move beyond surface-level reporting to conduct deep, methodologically sound analyses of our Gaming products, marketing campaigns, and player behaviour.




Your primary mission is to challenge assumptions, uncover the "why" behind the numbers, and provide clear, actionable recommendations that optimise our investments, enhance player engagement, and grow our market share.




This is a high-impact role for a data-driven storyteller who is passionate about the Gaming industry and thrives on turning complex questions into profitable business decisions.




Qualifications




Proven analytical experience as a data analyst, marketing analyst, or product analyst role, preferably within the e-commerce, mobile, or online Gaming/Gambling industry.
Advanced SQL skills and extensive experience querying and manipulating large, complex datasets from data warehouses like BigQuery, Redshift, or Snowflake.
Strong, practical understanding of statistical analysis, experimental design, and the principles of A/B testing (e.g., control groups, statistical significance), to extract reliable insights from imperfect data.
Highly proficient with business intelligence tools such as Looker (or a similar tool like Tableau/Power BI) for data visualisation and building intuitive dashboards.
Ability to connect data insights to real-world business results and financial outcomes, acting as a business partner rather than just an analyst.
Excellent communicator who is able to clearly explain complex findings to both technical and non-technical audiences in a simple and concise way.
Passion for casino games and a curiosity about the online gaming world, with a keen interest in understanding what drives player behaviour.




Additional Information




Designing, executing, and delivering post-mortems for marketing and promotional campaigns, going beyond top-line results to measure true incremental lift, ROI, and profitability, accounting for confounding variables and establishing clear attribution.
Applying a rigorous analytical framework to evaluate the performance of marketing activities and product changes (e.g., game positioning) while measuring the impact of these "tests," using statistical principles to assess their effectiveness and inform future strategy.
Analysing player behavior by segmenting our user base across various dimensions (e.g., New vs. Existing, Player Value Tiers, Game/Provider Preference) to better understand player loyalty and lifetime value.
Translating complex findings into compelling narratives and actionable recommendations for stakeholders in Gaming, Marketing, and Leadership; such insights will directly influence decisions on marketing spend, product strategy, and promotional calendars.
Developing and maintaining powerful, intuitive dashboards and reports in Looker to provide stakeholders with self-serve access to performance metrics.
Using LookML to define business logic, creating reusable data models, and ensuring data consistency and accuracy for all analytics built on top of our BigQuery data warehouse.




By applying to us you are agreeing to share your Personal Data in accordance with our Recruitment Privacy Notice - https://www.bet365careers.com/privacy-policy




At bet365, we're committed to creating an environment where everyone feels welcome, respected and valued. Where all individuals can grow and develop, regardless of their background. We're Never Ordinary, and we're always striving to be better. If you need any adjustments or accommodations to the recruitment process, at either application or interview, please don’t hesitate to reach out.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Description

Position at LeoVegas Group

About The Role

We are seeking a Data Analyst to support P&T initiatives by analyzing data, identifying trends, and providing actionable insights to drive decision-making.This role is a unique opportunity to venture into 'uncharted territories,' focusing on how our products work and analyzing the performance of new features to drive our commercial and product strategy forward.

As a Data Analyst, you will be a key player in investigating customer journeys and website search performance, providing data-driven recommendations that directly impact the user experience. Working in a fast-paced environment in a team based in Malta, Sweden, Poland, and Italy, you will have the chance to build projects from scratch, revamp dashboards, and find areas for improvement within our global gaming product.

YOU WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR:


Product Analysis: Analyze website search performance and player insights to identify "loopholes" and areas for product improvement.
Stakeholder Management: Build strong, mature relationships with product owners, management, and commercial teams to communicate data-driven recommendations.
Customer Journey Investigation: Use behavioral and product analytics to deep-dive into how users interact with our platforms.
Dashboard Development: Collaborate with the team to revamp existing dashboards and create new tracking projects centered on product analytics.
Proactive Reporting: Conduct proactive analysis of new feature performance and provide actionable insights to the wider data team.
Data Unification: Help unify previously separate analysis tasks following the merger of our behavioral and commercial functions.


OUR SUCCESSFUL CANDIDATE WILL HAVE THE FOLLOWING:

Essential Skills


Professional Experience: Minimum of 2+ years of experience in a Data Analyst role, preferably within a product-focused environment.
Advanced SQL: Strong proficiency in SQL for querying and managing complex data sets.
Analytics Expertise: Proven experience in behavioral and product analytics, specifically investigating customer journeys.
Statistical Knowledge: A solid foundation in statistics to ensure the accuracy and reliability of your findings.
Communication Skills: The ability to translate complex data into clear recommendations for non-technical stakeholders.
Proactive Attitude: A self-starter mindset with the maturity to take ownership of projects and build stakeholder trust.
Python Proficiency: Experience using Python for data manipulation or advanced analysis.


NICE TO HAVES


iGaming Experience: Previous experience within the iGaming industry is considered a significant asset.


Who We Are

At the core of LeoVegas Group is Team Leo. Our culture is our foundation and is what enables us to innovate, build, and lead as we trailblaze our way through the igaming industry.

We're a team of over 2000 innovators, initiators, and groundbreakers working in a fast-paced and agile environment across 19 offices worldwide.

Benefits


Hybrid work policy
4 weeks of Workation (T&C apply)
Well-being allowance to support your active lifestyle
Private health insurance
Discounts across a range of retailers, gyms, bars & restaurants
We offer an employee assistance program that can provide help and guidance during challenging moments.


JOIN US!

In our pride, we empower our teammates to find their roar and run with their wildest ideas. We don’t wait for things to happen; we pounce and make it happen!

Would you be a good fit for the Leo Pride - give us a roar!

**As our company working language is English, we’d like to see your CV in English, please**

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Sway Gaming

Sway is an iGaming company focused on building and scaling digital products for international markets. We work with small, highly capable teams and place strong emphasis on clarity, ownership, and execution. We operate in a fast-moving environment where decisions are data-informed, responsibility is clearly defined, and teams are expected to deliver with consistency and focus.




About the Role

We are seeking a skilled and analytical Data Analyst with strong SQL expertise and hands-on experience in building dashboards, analysing performance, and delivering actionable insights to stakeholders. This role is ideal for someone who is confident working autonomously, enjoys solving business problems with data, and has experience within the iGaming sector.

You will work closely with product, marketing, CRM, and operational teams, supporting data-driven decisions across the organisation.




Key Responsibilities

Build, maintain, and improve high-impact dashboards in tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Sigma.
Analyse player behaviour, marketing performance, and operational data to identify trends and opportunities.
Write, optimise, and validate complex SQL queries for analysis and reporting.
Apply statistical methods (A/B testing, regression analysis) to support business decisions.
Support data modelling and data warehousing initiatives by understanding data structures and relationships.
Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders, communicating insights clearly and confidently.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and usability across reports and analysis.
Contribute to ongoing improvements in analytics processes and data quality.




Required Skills & Experience

2+ year of experience in a dedicated data analytics role.
Expert-level SQL skills — ability to write and optimise complex queries.
Strong experience building dashboards in Tableau, Power BI, or Sigma Computing.
Hands-on experience with applied statistics (A/B testing, regression, hypothesis testing).
Understanding of data warehousing and data modelling concepts.
Ability to work independently and communicate insights effectively to non-technical stakeholders.
Industry experience in iGaming (analytics or operational roles).




Preferred Skills

Experience with Python for data analysis.
Experience with dbt, Git, or modern ELT/warehouse tools.
Experience working with Snowflake or similar cloud data warehouses.
Exposure to automations, ETL optimization, or data engineering tasks.
Familiarity with data science techniques (even if not used day-to-day).




What We Offer

Competitive salary + performance-based bonus.
Hybrid work model from our Malta office.
Private health insurance and wellness benefits.
Opportunity to work with modern tools, scalable data architecture, and a high-growth team.
International environment with strong cross-team collaboration.
A culture that values ownership, curiosity, and continuous learning.

Proven analytical experience as a data analyst, marketing analyst, or product analyst role, preferably within the e-commerce, mobile, or online Gaming/Gambling industry.
Advanced SQL skills and extensive experience querying and manipulating large, complex datasets from data warehouses like BigQuery, Redshift, or Snowflake.
Strong, practical understanding of statistical analysis, experimental design, and the principles of A/B testing (e.g., control groups, statistical significance), to extract reliable insights from imperfect data.
Highly proficient with business intelligence tools such as Looker (or a similar tool like Tableau/Power BI) for data visualisation and building intuitive dashboards.
Ability to connect data insights to real-world business results and financial outcomes, acting as a business partner rather than just an analyst.
Excellent communicator who is able to clearly explain complex findings to both technical and non-technical audiences in a simple and concise way.

Designing, executing, and delivering post-mortems for marketing and promotional campaigns, going beyond top-line results to measure true incremental lift, ROI, and profitability, accounting for confounding variables and establishing clear attribution.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
WE ARE LOOKING FOR DATA ANALYST

At Unikie, we develop secure, real-time technology solutions for demanding environments. Our expertise spans intelligent machines, connected vehicles, industry 4.0, and secure digital platforms. We work with leading global companies to build software and data solutions that operate reliably in real-world conditions. At the core of our work is a strong engineering culture, a low-hierarchy organization, and a collaborative way of working where people are trusted to take ownership.

Location: Oulu

We are now looking for a Data Analyst to join our team and help transform field data into actionable insights that guide hardware development and improve product robustness and feature quality.

About The Project

In this project, you will work with complex, real-world field data related to hardware performance and usage. Your mission is to analyze performance trends, uncover meaningful patterns, and turn current performance observations into clear design rules that inform where future hardware development should focus.

You will be working closely with cross-functional stakeholders, helping them translate business and engineering questions into data-driven analyses. The role combines hands-on data access and wrangling, statistical analysis, and the creation of clear visualizations that make complex findings easy to understand and act on.

Key Responsibilities


Access, clean, and analyze field data using AI/LLM tools, SQL, and Python.
Develop visualizations and dashboards that clearly communicate complex findings.
Apply statistical methods to uncover insights and support data-driven decision-making.
Translate business and engineering questions into well-structured technical analyses.
Communicate results and recommendations clearly to non-technical and technical stakeholders.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams in a fast-paced, iterative environment.


Your future colleagues are waiting for you to join our international team. Working with us is uncomplicated, there’s a good team spirit, low-hierarchy and nonexistent bureaucracy. Our way of working is flexible too, many of our team work remotely from home with visits to our office from time to time when needed. This role is equally challenging, and as such we offer a very good compensation.

Required Qualifications


2+ years of experience in data analysis or data science.
Strong proficiency in Python and SQL for data access, wrangling, and analysis.
Demonstrated ability to create clear, compelling visualizations (e.g., dashboards, reports, charts).
Comfortable working with ambiguity, experimentation, and iteration.
Ability to work in a highly collaborative environment with guided autonomy and flexibility.
Strong communication skills and ability to present findings to stakeholders.


NICE TO HAVE


Familiarity with a broad range of statistical techniques and good judgment on when to apply them.
Experience with AWS tools, such as SageMaker, for data processing or model development.
Experience working with hardware-related or field performance data.
Experience using AI/LLM-based tools to accelerate analysis and reporting.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Are you ready to join our digital revolution journey?

At Aleph , we’re not just part of the digital advertising landscape—we’re shaping its future. Representing the world’s leading platforms, including TikTok, Amazon, Google, and nearly 55 others, we operate in 130+ markets across new and existing geographies.

Our mission is to empower advertisers and brands to unlock the full potential of these platforms' advertising capabilities. By fostering long-lasting partnerships, we create limitless opportunities for people and businesses to advertise effectively at both local and global levels.

With a presence spanning continents, Aleph offers you the chance to be part of a fast-growing, innovative team where your work makes a direct impact. If you’re ambitious, forward-thinking, and eager to thrive in a dynamic, global environment, Aleph is the perfect place to build your career.

Role Overview

We are looking for a highly analytical Data Analyst to join our Business Intelligence team. You will act as a critical "backend engine," building the data foundations and analytical models that enable data-driven decisions for both our supply (platforms) and demand (sales) sides.

You will dive deep into complex datasets, build automated reporting pipelines, and uncover actionable insights that drive our strategy with global platforms like TikTok, Microsoft, Google, X, Spotify, and others.

What You 'll Do



Extract and analyze complex data.
Query, clean, and transform large datasets from multiple sources to uncover trends, performance metrics, and market insights that support strategic decision-making across the organization.
Build and maintain BI dashboards.
Design, develop, and maintain automated dashboards and reports using Looker Studio to provide real-time visibility into business performance for executive and partner discussions. Support strategic projects.
Conduct deep-dive analyses for platform RFP responses, Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs), and strategic initiatives that shape Aleph’s partnerships and revenue strategy. Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders.
Work closely with strategists, sales teams, and regional leadership to define data requirements, understand their business challenges, and deliver quantitative solutions.
Translate data into actionable recommendations.
Identify performance anomalies, revenue opportunities, and market risks by packaging raw data into structured, easy-to-consume analytical models and insights.
Ensure data integrity and governance.
Establish clear data validation frameworks, optimize queries, and maintain high standards for data accuracy and documentation internally.



Who You Are



Highly analytical and problem-solving oriented.
You have a strong quantitative background and enjoy tackling complex business questions through rigorous data analysis.
Technically proficient in data tools.
You possess strong SQL skills for data extraction and manipulation, and you are highly proficient in at least one major BI visualization tool (e.g., Power BI, Looker Studio). Advanced Excel/Google Sheets skills are a must; experience with Python a strong plus. Business-minded.You don’t just pull numbers; you understand the business context behind the data and can connect your analysis directly to revenue growth, efficiency, and key opportunities.
A clear communicator. You can translate complex technical findings and data models into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders and leadership
Structured and detail-oriented. You are meticulous about data accuracy, organize your code and queries logically, and ensure all deliverables meet a high-quality standard before they reach stakeholders.
Interested in digital media or advertising.
Experience in digital advertising analytics, media agencies, ad-tech, or similar environments is a strong plus, though not strictly required.



What You'll Love About Us



We care about your individuality by giving you freedom to grow and create within the company, regardless of your position.
Work in a dynamic team in a fast-paced industry of an international company with multi-cultural teams around the world .
Learn from the best: Our state of the art workshops guarantee the latest insights into digital advertising.
We offer an attractive compensation package above industry standards, with a minimum gross annual salary of 30,000 EUR. Final compensation will be determined during the interview process based on experience and qualifications.



#ALEPH

Why Join Us?

Aleph is a place of many cultures, perspectives, and talents. We support each other, creating an environment of giving and receiving. We value partnership and communication because we believe it takes a group of people to achieve great things. We are energized by our ever-changing industry, our curiosity keeps us learning and seeking out new opportunities.

We stay flexible and adaptable, and believe in moving with the speed of change. We encourage everyone to strive for more, providing our internal talent with growth opportunities and the ability to learn together.

#ALEPH

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job description

We build consumer products that grow fast. You'll build the data function that keeps up.




In 2026, the Financial Times named Wire Salad one of Europe's fastest-growing companies. We build brands and apps across fitness, skincare, human health, and pet health - products used by real people, every day, across many markets.




**This is our first dedicated data hire.** 

You'll report directly to the Head of Product, inherit an existing stack (BigQuery, Airbyte, Looker, Statsig) while having a clean slate to build the data function the way you think it should be built. There are no politics around who owns which metric. Just a fast-growing company that knows it needs great data - and is betting on you to deliver it.




The problem you're solving:

Speed creates a specific kind of mess. Experiments run in parallel across four verticals. Teams hold strong opinions on different numbers for the same thing. Your job is to keep the scoreboard honest, catch what's working before anyone else does, and make sure every team is reading from the same page.




What you'll actually do:

Build the data backbone: pipelines in Airbyte, models in BigQuery, dashboards in Looker, experiments in Statsig. Clean inputs, consistent definitions, documented logic - the foundation everything else runs on.
Own the metrics that matter: LTV:CAC, ROAS, LTV, retention, funnel conversion, payback periods, channel attribution. Define them once, get everyone aligned, and put an end to "wait, which number is right?"
Go proactive, not reactive: don't wait for someone to ask what's broken - build AI-assisted anomaly detection that flags it first. Spot the channel quietly leaking spend at 3am. Catch the cohort converting 40% better and explain why.
Democratise the data: not every question should land in your inbox. Build self-serve dashboards people actually use, write docs people actually read, and raise data literacy across the company.
Use AI like it's 2026, because it is: if a tool makes you 10x faster or sharper, we want you using it - and teaching the rest of us.
Raise the bar: question inconsistencies, chase down edge cases, document what you build, and hold everyone (including yourself) to a high standard of accuracy.




Requirements:

2+ years in analytics, analytics engineering, or BI - ideally in a fast-moving environment (ecommerce, consumer apps, growth-stage tech).
Strong SQL. CTEs, window functions, complex joins, query optimisation. You can look at a slow query and figure out why it's slow.
Data engineering instincts. You've built or maintained pipelines, thought about schemas, written tests, and genuinely care about data reliability. dbt experience is a strong plus.
Familiarity with a modern stack. Ideally BigQuery, Looker, Airbyte, and Statsig - but we care about fundamentals over tool-logo matching. Equivalent experience transfers.
Commercial brain. You read a chart and your next thought is "what does this mean for the business?" You push back on vanity metrics.
Ownership, for real. You notice something is off and fix it without being asked. You proactively follow through. You ship.
AI-native mindset. You're already using AI tools to accelerate your work, and curious about where to push further.
Fluent English, written and spoken.




Why work at WIRE SALAD?

Additional performance-based bonuses
Hybrid work model – balance between office and remote work
Company provided work computer (MacBook or Windows)
Additional health insurance after the probation period
Home office budget after the probation period
Team-building events & annual workation
Perks.lt account with numerous discounts and partner offers
Commuting expenses covered by the company

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
The Customization Group

Business Data Analyst

Build the analytics layer for a network shipping 2.5M custom products a day.


Riga, Latvia (Hybrid)
Full-time
€2,500-3000 gross/month


We're looking for a business analyst who wants to build, not just report. Someone who gets excited about defining the metrics that matter, has the instinct to push back when the data disagrees with the room, and uses AI as a natural part of how they work.

About TCG

The Customization Group is a global leader in mass customization and print-on-demand e-commerce. We manufacture up to 2.5 million unique, personalized products daily across production sites in Germany, Poland, the USA, and Latvia. Millions of customers find us through our 20+ online shops and 1,000+ partner stores. Our 750+ team members come from 40+ nationalities and work at the intersection of manufacturing, e-commerce, and AI.

At our scale, operational data is a goldmine of insight - but only if someone knows how to read it. That's the role we're hiring for.

The Role

You'll own the analytics layer for our sales, finance, and profitability data. You define the KPIs, build the Power BI reporting leaders actually use, and turn raw sales and cost data into the recommendations that account managers, shop managers, and top-level leadership execute on. You'll partner with sales, marketing, e-commerce, and production teams to translate business questions into analyses, surface the insights that matter, and drive process improvements with the data to back them up.

In a nutshell: Own the analytics and reporting layer. Extract, transform, visualize, interpret. Drive the process improvements the data points to.

What You'll Be Working On


→Manage daily operations and development of TCG analytics and reporting tools.
→Taking the sales forecasting process from manual to modelled - statistical methods, automation, the works.
→Redesigning sales and profit reporting so group managers can see which products, channels, and shops are actually profitable.
→Building Power BI dashboards leaders actually use, with confident DAX and a clean data model behind them.
→Defining the KPIs that matter across sales, marketing, e-commerce, and production -and getting them adopted.
→Migrating the reporting layer onto our new microservices architecture - clean, fast, scalable.
→Using AI tools to accelerate exploration and automate the repeatable parts of analysis


Who We're Looking For

Sounds like you?


→3-5 years in business analysis inside a data-rich operational environment - e-commerce, manufacturing, or similar.
→Power BI to a depth where you've built dashboards people actually depend on, with confident DAX and data modelling.
→Track record of defining KPIs and turning analysis into adopted process changes - not just reports that get sent.
→You've built your own analytics workflows or automations using AI - it's part of how you work, not something you're planning to explore.
→Working SQL and exposure to statistical methods for forecasting.
→Hands-on experience with financial or profitability data in a previous role - costing, margin analysis, P&L reporting, or similar.
→Fluency in English.


Probably not a fit if...


→You prefer fully-defined requirements over figuring out what the business should be measuring in the first place.
→You see analytics as building reports on request, rather than partnering on decisions.
→You see AI as a future investment rather than a current tool.


What We Offer


→ €2,500-3000 gross/month, depending on level. Additional performance-based annual bonus on top of base salary.
→International experience in our globally spread Teams.
→The flexibility that suits your lifestyle: In-house, hybrid way of working.
→Private Health insurance with a compensation package after the probation period.
→Additional leave on special occasions.
→Variety of packed breakfast, lunch, and snacks in the office.
→Valuable knowledge and excellent experience for future development.
→Connection with your colleagues through Team building and company events.


Interested?

Send us your CV. No cover letter needed - if you want to add a few lines on a dashboard or KPI you're proud of, we'd read it.

The Customization Group


Riga, Latvia
thecustomizationgroup.com We welcome applications from all backgrounds. What matters is what you can build.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job description

We build consumer products that grow fast. You'll build the data function that keeps up.




In 2026, the Financial Times named Wire Salad one of Europe's fastest-growing companies. We build brands and apps across fitness, skincare, human health, and pet health - products used by real people, every day, across many markets.




**This is our first dedicated data hire.** 

You'll report directly to the Head of Product, inherit an existing stack (BigQuery, Airbyte, Looker, Statsig) while having a clean slate to build the data function the way you think it should be built. There are no politics around who owns which metric. Just a fast-growing company that knows it needs great data - and is betting on you to deliver it.




The problem you're solving:

Speed creates a specific kind of mess. Experiments run in parallel across four verticals. Teams hold strong opinions on different numbers for the same thing. Your job is to keep the scoreboard honest, catch what's working before anyone else does, and make sure every team is reading from the same page.




What you'll actually do:

Build the data backbone: pipelines in Airbyte, models in BigQuery, dashboards in Looker, experiments in Statsig. Clean inputs, consistent definitions, documented logic - the foundation everything else runs on.
Own the metrics that matter: LTV:CAC, ROAS, LTV, retention, funnel conversion, payback periods, channel attribution. Define them once, get everyone aligned, and put an end to "wait, which number is right?"
Go proactive, not reactive: don't wait for someone to ask what's broken - build AI-assisted anomaly detection that flags it first. Spot the channel quietly leaking spend at 3am. Catch the cohort converting 40% better and explain why.
Democratise the data: not every question should land in your inbox. Build self-serve dashboards people actually use, write docs people actually read, and raise data literacy across the company.
Use AI like it's 2026, because it is: if a tool makes you 10x faster or sharper, we want you using it - and teaching the rest of us.
Raise the bar: question inconsistencies, chase down edge cases, document what you build, and hold everyone (including yourself) to a high standard of accuracy.




Requirements:

2+ years in analytics, analytics engineering, or BI - ideally in a fast-moving environment (ecommerce, consumer apps, growth-stage tech).
Strong SQL. CTEs, window functions, complex joins, query optimisation. You can look at a slow query and figure out why it's slow.
Data engineering instincts. You've built or maintained pipelines, thought about schemas, written tests, and genuinely care about data reliability. dbt experience is a strong plus.
Familiarity with a modern stack. Ideally BigQuery, Looker, Airbyte, and Statsig - but we care about fundamentals over tool-logo matching. Equivalent experience transfers.
Commercial brain. You read a chart and your next thought is "what does this mean for the business?" You push back on vanity metrics.
Ownership, for real. You notice something is off and fix it without being asked. You proactively follow through. You ship.
AI-native mindset. You're already using AI tools to accelerate your work, and curious about where to push further.
Fluent English, written and spoken.




Why work at WIRE SALAD?

Additional performance-based bonuses
Hybrid work model – balance between office and remote work
Company provided work computer (MacBook or Windows)
Additional health insurance after the probation period
Home office budget after the probation period
Team-building events & annual workation
Perks.lt account with numerous discounts and partner offers
Commuting expenses covered by the company




Financial Remuneration:

The salary range is 2500-3000EUR NETO

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
About MBRYONICS

MBRYONICS is at the forefront of free space optical communications. Our product portfolio covers a wide range of emerging technologies from photonic integrated circuits to advanced optical systems and embedded software solutions. Our optical communications systems are integrated and deployed in satellite communications systems. The work is exciting and challenging and is at the forefront of the technology domains. As part of our expansion, we are currently looking for a suitably motivated - Business Intelligence Analyst - to join our group.

As the BI Analyst at MBRYONICS, you will play a pivotal role in supporting the delivery of critical components and features within our mission to build the Internet in Space. You will be responsible for working with both our Strategic Bid team, the Program Management Office, Finance, Operations and the Research and Development teams to support the delivery of solutions to partners and end-customers that contribute to our overall success.

What You'll Do


Data Analysis and Reporting: Collate and analyse data from several MB systems such as sales, planning, finance, operations and research and development. Respond to questions on the data from the Head of PMO by providing relevant reports and visualization/dashboards.
Collaborate: Collaborate with departments to systematise the identification, collation, integration and aggregation of key business data to provide business insights
Manage: Create and maintain a centralized database for the purposes of BI reporting
Automate: Assist the Head of PMO to automate data collection, integration and aggregation
Communicate: Communicate findings verbally, through written reports and visually through dashboards using tools such as Tableau and Power BI.
Maintain Standards and Processes: Maintain the PMO repository of relevant project standards, processes, forecasts and change records.
Innovate: Work with the Head of PMO, Project Managers and Product Owners to innovate on data collection, maintenance and reporting


Who You Are


Analysis and Reporting: Experienced in database management and analysing data from project, resource and financial systems and reporting on key outcomes in a concise and clear manner to provide business insights and decision-grade information.
Technical/Software: Experienced with one or more business intelligence and visualisation tools such as Tableau, Power BI and Python/R
Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to analyse complex data sets and formulate these into reports and dashboards that provide the right level of information for the audience and that enable timely decision-making.
Collaboration: Able to foster great collaboration and working relationships across departments to provide the business with timely business insights
Problem-solving: Strong analytical, critical thinking, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities.
Technical Acumen: Able to understand complex technical concepts related to optical communications, satellite systems, or high-speed data transfer.
Strategic Thinking: Able to connect data trends to overarching strategic objectives and company vision.


Desirable Skills & Experience


Third-level qualification in data analysis or related discipline
5+ years of experience in data analysis and visualization/reporting role, preferably in the telecommunications, space or technology industry or regulated manufacturing.


Nice to Have


Familiarity with ECSS (European Co-operation for Space Standards), Aerospace standards, space technologies and space industry trends
Experience working in a research and development environment.
Background in a fast-paced, high-tech environment.


What's In It For You

Career Growth: Contribute to a world-class space-age communication program while developing your leadership skills in a state-of-the-art manufacturing environment

Collaboration: Work with talented professionals across disciplines to drive impactful solutions

Innovation: Be part of a team pioneering advancements in manufacturing engineering for cutting-edge FSO systems

Work-Life Balance: Enjoy flexible work schedules and a comprehensive benefits package

If you're ready to take your career to new heights and drive the future of space-age communication, apply now and join the MBRYONICS team as our Business Intelligence Analyst .

Diversity, Inclusion and Equal Opportunities

Core ethical values, diversity and inclusion are invaluable to MBRYONICS’ business success. We recognise that the broad perspectives that result from recruiting, developing and retaining a globally diverse workforce promotes innovation and helps us succeed as a business enterprise. It is the policy and practice of the Company to provide equal opportunities to all employees and applicants. The Company will not tolerate discrimination on the basis of gender, race, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, civil status, family status, disability, age, or membership of the Traveller community.
