Title: Analytic Support Analyst
Description:
Cencora, previously known as AmerisourceBergen, is a leading global pharmaceutical solutions organization centered on improving lives. Ranked #21 on the Global Fortune 500, our team members are united in our responsibility to create healthier futures.

Our Shared Service Center in Lithuania is experiencing rapid growth and we have many diverse and exciting roles in Customer Service, Operations, IT, Finance and HR. Join us and make a positive impact on human and animal health.

Job Details

We are seeking an experienced Analyst to join our Analytic Support team under the guidance of the Analytic Support Manager. This role offers the opportunity to lead impactful projects, work across departments to deliver analytics solutions, and support data-driven decision-making across the organization.

This is a dynamic role with opportunities for professional growth, including advanced analytics, project management, and leadership development.

Responsibilities


Operate as the first point of contact for World Courier employees by keeping track of reporting incidents.
Ensure the resolution of basic incidents, and/or escalate to senior team members based on incident complexity.
Support business requests related to reporting system access and reporting system usage.
Create, update and debug Alteryx workflows.
Perform initial assessment of SQL stored procedures to identify potential data errors.
Assist in administration of Alteryx Server and Alteryx Gallery environments.
Actively review queues and monitor tool usage.
Run periodic reports in Excel. Ensure timely report delivery to stakeholders.
Support data extraction from SQL database based on requests from different business segments.
Serve as a resource for interpreting data and information.
Create and/or update documentation, self-service guides reporting tools.
Work closely with all other teams in Analytics to coordinate cross-functional activities and ensure deliverables are met.
Support senior team members on enhancements to existing tools and processes.


Education


Bachelor’s degree in a relevant discipline (e.g., Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics).


Work Experience


Minimum 3+ years of experience in analytics and reporting, ideally within logistics or a related field.


Skills And Knowledge


Planning, analytical, and conceptual skills to evaluate business problems to identify and apply appropriate solutions.
Knowledge of Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Excel (essential).
Ability to write and understand Structure Query Language (SQL).
Experience with relational databases (e.g. Microsoft SQL) and data management (desirable).
Ability to create and/or understand one of the following - Alteryx Designer, Power BI (desirable).
Experience with ServiceNow or any other ticket management system (desirable).
Ability to effectively participate in cross-functional global teams.
Intellectual curiosity and a self-starter mindset.
Strong organizational skills with attention to detail.
Ability to build strong relationships and collaborate across teams.


Salary: 2,135.94 - 3,051.37 eur gross/month

What Cencora offers

We offer a competitive annual bonus, life insurance from Day 1, a best-in-class health insurance package, and up to 6 fully paid benefit days a year. As a Cencora employee, you have the benefit of our referral bonus scheme, our boundless learning opportunities and our global Employee Assistance Program. We have a wonderful office location in Quadrum, equipped with everything you need for a small break at work and fresh snacks at all times. Become part of our purpose-driven, multicultural team now and help us create healthier futures

Full time

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
Summary

We're looking for a Business Analyst who is passionate about transforming data into actionable insights. In this role, you'll help build the analytical foundation that powers decision-making across the company, from customer engagement and product usage to subscriber conversion and retention.

You'll work closely with product, growth, customer success, and leadership teams to uncover opportunities, answer key business questions, and ensure everyone has access to reliable, meaningful data.

Key Responsibilities


Build and maintain data frameworks that enable quick, reliable reporting and self-serve insights across the organization.
Build reusable analytical models and tools (e.g. cohort frameworks, conversion models) to enable fast, flexible decision-making.
Design and maintain reports and dashboards covering customer engagement and product analytics, with emphasis on event tracking and behavioral patterns.
Deliver ad-hoc analyses that support key business questions, especially around subscriber conversion, retention, and feature usage.
Ensure data quality, consistency and reliability across tools and databases.
Help scale our analytics foundation: improving structure, expanding coverage, and keeping documentation up to date.


Key Qualifications


3+ years of experience in a business analyst, product analyst, or data analyst role, ideally in a consumer tech or SaaS environment.
Strong analytical skills with ability to work with complex datasets.
Strong skills in Sheets/Excel and BI tools.
Strong working knowledge of SQL; experience with event-based product analytics tools (e.g. PostHog, Mixpanel, or similar) is a plus.
Ability to create, manage and automate custom dashboards and reporting pipelines.
A good communicator who can translate business requirements into analytical outputs and relay findings into clear recommendations for non-technical stakeholders.
Self-directed, organized, and comfortable operating in a fast-moving startup environment.
Familiarity with tools like Google Colab or Python for data pipeline and analysis work.
Strong communication skills in English
Experience with subscription or RevenueCat-based data models is a plus.
Background in consumer apps or connected hardware products is a plus.


Join us


Work on a project that can truly change how millions of people perceive nature and help them make a lasting impact on global biodiversity for generations to come.
Engage with a passionate, dynamic, and talented team.
Be part of the fast-paced, innovative environment of a rapidly growing startup, with abundant opportunities for personal and professional growth.
Enjoy competitive stock options, at least 25 days of paid leave, and an open-ended contract with a trial period.
Receive your own Birdbuddy Feeder and Birdbuddy Premium Family subscription.


If this describes you, we invite you to submit your application to help us learn more about you and your potential contributions to our mission to connect people with nature through technology.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
About MBRYONICS

MBRYONICS is at the forefront of free space optical communications. Our product portfolio covers a wide range of emerging technologies from photonic integrated circuits to advanced optical systems and embedded software solutions. Our optical communications systems are integrated and deployed in satellite communications systems. The work is exciting and challenging and is at the forefront of the technology domains. As part of our expansion, we are currently looking for a suitably motivated - Business Intelligence Analyst - to join our group.

As the BI Analyst at MBRYONICS, you will play a pivotal role in supporting the delivery of critical components and features within our mission to build the Internet in Space. You will be responsible for working with both our Strategic Bid team, the Program Management Office, Finance, Operations and the Research and Development teams to support the delivery of solutions to partners and end-customers that contribute to our overall success.

What You'll Do


Data Analysis and Reporting: Collate and analyse data from several MB systems such as sales, planning, finance, operations and research and development. Respond to questions on the data from the Head of PMO by providing relevant reports and visualization/dashboards.
Collaborate: Collaborate with departments to systematise the identification, collation, integration and aggregation of key business data to provide business insights
Manage: Create and maintain a centralized database for the purposes of BI reporting
Automate: Assist the Head of PMO to automate data collection, integration and aggregation
Communicate: Communicate findings verbally, through written reports and visually through dashboards using tools such as Tableau and Power BI.
Maintain Standards and Processes: Maintain the PMO repository of relevant project standards, processes, forecasts and change records.
Innovate: Work with the Head of PMO, Project Managers and Product Owners to innovate on data collection, maintenance and reporting


Who You Are


Analysis and Reporting: Experienced in database management and analysing data from project, resource and financial systems and reporting on key outcomes in a concise and clear manner to provide business insights and decision-grade information.
Technical/Software: Experienced with one or more business intelligence and visualisation tools such as Tableau, Power BI and Python/R
Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to analyse complex data sets and formulate these into reports and dashboards that provide the right level of information for the audience and that enable timely decision-making.
Collaboration: Able to foster great collaboration and working relationships across departments to provide the business with timely business insights
Problem-solving: Strong analytical, critical thinking, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities.
Technical Acumen: Able to understand complex technical concepts related to optical communications, satellite systems, or high-speed data transfer.
Strategic Thinking: Able to connect data trends to overarching strategic objectives and company vision.


Desirable Skills & Experience


Third-level qualification in data analysis or related discipline
5+ years of experience in data analysis and visualization/reporting role, preferably in the telecommunications, space or technology industry or regulated manufacturing.


Nice to Have


Familiarity with ECSS (European Co-operation for Space Standards), Aerospace standards, space technologies and space industry trends
Experience working in a research and development environment.
Background in a fast-paced, high-tech environment.


What's In It For You

Career Growth: Contribute to a world-class space-age communication program while developing your leadership skills in a state-of-the-art manufacturing environment

Collaboration: Work with talented professionals across disciplines to drive impactful solutions

Innovation: Be part of a team pioneering advancements in manufacturing engineering for cutting-edge FSO systems

Work-Life Balance: Enjoy flexible work schedules and a comprehensive benefits package

If you're ready to take your career to new heights and drive the future of space-age communication, apply now and join the MBRYONICS team as our Business Intelligence Analyst .

Diversity, Inclusion and Equal Opportunities

Core ethical values, diversity and inclusion are invaluable to MBRYONICS’ business success. We recognise that the broad perspectives that result from recruiting, developing and retaining a globally diverse workforce promotes innovation and helps us succeed as a business enterprise. It is the policy and practice of the Company to provide equal opportunities to all employees and applicants. The Company will not tolerate discrimination on the basis of gender, race, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, civil status, family status, disability, age, or membership of the Traveller community.

Title: BI Data Analyst
Description:
Growth Leads is an online lead generation company that creates quality leads in highly competitive verticals, such as iGaming, Financials, Health and Communications. We focus on building high-end websites with quality content that let customers make an informed decision about the product they’re about to buy or sign-up to. We pride ourselves on adopting a customer-focused approach to quality and usability.

About The Role

We are looking for an experienced Data Analyst who is motivated to take on an amazing new adventure: developing and maintaining data analysis structures and derive actionable recommendations to boost company revenue.

What you'll do


Monitor, analyze, and report on the performance of our different acquisition channels.
Develop and maintain dashboards to track key metrics such as traffic sources, conversion rates, lifetime values.
Design and implement data strategies to optimize our marketing efforts.
Translate complex datasets into actionable insights to guide marketing decisions and growth initiatives.
Analyze user journeys to assess the potentials for optimization for our acquisition channels
Design and analyze A/B tests for landing pages, offers, and affiliate traffic strategies.
Develop predictive models to forecast traffic, conversion, and revenue outcomes across affiliate segments.
Ensure the accuracy, integrity, and consistency of affiliate marketing data by working closely with engineering and product teams.
Define and enforce data collection standards and best practices.
Communicate findings and recommendations clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
Work with tools such as SQL, Python/R, Google Analytics, Looker/Tableau
Identify reporting issues through anomaly detection and behavioral analysis.
Share best practices and help foster a data-driven culture.


What we're looking for


5+ years of experience in data analytics, ideally with 2+ years specifically in performance or affiliate marketing analytics.
Proficiency in SQL: Strong ability to query large datasets across multiple tables and optimize complex joins.
Experience with Analytical Tools: Hands-on experience with data visualization and BI platforms (e.g., Looker, Tableau, Power BI).
Statistical Programming: Working knowledge of Python or R for advanced analytics, data wrangling, and automation.
A/B Testing & Experimentation: Experience designing, executing, and analyzing split tests with an understanding of statistical significance.
Data Modeling & Forecasting: Ability to create predictive models and perform regression, cohort, and lifetime value analysis.
Deep knowledge of affiliate channels, performance marketing KPIs, attribution models, and conversion funnels is a plus
Proven track record of turning data into actionable insights that drive business decisions and ROI improvements.
Experience working in fast-paced digital or e-commerce environments.
Strong Communication Skills: Ability to present complex data in a clear, concise, and actionable manner to non-technical stakeholders.
Business Acumen: Capable of understanding marketing strategy and aligning data initiatives with business goals.
Problem-Solving Mindset: Analytical thinker with a proactive approach to identifying and solving problems.


What we offer


Real Impact: Your insights will directly shape marketing strategy and revenue decisions — this is a role where data drives the business, not just the reports
Interesting & Diverse Data: Work across multiple competitive verticals and acquisition channels, with complex, real-world datasets that will challenge and grow your analytical skill set
Collaborative Environment: Work closely with engineering, product, and marketing teams in a dynamic, data-driven culture that values your expertise
Competitive Compensation: A competitive salary, performance-based incentives, and private healthcare benefits
Flexible & Remote-Friendly: Work remotely, on-site, or hybrid — with the flexibility to suit your lifestyle

Title: Data Analytics Analyst
Description:
Octus

Octus is a leading global provider of credit intelligence, data, and analytics. Since 2013, tens of thousands of professionals across hedge fund, investment banking, management consulting, and law firm verticals have come to rely on Octus to make better, faster, and more confident decisions in pace with the fast-moving credit markets.
For more information, visit: https://octus.com/

Working at Octus

Octus hires growth-minded innovators and trailblazers across the globe to drive our business and culture. Our core values – Action Oriented, Customer First Mindset, Effective Team Players, and Driven to Excel – define an organizational ethos that’s as high-performing as it is human. Among other perks, Octus employees enjoy competitive health benefits, matched 401k and pension plans, PTO, generous parental leave, gym subsidies, educational reimbursements for career development, recognition programs, pet-friendly offices (US only), and much more. 

Role

Octus Fundamentals delivers comprehensive financial data for informed credit decisions, covering reported and standardised financials, KPIs, tear sheets, and complex capital structures for more than 3,000 corporates globally across both publicly listed and privately held companies. Our dataset encompasses corporates across the sub-investment-grade liquid credit and private credit universe. 

We are looking for a Data & Analytics Analyst to join our Fundamentals EMEA team. The successful candidate will play a central role in building and maintaining market-wide datasets that enable our subscribers – professionals across hedge funds, investment banks, and management consulting firms – to benchmark issuers across sectors, regions, instrument types, and company sizes. The role sits at the intersection of financial analysis, data engineering, and product development.

This is a hands-on, high-impact position offering direct exposure to how leading credit market participants use data in their workflows, with meaningful opportunities to shape how our products evolve.

Responsibilities:

Fundamentals Data Analytics

Own and regularly update the Market Data file, maintaining accuracy and completeness of aggregated metrics across Octus's non-investment-grade liquid credit coverage universe
Launch a similar Market Data file for the private credit universe 
Respond to ad-hoc client data requests, running custom analyses and extracts to meet specific benchmarking or screening needs. This entails producing anonymised benchmarking analyses across sectors, regions, instrument types (bonds, loans) and company sizes. 

Data Quality & Mapping 

Develop and enhance the financial data quality control framework, with the goal of minimising data errors and providing feedback and training to financial analysts on best practices.
Collaborate with the Fundamentals financials and capital structure analyst teams to resolve data discrepancies and maintain dataset integrity
Support internal and external mapping exercises, ensuring consistency between company identifiers, instrument references, and data sources across systems

Automation & AI

Identify, design, and implement automation solutions to streamline repetitive data operations and reporting workflows across the Fundamentals department
Leverage AI tools and large language models to enhance data extraction, classification, and analysis pipelines
Build and maintain dashboards and query templates to support the broader analyst team with scalable, self-serve data access

Cross-functional Support & Product Development

Assist the Fundamentals analyst teams with ad-hoc internal data requests, query setup, and analytical tasks
Develop a deep understanding of how Octus clients use the platform and contribute ideas for improving data products and user experience
Requirements:
2+ years of experience in a data analytics, financial data, or credit research role
Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business, Finance, Accounting, Data Science, Engineering, or a related discipline
Knowledge of financial reporting and accounting standards, and the ability to interpret financial statements and assess financial performance
Familiarity with credit instruments and credit market dynamics; understanding of investment banking and credit analysis workflows is a strong advantage
Advanced Excel skills including complex formulas, pivot tables, macros, Power Query, and large-dataset manipulation
Proficiency in Python and/or SQL for data manipulation, automation scripting, and workflow optimisation
Experience with Microsoft automation and visualisation tools: Power Automate, Power BI, or equivalent platforms
Working knowledge of AI tools and large language models (e.g. ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude) for data extraction, document analysis, or process automation; experience building AI-assisted workflows is a plus

Equal Employment Opportunity

Octus is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, marital status, pregnancy, veteran status, or any other legally protected status. We strive to create an inclusive and diverse work environment where all individuals are valued, respected, and treated fairly. We believe that diversity enriches our workplace and enhances our ability to innovate and succeed.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Who We Are

Ovoko is transforming Europe’s €30B+ used car parts market - a massive, fragmented industry still largely offline. We connect 6,000+ scrapyard owners, car parts sellers and dismantlers with millions of buyers through our marketplace, SaaS and logistics platform, making it effortless to trade parts across borders. With over 35 million parts listed, we’ve grown into one of Europe’s fastest-scaling e-commerce companies, but the real opportunity lies ahead, as we build a real category-defining platform worldwide.

About The Role

Fuelled by a recent $20M Series B funding round and consistent triple-digit year-over-year growth, Ovoko is continuing to scale its Data & Analytics function. Over the past year, our Data Org has focused on building strong foundations - modernising data architecture, tooling, and ways of working. Now, the focus is shifting towards driving real business impact through data.

To support this next phase, we are expanding our Data Team and looking for a Data Analyst to join us. This role will be part of a growing team working closely with multiple product and business areas across Ovoko: Product, Money, Business, Marketing and more. The exact scope may evolve depending on your strengths and interests - from product analytics to business operations or other domains.

If you're curious, proactive, and excited about solving real business problems with data - this role is a great opportunity to grow and make an impact in one of Europe’s fastest-growing e-commerce startups.

IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL


Define KPIs, identify business opportunities, and estimate their impact using data insights.
Build dashboards, implement simple ETL, and utilize ML techniques for complex solutions.
Design and analyze experiments (A/B tests).
Use advanced analytics and ML techniques for complex data solutions.
Work with modern tooling such as Snowflake, Looker, Mixpanel, and explore opportunities in the evolving AI landscape.

Title: DATA ANALYST (F/M/D) | IGNITIS RENEWABLES
Description:
Do You want to be part of „Ignitis Group“?

We are looking for a Data Analyst to join the Operations and Maintenance department at Ignitis Renewables. You will play a key role in quantifying asset performance and identifying trends to ensure responsible ownership. You’ll collaborate closely with Asset Supervisors, using Python and SQL to transform data into reliable dashboards and statistical analyses. If that speaks to you, we’d be happy to welcome you to our team!

You will contribute to green and secure energy ecosystem creation by:



Using data to solve complex problems and find ways to improve asset performance, even when working with uncertain information
Writing and maintaining the code and tools (using Python, SQL) that keep our data workflows organized and reliable
Monitoring operational data to identify trends, detect anomalies, and highlight potential issues or opportunities related to asset performance
Contributing to the team’s roadmap and analytical priorities by providing insights that support operational decision-making and long-term performance optimization
Presenting analytical findings clearly through dashboards, reports, and discussions with stakeholders to support data-informed decisions
Documenting analytical workflows, datasets, dashboards, and coding standards to ensure transparency, reproducibility, and knowledge sharing within the team



Required for this position:



A degree in a technical field such as data analytics, engineering, mathematics, or a related discipline, or equivalent experience working in a data-driven environment
2–5 years of experience in data analytics, delivering dashboards, reports, and analyses that support operational or business decision-making
Strong proficiency in SQL and Python for data cleaning, statistical analysis, and extracting insights from complex datasets
Practical experience with version control tools such as Git and GitLab to maintain organized, reproducible, and auditable code
Experience building and maintaining Power BI dashboards connected to SQL databases to support operational and ad-hoc reporting
Familiarity with collaboration tools such as Jira, Confluence, and Microsoft Teams for coordinating work and documenting analytical outputs
High proficiency in English to clearly communicate analytical findings, write reports, and collaborate with international stakeholders
Ability to work independently on recurring analytical tasks while applying statistical reasoning and creativity to solve new or complex problems



In return for creating a more sustainable future, we promise:



To take part in an unfolding energy transformation process to create a 100% green and secure energy ecosystem for both current and future generations
International team that is working on dynamic renewable energy projects
A hybrid work model (3+2) and flexible working hours
Extensive learning and qualification enhancement programs
Attention to physical and emotional health: wellness mentor initiatives and psychological support etc.
Additional paid days for health improvement, short-term illness, volunteering, and accumulated work experience



Join a growing and increasingly international team of energy smart people united by a common purpose. We all play our part in creating a 100% green and secure energy ecosystem for current and future generations.

And the way to achieve this is certainly not to wait for the perfect moment. We believe that the power to act comes from the charge we bring to our work every day. United by purpose. Owning the challenge. Passion for reinvention. Pursuing excellence. These are our mindsets. Together, they yield the energy that drives everything we do. The energy generated by our choices. If you are guided by the same mindsets – we look forward to welcoming you to our team!

As we pursue our strategic goals to increase gender balance in Ignitis Group, we are indicating “f/m/d” next to job titles, meaning we welcome candidates of all genders – female, male or diverse.

Ignitis Group ensures equal opportunities. We welcome all applicants and do not discriminate against race, religion, gender, sexual orientation or gender identity, age, disability or other personal traits unrelated to work obligations.

Salary range for this position group: is 2770 to 4150 EUR (before taxes). The final offer depends on experience and competence. Variable part of remuneration may be paid depending on results.

About us

Do you want to contribute to projects that are changing the future of green energy generation across the Baltics? Ignitis Renewables is a part of Ignitis Group – a renewables-focused integrated utility. Our purpose is to create a 100% green and secure energy ecosystem for current and future generations.

Ignitis Renewables focuses on developing green generation and green flexibility technologies. By developing onshore and offshore wind, solar, battery and power-to-X technologies, the company is implementing Ignitis Group’s strategic priority to deliver 4–5 GW of installed green capacities by 2030.

www.ignitisrenewables.com

Title: Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst
Description:
Over 50,000 customers globally trust our end-to-end, cloud-driven networking solutions and rely on our top-rated services and support to accelerate their digital transformation efforts and deliver progress like never before and with double digit growth year over year, no provider is better positioned to deliver better outcomes on scale, than Extreme.

Come become part of something big with us! We are a global leader, with hubs in Europe, North America, South America, Asia Pacific, and the Middle East.

Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst

About The Role

We are seeking a “Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst” to join our Extreme Networks Global Operations organization, where you'll turn complex operational data into clear, actionable insight that drives decisions and automation across our Global Supply Chan and Order Management teams. This role sits at the intersection of analytics, AI, and supply chain strategy, partnering with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities, build analytical products, and tell the stories behind the numbers. The ideal candidate is a curious, technically strong analyst who is equally comfortable writing SQL, using AI tools, framing a business problem, managing a project, and presenting findings to stakeholders. You should be excited about the rapid evolution of AI and agentic workflows and how they can be applied to supply chain decision-making and you should be actively using AI tools in your work today.

A Note On Experience

This role requires commercially delivered analytical work. We will ask you at interview to walk through a specific project from problem through to business outcome that you owned and delivered in a professional setting. Academic or course-based projects do not qualify. If your experience is primarily from an academic programme, this role is not the right fit at this time

Key Responsibilities


Partner with supply chain stakeholders to scope problems and translate them into structured, measurable, automated workstreams.
Build and maintain python/SQL/Snowflake-based data models, dashboards, and self-service analytics for our global operations teams
Help operationalize AI and agent-based solutions to automate recurring analyses, augment decision support, and supply chain processes.
Apply statistical methods to inform data driven decisions across our global operations function.
Manage your projects end-to-end: scoping, stakeholder alignment, data discovery, modelling, validation, and rollout.
Translate analytical findings into compelling narratives (written, visual, and verbal) tailored to operational, business, and technical audiences.
Define and track KPIs that measure supply chain performance and flag interventions when metrics drift.
Contribute to ongoing improvements in data quality, documentation, and analytical standards across the team.


Required Skills & Qualifications


3-5 years experience in business analytics, data analytics, or a comparable quantitative role.
Demonstrated use of AI tools such as Claude, MS Copilot, or ChatGPT – in an analytical workflow, with a genuine appetite to develop agentic and automation capabilities in role.
Strong python skills and hands-on experience with Snowflake (or a comparable cloud data warehouse such as BigQuery, Redshift, or Databricks).
Demonstrated project management ability, owning deliverables across multiple stakeholders and timelines.
Solid grounding in descriptive statistics, and analytics fundamentals.
Strong data storytelling skills: the ability to distil complexity into clear visuals and narratives that drive action.
Supply chain domain knowledge: familiarity with demand and supply planning, inventory optimization, order management, logistics, procurement, or manufacturing operations.
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).


Technical Skills Required


Programming for analytics: Python or R for modelling, automation, and analytics workflows.
BI and visualization tools: Tableau, Sigma, Power BI, Looker, or similar — both for building and for design literacy.
ERP / source systems: Exposure to Oracle Fusion Cloud or similar transactional systems.


Preferred / Additional Skills: These are skills that would meaningfully strengthen a candidate for this role:


Practical experience applying agentic workflows (e.g., LLM-based assistants, retrieval-augmented analytics, automated reasoning agents) to analytical or operational problems.
Master's or PhD in a quantitative field (Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).
Forecasting and optimization: time-series methods as well as other optimization techniques.
Machine learning fundamentals: classification, regression, clustering, and an understanding of when ML is and isn't the right tool.
Process improvement methodologies: Lean, Six Sigma, or equivalent structured problem-solving frameworks.
Stakeholder management and influencing: navigating ambiguity, managing competing priorities, and building trust with non-technical partners.


What Success Looks Like


Within 3 months: deeply understand our supply chain data landscape, key stakeholders, and top-priority business questions.
Within 6 months: deliver at least one high-impact analytical product or AI-enabled workflow that changes how a decision is made.
Within 12 months: become a trusted analytics partner to supply chain stakeholders and a contributor to the broader analytics team's standards and practices.


Salary Is Based On Qualifications And Experience Between 60-70K

Extreme Networks Benefits

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Company Overview

Holafly is a high-growth scale-up revolutionising how businesses and travellers connect to the internet abroad. Since 2018, we’ve empowered travellers in over 200 destinations worldwide with secure and reliable eSIM solutions. With a team of 500+ professionals across multiple countries, we are scaling globally to support travellers with seamless, unlimited data connectivity.

We’re not just connecting people—we’re enabling freedom and peace of mind, ensuring our users stay connected from the second they land, wherever their journey takes them.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly analytical and detail-oriented Data Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will be responsible for transforming complex data into understandable and conclusive insights. You will play a key role in training different business areas on the effective use of our Business Intelligence (BI) tools and advising them on how to maximize the value derived from our data. If you have a passion for data storytelling, strong communication skills, and enjoy empowering others with data, we encourage you to apply.

Responsibilities


Collect, clean, analyse, and interpret large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights.
Create clear, concise, and impactful visualisations, reports, and dashboards that effectively communicate findings to various stakeholders.
Develop and deliver training sessions to educate different business units on the functionality and best practices of BI tools.
Provide expert advice and guidance to the business area on how to leverage data to answer key business questions and drive informed decision-making.
Collaborate with data engineers and other teams to ensure data accuracy, accessibility, and consistency.
Identify opportunities for process improvements and data optimisation.
Stay up-to-date with industry best practices and emerging trends in data analysis, BI tools, and data visualisation.


Qualifications


Proven experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar analytical role.
Strong proficiency in SQL.
Extensive experience with Business Intelligence (BI) tools (e.g., Looker Studio, Looker, Tableau, Power BI, Qlik Sense).
Excellent data visualization skills and the ability to present complex information clearly and concisely.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical thinking abilities.
Exceptional communication and presentation skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.
Detail-oriented with a strong commitment to data accuracy and integrity.
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, Business Analytics, or a Business related discipline.


Hybrid Modality

We focus on trust and performance. We are implementing a Two phased approach for our Hybrid Modality:

Phase 1: While the teams are establishing ways of working, building trust, and developing their rhythm, we ask that you’re present in the office Three days a week. In-person proximity is key to accelerating the forming stage.

Phase 2: Once the teams are performing, we move to a more relaxed hybrid model. We ask for a minimum of one day per week in the office, centred around critical ceremonies such as sprint planning, reviews, and retrospectives.

Benefits & Perks


25 Days of PTO
5% pension contribution
€ 3000 for Medical/Health coverage
Self development budget of $ 500


During a trip to Thailand, Pedro and Lidia, the founders of Holafly, experienced the frustration of not having an internet connection.

Inspired by their own experiences, they came up with a simple but brilliant idea: why not allow travellers to buy data plans for any destination from their home country?

Our passion for providing easy and convenient connectivity to travellers around the world drives everything we do.

We love our work and we do it with pride. Stay connected wherever you go.

Title: Risk Analyst
Description:
Role Overview:

EEZE is expanding rapidly across multiple live casino studios and international offices and as a result, we are seeking a Risk Analyst to join our growing team. The Risk Analyst plays a key role in monitoring, assessing, and managing the integrity of live games and gaming environments. The primary goal of the role is to proactively identify and mitigate fraudulent activities affecting game integrity, ensuring prompt detection, analysis, and resolution.




Key Responsibilities:

Investigate and assess game performance and player data for signs of advantage play, fraud, collusion and game malfunctions.
Conduct detailed analyses of player data and game metrics to identify risks and trends that may impact gaming integrity.
Provide support and assistance to Risk Analysts in assessing game performance and identifying potential risks.
Collaborate with GIRM Team and the Product team to assess game performance and provide insights for improvements.
Maintain familiarity with the games at a facility and with strategies and techniques used by players at such games.
Assist in implementing and maintaining systems and tools for continuous risk monitoring.
Provide reporting and documentation on identified risks and ongoing investigations.
Report any abnormal issues or situations to the Head of Gaming Integrity and Risk Management.
Perform additional duties as assigned by the manager in relation to the position.




Qualifications & Skills:

Higher education or ongoing studies.
Previous experience in related field.
A mathematical or quantitative academic background, relevant professional experience, or demonstrable expertise in data analysis.
Excellent knowledge of English (both written and spoken).
Mathematical and analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex data.Solid understanding of gaming security, fraud prevention, and risk management practices.
Advanced knowledge of MS Office, especially Excel and Word.
Key personal traits: critical thinking, detail oriented and capable of working independently.




What’s in it for you?

Experience a dynamic and team-orientated work environment.
Opportunities for personal growth and learning.
An open, inclusive and supportive team where you will be valued, and your suggestions will be welcome.
24 days paid holiday per year. This is in addition to local public holidays.
Hybrid Working (4 days office/1 day home).
Life Assurance (2x annual salary).
Private Medical Insurance.
€400 annual wellness allowance.
Team Building Opportunities.
€80 Monthly lunch allowance
Parking (limited).
Local discounts and more.

  • Collaborate with GIRM Team and the Product team to assess game performance and provide insights for improvements.
  • Maintain familiarity with the games at a facility and with strategies and techniques used by players at such games.
  • Assist in implementing and maintaining systems and tools for continuous risk monitoring.
  • Provide reporting and documentation on identified risks and ongoing investigations.
  • Report any abnormal issues or situations to the Head of Gaming Integrity and Risk Management.
  • Perform additional duties as assigned by the manager in relation to the position.


Qualifications & Skills:

  • Higher education or ongoing studies.
  • Previous experience in related field.
  • A mathematical or quantitative academic background, relevant professional experience, or demonstrable expertise in data analysis.
  • Excellent knowledge of English (both written and spoken).
  • Mathematical and analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex data.Solid understanding of gaming security, fraud prevention, and risk management practices.
  • Advanced knowledge of MS Office, especially Excel and Word.
  • Key personal traits: critical thinking, detail oriented and capable of working independently.


What’s in it for you?

  • Experience a dynamic and team-orientated work environment.
  • Opportunities for personal growth and learning.
  • An open, inclusive and supportive team where you will be valued, and your suggestions will be welcome.
  • 24 days paid holiday per year. This is in addition to local public holidays.
  • Hybrid Working (4 days office/1 day home).
  • Life Assurance (2x annual salary).
  • Private Medical Insurance.
  • €400 annual wellness allowance.
  • Team Building Opportunities.
  • €80 Monthly lunch allowance
  • Parking (limited).
  • Local discounts and more.

Title: Product Data Analyst
HTML:
Working at Omnisend is... kind of boring. Not the soul-crushing kind of boring — more like the “no drama, no chaos, just smart people doing great work” kind. The kind that lets you focus on what matters, without pointless meetings, micromanagement, or bureaucracy. We’re data-driven. We test, we iterate, we improve. Gut feelings don’t drive decisions — insights do. We trust each other. We’re transparent, give real feedback, and actually listen. No egos, no hand-holding — just a team of experienced professionals moving fast, adapting easily, and delivering impact. Omnisend is customer-funded and customer-driven. Our work is rooted in solving real problems that help ecommerce brands grow. No empty promises — we bring in results. And yes, we have fun. But the grown-up kind — workations, team gatherings, the occasional party. No forced fun, no awkward icebreakers. Just genuine moments with a team that actually enjoys working together.

We are looking for a Product Data Analyst to join our Data Ops team. In this position, you will collaborate closely with product teams. You will look for the biggest business opportunities, explain user behavior, raise and test hypotheses, use advanced statistical methods and predictive models, forecast future trends, and perform deep-dive analysis. You will not work on small ad-hoc data requests, we expect that our data driven product managers will take care of this, while you will be taking care of the advanced statistics. We are looking for an independent and experienced professional who would contribute to a data driven approach in the company. Interpreting data and deducting actionable insights should be your main competence, with a rich record of successful high-impact projects over your career.

Some tools we use: BigQuery, Superset, dbt, Hightouch, Mixpanel, Dagster, Python and Airbyte.

What you’ll do @ Omnisend:


  • Build predictive data models using advanced statistical methods;
  • Provide actionable insights from data;
  • Identify business opportunities and estimate their impact;
  • Lead data-driven decision-making in a group of stakeholders;
  • Build dashboards;
  • Coach on best practices in data analysis to foster a data-driven culture.


We expect that you’ll bring:


  • Highly skilled in data visualization and story-telling;
  • Ability to translate complex data into clear business insights;
  • Experience building predictive models using advanced statistical methods;
  • Experience in Superset, Power BI, Looker, Tableau, or similar visualization tools;
  • Working experience with SQL;
  • Business-driven mindset;
  • Good knowledge of statistical analysis;
  • Education in statistics, mathematics, econometrics, economics, or related fields;
  • Fluency in English.


Snapshot of your impact, by the 3-month mark:


  • Complete Omnisend new-hire onboarding and job-specific training for the Marketing Data Analyst role;
  • Gain clarity on expectation of your role and gain understanding of your team’s processes;
  • Identify needs for your TEAM and ROLE and build out your OKRs for the quarter;
  • Fully onboard on using team’s tools like Airbyte, dbt, Hightouch, Dagster and Superset;
  • Get familiar with all product teams;
  • Gain clarity on Product team’s processes;
  • Start building the foundation of strong relationships with Omnisend teammates.


What we offer in return:


  • Gross salary starting point from 4200 EUR/month based on experience;
  • Quarterly salary reviews;
  • An unlimited learning budget for self-improvement complying with the best interest of Omnisend (after 3 months);
  • Working methods and best practices inspired by the best tech companies in Silicon Valley;
  • Senior colleagues enable us to have a high-performing, productive environment where your growth will accelerate;
  • Flexible working hours and remote work possibilities;
  • Private health insurance;
  • Unlimited access to psychotherapy;
  • Workstation budget (420 Eur);
  • Work anniversary gifts like house cleaning services, bikes, or scooters;
  • Attractive workations and team-led team-building events.


Omnisend is an email and SMS marketing platform built for ecommerce brands. With one-click integration to major ecommerce platforms, intuitive automation, and powerful email templates, we make sophisticated marketing effortless — no learning curve required.

Award-winning 24/7/365 customer support? Check. A platform trusted by growing brands and seasoned pros alike? Absolutely. The idea that “great marketing needs to be expensive”? Our 150,000+ customers prove that wrong every day.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Staff Data Analyst
Description:
About Analog Devices

Analog Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ: ADI ) is a global semiconductor leader that bridges the physical and digital worlds to enable breakthroughs at the Intelligent Edge. ADI combines analog, digital, and software technologies into solutions that help drive advancements in digitized factories, mobility, and digital healthcare, combat climate change, and reliably connect humans and the world. With revenue of more than $9 billion in FY24 and approximately 24,000 people globally, ADI ensures today's innovators stay Ahead of What's Possible™. Learn more at www.analog.com and on LinkedIn and Twitter (X).

Staff Data Analyst, Engineering Analytics

Location: Limerick, Ireland

Overview

As a Staff Data Analyst on the Engineering Enablement team, you will own the development and evolution of analytics dashboards that surface insights on product development and engineering tool usage. These dashboards synthesize large amounts of engineering data from various sources and are broadly used by both engineering teams and executive leadership. You will rely on sound judgment, curiosity, and strong communication skills to translate ambiguous engineering workflows into well-defined metrics and clear, meaningful reports.

Responsibilities


Engage directly with engineering domain teams (design, verification, IP, AI etc.) to understand workflows, identify high-value data sources, and define key telemetry and productivity metrics.
Define data schemas, grain, and transformation logic needed for each data source, and communicate those requirements clearly to the Central Data team for implementation.
Design and maintain a Power BI semantic model (data model, relationships, DAX measures) that serves as a shared analytical foundation across multiple thin reports.
Build, publish, and iterate on Power BI dashboards and reports that deliver clear insights to engineering managers and leadership.
Collaborate with Central Data team to scope, prioritize, and validate pipeline work; serve as the functional owner of data correctness and metric definitions.
Document metric definitions, data lineage, and report logic to ensure institutional knowledge is durable and auditable.
Identify opportunities to expand impact — new data sources, new engineering personas, new use cases — and drive those through requirements definition and delivery.


Qualifications


8+ years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or a related field.
Degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Business Analytics, Statistics, or a related quantitative field.
Expert-level skills in Power BI or similar tool (Qlikview, Tableau, DataStudio, etc.), including data modeling, DAX, report design, workspace/deployment pipeline management.
Strong SQL skills; ability to write and review transformation logic even when not directly authoring pipelines.
Demonstrated experience working directly with non-technical stakeholders to define metrics and translate domain knowledge into data requirements.
Strong written and verbal communication skills; comfortable presenting findings and influencing priorities with engineering and executive leadership.
Experience owning an analytics product end-to-end — from requirements through delivery and ongoing maintenance.


Additional Qualifications (nice To Have)


Familiarity with consuming data from Snowflake, dbt, or similar modern data stack components.
Certifications in Power BI or related Microsoft data tools.
Experience with Python for data validation, exploration, or lightweight transformation.
Experience in the semiconductor industry or familiarity with EDA tools and flows.


For positions requiring access to technical data, Analog Devices, Inc. may have to obtain export licensing approval from the U.S. Department of Commerce - Bureau of Industry and Security and/or the U.S. Department of State - Directorate of Defense Trade Controls. As such, applicants for this position – except US Citizens, US Permanent Residents, and protected individuals as defined by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3) – may have to go through an export licensing review process.

Analog Devices is an equal opportunity employer. We foster a culture where everyone has an opportunity to succeed regardless of their race, color, religion, age, ancestry, national origin, social or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, pregnancy, parental status, disability, medical condition, genetic information, military or veteran status, union membership, and political affiliation, or any other legally protected group.

Job Req Type: Experienced

Required Travel: Yes, 10% of the time

Shift Type: 1st Shift/Days

Title: Big Data Analyst
Description:
At ReversingLabs, our software supply chain security and threat intelligence solutions have become essential to advancing cybersecurity maturity around the globe. We're on a journey to expand adoption and accelerate growth by hiring top talent across the security industry.

Notable breaches such as SolarWinds, CircleCI, and 3CX have elevated software supply chain security as a top initiative across every organization developing or purchasing software. Only ReversingLabs delivers the software package analysis speed and intelligence needed to protect against this critical risk area.

Our vision is clear - arming every company with the insight to ensure every piece of software built or bought is safe and every threat detected. An innovative vision requires innovative talent to help forge this transformational journey at ReversingLabs. ReversingLabs is recognized by the cybersecurity industry as a leader in innovation. The $56M investment from Crosspoint Capital will support our growth and innovation efforts to build scalable, automated, and intelligent software security solutions.

We are looking for a passionate Big Data Analyst with a keen eye for detail and the ability to understand data. The successful candidate will turn data into information, information into insight, and insight into business decisions.

You will be responsible for developing and managing our master data set, developing reports, and troubleshooting data issues. To do well in this role, you need a keen eye for detail, some experience as a data analyst, and an understanding of popular data analysis tools and databases.

We offer file reputation records for over 50 billion samples, making our database the largest reputation database. Our customers receive the information through more than 50 REST APIs and Feeds. By performing hands-on data research, you will provide insights, uncover potential issues, and enhance the decision-making process. You will perform complex analysis on large data sets using appropriate data analysis techniques and tools with a strong focus on quality.

Responsibilities


Gathering data from various sources and then cleansing, organizing, processing, and analyzing it to extract valuable insights and information
Working with various database types to handle large amounts of data in structured, semi-structured, and unstructured form
Developing reports and dashboards (charts, tables, ...) with data analysis results to help different audiences make better decisions
Identifying, analyzing, and interpreting trends or patterns in complex data sets
Understanding sources and lineage of data to control the quality of our data sets
Collaborating with team members to prioritize business and information requirements
Ensure data quality by implementing data validation and cleansing processes
Performing routine analysis tasks to support day-to-day business
Applying statistical analysis methods for consumer data research and analysis purposes
Maintain and improve existing data pipelines
Closely collaborating with both the IT teams and customer-facing teams to accomplish company goals and prove value to our customers


Requirements


Strong analytical skills with the ability to collect, organize, analyze, and disseminate significant amounts of information with attention to detail and accuracy
Experience with data visualization tools
Solid understanding of data warehousing concepts and ETL/ELT processes
Experience with some programming languages (Python, Java, ...)
Experience with databases and writing queries
Working proficiency in written and spoken English
Enthusiasm for teamwork, constant learning, and adapting to new circumstances


Desired Skills


Knowledge of statistics and experience with statistical packages for analyzing datasets
Proficiency in creating complex data models, database design, and data mining techniques
Expertise in using techniques like clustering, association rule mining, and customer segmentation to uncover valuable insights
Proven ability to understand and apply data insights to solve real-world business problems
Experience in creating concise and informative analysis reports and summaries that effectively communicate key findings and recommendations


Benefits


Hybrid work options (paid accommodation & transportation to Zagreb during onboarding for remote employees)
Flexible working hours
Generous compensation and a bonus system based on annual performance
Hefty personal education budget and possibility to attend leading conferences and seminars in the field
Company library and possibility to order books of choice via Amazon
Permanent contract in a fast-growing global company with Fortune 500 & governmental agencies as clients
Challenging projects in a dynamic, collaborative team
Great career advancement opportunities - clear goals & internal promotions
Employee referral bonus program, EUR 1,060 net for junior position, EUR 2,123 net for mid to senior positions, and EUR 2,654 net for principal/managerial positions
Multisport card, annual health checkup, newborn child allowance, rent-cost, and 3rd pillar pension benefits
Wellness Weekends - quarterly, company-wide three day weekend, starting with a company paid Friday off for all employees
Fully covered car garage in Radnička for all employees

Title: Data Lead (Growth & Analytics)
Description:
Mid-senior position | Part-time or Full-time | On-site @Ljubljana




About the role

We're building a real-world social platform. Matched meetups that get people out of their homes and turn strangers into friends. It works. Now we need someone to build the data infrastructure that helps us scale it.




As our Data Lead (Growth & Analytics) you'll own everything data: event tracking, pipelines, the dashboards that drive our decisions. If a metric matters to the business, you built the system that surfaces it.




We're an AI-first team - that includes BI. We're building AI-powered dashboards and we want someone who finds that exciting and has done this in the past.




You'll be one of our first 10 team members. Early enough to shape how we instrument, what we trust, and how decisions get made. If you've spent years in a SaaS or subscription business and you intuitively know which numbers lie and which ones matter, you'll fit right in.




It's fast. It's sometimes messy. The problems are real and the impact is direct.




You’ll be a great fit if you:

Work best without permission
Default to "how could I systemize this"
Had weird teenage hobbies
Spend your Sundays on side projects
Shipped something this week
Are good at chess, poker or Monopoly
Forget to grab lunch because you’re in the zone
Get the best ideas while showering
Have left a job because the pace was too slow
Are more motivated by winning than by being right
Find bureaucracy physically painful




What you’ll be doing

Data infrastructure

Own data architecture decisions and tooling selection across marketing, revenue, finance, product, and operations
Set up and maintain a data warehouse
Set up experimentation tooling
Ensure data accuracy, freshness, and consistency
Implement data validation, anomaly detection, and monitoring

Tracking

Build attribution tracking for Meta Ads, Google Ads, email and other channels
Measure reliable data at every step of the acquisition funnel
Diagnose and resolve tracking discrepancies across platforms
Work with other teams to implement and QA tracking changes

Data analysis

Translate data into clear, actionable recommendations for leadership
Build forecasting models for revenue, churn, and growth scenarios
Identify leading indicators and growth constraints
Support strategic decisions around pricing, roadmap, and scale strategy
Implement cohort dashboards, churn prediction tools, and LTV/CAC reporting
Segmentation by geographies, niches, channels, etc
User feedback analysis
Operational efficiency analysis

Experimentation

Set success metrics and guardrails for experiments
Run A/B tests on landing pages, onboarding funnels, pricing pages, upsells, etc
Evaluating A/B tests and suggesting new ones

Product & business insights

Analyze user behavior across onboarding, activation, engagement, and retention
Identify friction points and drop-offs in core user journeys
Partner with product, marketing, and ops to drive improvements




Requirements

Essential:

Fluent in English (written and spoken)
At least 3 years of relevant experience
Experience working with a subscription/SaaS business (familiar with metrics such as MRR, churn, cash conversion cycle,...)
Comfortable working with SQL, data modeling, BI, retention/LTV analysis, attribution, and forecasting.
AI-first mindset - ability to heavily rely on AI tools and build in such a way that we can keep leveraging AI in the future
Strong revenue strategy mindset: understands monetisation, pricing, payback logic, and CAC/LTV trade-offs.
Comfortable working in ambiguity, adapting to changes and wearing multiple hats
Ability to identify ideal software and integrate it into the company’s workflow
Attention to detail and the ability to work independently without constant oversight
A lot of drive, energy, and time.
Ability to work from our office in Ljubljana city center

Bonus points:

Previous startup experience (especially early-stage)
Experience building teams from scratch
You are based close to Ljubljana (or willing to move)




About us

We help people meet new friends through real-life events all around the world.




We’re building something ambitious. Small team. Global startup. Serious brains. Real responsibility. Fast growth. Mobile app / B2C SaaS.




We are past product-market fit and are now in fast-scaling mode.




If you want to ship fast, work hard, own systems end-to-end, and grow with the company - this is for you.




Founded by Jani Pravdič and Matic Jesenovec – entrepreneurs who've together built multiple businesses impacting hundreds of thousands of people and generating well over eight figures in revenue. We're now building something that we believe has the potential to grow much larger and have a greater impact than anything we’ve done before.




What’s in it for you

Working closely with the founders
Working in a high-performance team
It takes us hours instead of weeks to launch/implement something
We test things and we listen to the numbers, not who is the loudest in the room
Failure is part of the process; we accept it and learn from it
Opportunity to grow into a higher role
The chance to genuinely impact many thousands of lives globally
A front-row seat in an exciting, fast-growing start-up
Competitive salary
Performance bonuses




Our values

Strategic Boldness: We do what it takes to deliver faster, farther, and louder.
Agility > Perfection: We value action over polish, effectiveness over effort. Ship fast, refine later.
Scrappy & Curious: We test, learn, and adapt constantly. Resourceful beats resource-rich.
Ambition + Humility: We learn from the best, then push beyond. Inspiration fuels our innovation.
Long Game Thinking: We’re impatient with action, patient with results. Correct quickly, stay the course.




Next steps

We’re looking for someone to start as soon as possible. So if the role sounds exciting, please follow these steps:

Fill out the application form.
Our team carefully reviews every application, so the selection process may take some time.
The best candidates will receive an additional test assignment.
Those who perform best on this task will be invited to an extensive interview.




If you do not hear from us within 14 days of submitting your application, it means we have decided to continue the process with other candidates at this time.

We're building a real-world social platform. Matched meetups that get people out of their homes and turn strangers into friends. It works. Now we need someone to build the data infrastructure that helps us scale it.


As our Data Lead (Growth & Analytics) you'll own everything data: event tracking, pipelines, the dashboards that drive our decisions. If a metric matters to the business, you built the system that surfaces it.


We're an AI-first team - that includes BI. We're building AI-powered dashboards and we want someone who finds that exciting and has done this in the past.


You'll be one of our first 10 team members. Early enough to shape how we instrument, what we trust, and how decisions get made. If you've spent years in a SaaS or subscription business and you intuitively know which numbers lie and which ones matter, you'll fit right in.


It's fast. It's sometimes messy. The problems are real and the impact is direct.


You’ll be a great fit if you:

  • Work best without permission
  • Default to "how could I systemize this"
  • Had weird teenage hobbies
  • Spend your Sundays on side projects
  • Shipped something this week
  • Are good at chess, poker or Monopoly
  • Forget to grab lunch because you’re in the zone
  • Get the best ideas while showering
  • Have left a job because the pace was too slow
  • Are more motivated by winning than by being right
  • Find bureaucracy physically painful


What you’ll be doing

Data infrastructure

  • Own data architecture decisions and tooling selection across marketing, revenue, finance, product, and operations
  • Set up and maintain a data warehouse
  • Set up experimentation tooling
  • Ensure data accuracy, freshness, and consistency
  • Implement data validation, anomaly detection, and monitoring

Tracking

  • Build attribution tracking for Meta Ads, Google Ads, email and other channels
  • Measure reliable data at every step of the acquisition funnel
  • Diagnose and resolve tracking discrepancies across platforms
  • Work with other teams to implement and QA tracking changes

Data analysis

  • Translate data into clear, actionable recommendations for leadership
  • Build forecasting models for revenue, churn, and growth scenarios
  • Identify leading indicators and growth constraints
  • Support strategic decisions around pricing, roadmap, and scale strategy
  • Implement cohort dashboards, churn prediction tools, and LTV/CAC reporting
  • Segmentation by geographies, niches, channels, etc
  • User feedback analysis
  • Operational efficiency analysis

Experimentation

  • Set success metrics and guardrails for experiments
  • Run A/B tests on landing pages, onboarding funnels, pricing pages, upsells, etc
  • Evaluating A/B tests and suggesting new ones

Product & business insights

  • Analyze user behavior across onboarding, activation, engagement, and retention
  • Identify friction points and drop-offs in core user journeys
  • Partner with product, marketing, and ops to drive improvements


Requirements

Essential:

  • Fluent in English (written and spoken)
  • At least 3 years of relevant experience
  • Experience working with a subscription/SaaS business (familiar with metrics such as MRR, churn, cash conversion cycle,...)
  • Comfortable working with SQL, data modeling, BI, retention/LTV analysis, attribution, and forecasting.
  • AI-first mindset - ability to heavily rely on AI tools and build in such a way that we can keep leveraging AI in the future
  • Strong revenue strategy mindset: understands monetisation, pricing, payback logic, and CAC/LTV trade-offs.
  • Comfortable working in ambiguity, adapting to changes and wearing multiple hats
  • Ability to identify ideal software and integrate it into the company’s workflow
  • Attention to detail and the ability to work independently without constant oversight
  • A lot of drive, energy, and time.
  • Ability to work from our office in Ljubljana city center

Bonus points:

  • Previous startup experience (especially early-stage)
  • Experience building teams from scratch
  • You are based close to Ljubljana (or willing to move)


About us

We help people meet new friends through real-life events all around the world.


We’re building something ambitious. Small team. Global startup. Serious brains. Real responsibility. Fast growth. Mobile app / B2C SaaS.


We are past product-market fit and are now in fast-scaling mode.


If you want to ship fast, work hard, own systems end-to-end, and grow with the company - this is for you.


Founded by Jani Pravdič and Matic Jesenovec – entrepreneurs who've together built multiple businesses impacting hundreds of thousands of people and generating well over eight figures in revenue. We're now building something that we believe has the potential to grow much larger and have a greater impact than anything we’ve done before.


What’s in it for you

  • Working closely with the founders
  • Working in a high-performance team
  • It takes us hours instead of weeks to launch/implement something
  • We test things and we listen to the numbers, not who is the loudest in the room
  • Failure is part of the process; we accept it and learn from it
  • Opportunity to grow into a higher role
  • The chance to genuinely impact many thousands of lives globally
  • A front-row seat in an exciting, fast-growing start-up
  • Competitive salary
  • Performance bonuses


Our values

  • Strategic Boldness: We do what it takes to deliver faster, farther, and louder.
  • Agility > Perfection: We value action over polish, effectiveness over effort. Ship fast, refine later.
  • Scrappy & Curious: We test, learn, and adapt constantly. Resourceful beats resource-rich.
  • Ambition + Humility: We learn from the best, then push beyond. Inspiration fuels our innovation.
  • Long Game Thinking: We’re impatient with action, patient with results. Correct quickly, stay the course.


Next steps

We’re looking for someone to start as soon as possible. So if the role sounds exciting, please follow these steps:

  1. Fill out the application form.
  2. Our team carefully reviews every application, so the selection process may take some time.
  3. The best candidates will receive an additional test assignment.
  4. Those who perform best on this task will be invited to an extensive interview.


If you do not hear from us within 14 days of submitting your application, it means we have decided to continue the process with other candidates at this time.

Title: Product Data Analyst
Description:
About Phorest

Would you like to create the future of a product used by thousands of businesses and millions of consumers? Phorest Salon Software powers over 11,000 hair and beauty salons in Ireland, UK, Germany, Australia, Finland, USA, Canada, UAE and Netherlands and the platform processes 7 million appointments a month for hair and beauty consumers. Phorest is the number 1 salon software brand for premium independent salons in those markets and our next goal is to become a platform upon which other companies can build applications for premium salons.

Our system touches every point of the salon experience. We have the in-salon software desktop application, Phorest Go (the native staff application), online bookings website for salons, and custom native apps per salon. In a typical month, Phorest processes 3 million appointments and we send over 3 million SMS and 4 million emails. We process 200k online bookings per month and over 150 custom built white label native apps.

The Opportunity

As a Product Data Analyst at Phorest, you’ll be embedded at the heart of our product development process, turning data into clear, actionable insights that shape how we build and improve our software. Working closely with Product Managers, Designers, and Engineers, you’ll help the team understand user behaviour, measure the impact of product changes, and identify opportunities to deliver more value to salons and their clients.

You’ll own product analytics end-to-end — from defining metrics and building dashboards to conducting deep-dive analyses and presenting findings to stakeholders. This is a role for someone who is genuinely curious about how products are used, comfortable working in a fast-moving agile environment, and confident translating complex data into decisions.

What You'll Do


Partner with Product Managers and teams to define success metrics, instrument features, and measure the impact of product changes and releases.
Build and maintain Looker dashboards and explores that surface product usage, engagement, retention, and funnel metrics to stakeholders across the business.
Conduct deep-dive analyses on user behaviour, feature adoption, and churn signals to surface actionable insights that inform product roadmap decisions.
Define and maintain a product metrics framework, ensuring consistency in how KPIs are measured and reported across teams.
Support A/B testing and experimentation, helping teams design experiments, interpret results, and make statistically sound decisions.
Collaborate with Data Engineering to ensure the right events and data points are captured, pipelines are reliable, and the data model supports product analytics needs.
Proactively monitor product metrics, flagging anomalies, trends, and opportunities to the relevant teams.
Contribute to data literacy across product teams, coaching team members on self-serve analytics, dashboard use, and metric interpretation.
Support ad hoc analytical requests from product and leadership, delivering timely and rigorous analysis.


Who You Are


3+ years of experience in data analytics, ideally with some of that time in a product analytics context or working directly alongside a product team.
Strong proficiency in SQL, with the ability to write complex queries across large datasets.
Hands-on experience with Looker (LookML, dashboards, explores) — a core requirement for this role.
Solid understanding of the product development lifecycle and how data fits into agile ways of working.
Strong data visualisation and communication skills — you can tell a clear story with data for both technical and non-technical audiences.
Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail and a rigorous approach to data accuracy.
Self-starter who can manage competing priorities and work independently in a fast-moving environment.


The following are preferred but not required:


Experience in the hair & beauty industry or a vertical SaaS business serving SMEs.
Experience with Python or R for more advanced analysis.


Benefits

🧘 Your wellbeing is important to us - we provide private healthcare, 2 Wellness Days, an employee assistance program and a free online GP service.

💰 As part of our Financial Wellbeing, we provide competitive Compensation, an Employee Share Purchase Scheme, Pension, Life Assurance, and Income Protection.

🚵🏿 We help you travel by providing a bike to work scheme as well as tax saver transport tickets.

🦸‍♀️ We support the women who work in Phorest by offering 2 weeks leave for Fertility Treatment, Pregnancy Loss and Menopause.

🍼 We care for your family and provide Enhanced Maternity and Paternity Benefits.

🌳 We grow our own timber! We provide a great learning environment and extensive development opportunities. We run development programs and provide access to many online resources including LinkedIn learning.

🏠 Moving house? Phorest employees get 3 moving days.

Want to learn more about Phorest? Check out nothingventured.rocks for our blog and Insights on building an evergreen company from the team here at Phorest.

Phorest is an equal opportunity employer. For this position, flexi-time and working from home is possible. We are also open to remote work. Get in touch to ask for more information or to chat about your future with Phorest!

Research shows that while men apply to jobs when they meet an average of 60% of the criteria, women and other marginalised folks tend to only apply when they check every box. So if you think you have what it takes, but don't necessarily meet every single point on the job description, please still get in touch. We'd love to have a chat and see if you could be a great fit.

Title: DATU ANALĪTIĶIS
Description:
Storent Europe ir viens no nedaudziem Latvijas uzņēmumiem, kas veiksmīgi paplašinājies gan Ziemeļeiropā, gan Amerikas Savienotajās Valstīs. Attīstot savu biznesu, aicinām pievienoties komandai datu analītiķi, kurš palīdzēs nodrošināt kvalitatīvu datu analīzi un sniegs biznesam būtiskus ieskatus starptautiskā mērogā.

Šī loma ir vērsta uz biznesa un finanšu datu analīzi, datu kvalitāti un lēmumu pieņemšanas atbalstu.

Mēs piedāvājam:

Karjeras iespējas

un pastāvīgu darba vietu stabilā, starptautiskā uzņēmumā

Spēcīgu komandu līdzās

apmācības un komandas atbalstu darbu veikšanā

Labumu paketi

sociālās garantijas, veselības apdrošināšana, sporta un izklaides pasākumi

Stabilu atalgojumu

2500 - 3500 EUR mēnesī atkarībā no pieredzes

Darbu ar lieliem datu apjomiem

un ar reālu iespēju veikt ietekmi uz biznesa procesiem un lēmumiem

Elastīgu darba režīmu

darbu hibrīda modelī ar iespēju pašam veidot savu darba dienu

Galvenie pienākumi:


Apkopot, analizēt un validēt datus no dažādām sistēmām (ERP, Microsoft Dynamics 365 u.c.)
Nodrošināt datu kvalitāti, identificēt neatbilstības un ierosināt uzlabojumus
Sagatavot regulāras un ad hoc atskaites par KPI, rentabilitāti un biznesa rezultātiem
Analizēt finanšu un operatīvos datus, salīdzināt tos ar budžetu un prognozēm
Izstrādāt un uzturēt datu modeļus un atskaites (Excel vidē un citos rīkos)
Sniegt rekomendācijas biznesa efektivitātes uzlabošanai, balstoties uz datu analīzi
Sadarboties ar finanšu, IT un citām biznesa komandām
Dokumentēt datu loģiku un struktūras


Prasības kandidātam:


Vismaz 3 gadu pieredze datu analīzē, finanšu analīzē (FP&A) vai līdzīgā jomā
Labas SQL zināšanas un pieredze darbā ar datiem no dažādām sistēmām
Ļoti labas Excel prasmes (datu analīze, modeļi, formulas)
Izpratne par datu struktūrām un biznesa datu loģiku
Zināšanas par datu verifikācijas metodēm un prasme pārbaudīt datu pareizību
Spēja analizēt lielus datu apjomus un identificēt būtiskāko
Labas angļu valodas zināšanas, kas nepieciešamas programmu lietošanai un saziņai ar kolēģiem grupas līmenī


Prasmes un kompetences:


Spēcīga analītiskā domāšana un problēmu risināšanas prasmes
Spēja interpretēt datus un pārvērst tos skaidros secinājumos
Spēja vienkārši izskaidrot sarežģītus datus biznesa lietotājiem
Augsta precizitāte un atbildība darbā ar datiem
Labas komunikācijas un sadarbības prasmes


Par priekšrocību tiks uzskatīta:


Pieredze darbā ar ERP vai finanšu sistēmām
Pieredze ar Power BI, QlikView vai līdzīgiem rīkiem
Pieredze starptautiskā vidē
Izpratne par budžetēšanu un finanšu analīzi


Vēlies saņemt vairāk info?

Sazinies ar mūsu personāla atlases speciālisti.
