Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Department: Finance

Employment Type: Permanent

Location: Burlington, Massachusetts

Compensation: $100,000 - $120,000 / year

Description

At Veriforce, we help companies in high-risk industries keep their people, worksites, and supply chains safe and compliant. As a global leader in contractor and supply chain risk management, our SaaS platform simplifies contractor onboarding, credentialing, and compliance while providing powerful analytics and insights. Beyond software, we offer expert support, training, and consulting so our clients can operate with confidence. Together, we’re making workplaces safer every day.

As our business scales and our product portfolio becomes more sophisticated, we are looking for a detail-obsessed Data Analyst to ensure our internal data is complete, accurate, and trustworthy. This is a great opportunity for someone with a sharp analytical mindset who’s eager to dive into complex datasets and partner closely with business leaders in a growing SaaS/tech company backed by Apax Partners.

What That Means Day-to-day

The data analyst will play a key role in supporting data-driven decision-making across the organization by analyzing business performance, contractor behavior, and data integration.

This role is responsible for collecting and analyzing data from multiple systems, including CRM platforms, Billing Systems (Zuora), financial Systems (NetSuite), and product analytics tools to generate insights that drive growth, operational efficiency, and improved contractor outcomes. The data analyst will monitor and analyze core metrics to help leadership understand business performance and identify opportunities for improvement.


Build and maintain dashboards and reports for leadership using tools such as Power BI and Alteryx
Help automate the current manual reporting
Deepen analysis of contractor data to identify key drivers for churn or expansion
Support forecasting and budgeting processes with data insights
Analyze key business metrics to provide deeper insights into the business
Work with teams to ensure data integrity and consistency
Translate complex data into clear insights and recommendations


What You’ll Need To Be Successful

The ideal candidate brings strong data fundamentals, thrives in ambiguity, and has the technical horsepower to work with complex datasets while maintaining business acumen to influence senior leaders. This person is a highly detail-oriented self-starter who can balance precision and speed in a fast-paced environment, manage competing priorities independently, and collaborate effectively with others through strong interpersonal skills.


5+ years of data analytics experience with demonstrated expertise in SQL, Power Query or Alteryx, PowerBI and Excel skills, preferably in high-growth or PE-backed companies
Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, Statistics or related field
Comfort working with data from multiple systems and building integrated reporting solutions
Exceptional analytical abilities with proven problem-solving skills and intellectual curiosity
Ability to translate complex data concepts for non-technical stakeholders with clarity and impact
Strong business acumen with the ability to connect financial metrics to operational drivers


Preferred Skills


Experience with financial systems (NetSuite or similar ERP platforms)
SaaS or subscription-based business model experience
International/multi-entity company experience
M&A or integration experience


What You’ll Get In Return

Here are just a few of the great reasons you should join our team!


Work with a global team! We have colleagues and customers across North America and overseas.
Veriforce is a great place to work! Our leaders and teams cite culture as one of the top reasons this is a great place to build your career.
Veriforce provides
100% paid employee medical and dental insurance
Monthly contributions to Health Savings Accounts
A 401(k) match that is immediately fully vested
Outstanding time off benefits
Paid time off for volunteer activities
Remote work

All job offers will be contingent on successful completion of a drug screen and background check.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking to hire a data analyst to join our data team. You will take responsibility for managing our master data set, developing reports, and troubleshooting data issues. To do well in this role you need a very fine eye for detail, experience as a data analyst, and a deep understanding of the popular data analysis tools and databases. Data Analyst Responsibilities: Managing master data, including creation, updates, and deletion. Managing users and user roles. Provide quality assurance of imported data, working with quality assurance analysts if necessary. Commissioning and decommissioning of data sets. Processing confidential data and information according to guidelines. Helping develop reports and analyses. Managing and designing the reporting environment, including data sources, security, and metadata. Supporting the data warehouse in identifying and revising reporting requirements. Supporting initiatives for data integrity and normalization. Assessing tests and implementing new or upgraded software and assisting with strategic decisions on new systems. Generating reports from single or multiple systems. Troubleshooting the reporting database environment and reports. Evaluating changes and updates to source production systems. Training end-users on new reports and dashboards. Providing technical expertise in data storage structures, data mining, and data cleansing. Data Analyst Requirements: Bachelor s degree from an accredited university or college in computer science. Work experience as a data analyst or in a related field. Ability to work with stakeholders to assess potential risks. Ability to analyze existing tools and databases and provide software solution recommendations. Ability to translate business requirements into nontechnical, lay terms. High-level experience in methodologies and processes for managing large-scale databases. Demonstrated experience in handling large data sets and relational databases. Understanding of addressing and metadata standards. High-level written and verbal communication skills.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Boston VA Research Institute, Inc. (BVARI)




We are a Boston-based epidemiology and biomedical research organization. We are actively engaged in several clinical trials, pharmacoepidemiology, chronic disease epidemiology, genetic epidemiology, biostatistical and genomics research. Our research team oversees study data-related activities and quality improvement initiatives to improve the way translational research is conducted and clinical care is delivered. The multi-disciplinary center consists of a highly talented group of biomedical researchers, clinicians, epidemiologists, biostatisticians, data analysts/programmers, project coordinators, and informatics team with academic affiliations to Harvard Medical School and Boston University. We are looking for candidates with a service-oriented mentality, high sense of ownership of assigned tasks and the ability to solve complex data-related problems.




Project Background:

This role is involved in managing data aspects of large nationwide randomized clinical trials as well as integrating electronic health record (EHR) data with the Million Veteran Program (MVP) data to build cohorts for clinical and genetic research, enabling robust investigation of complex health outcomes in Veterans.




General Statement of Duties:

The Data Analyst will work with the study teams to gather study data requirements, pull all necessary data from electronic health records (EHR), and perform data cleaning, and aggregation/synthesis as appropriate. The Data Analyst will a) generate recruitment lists to the enrollment sites of national clinical trials and perform quality assurance on the data generated, b) develop reports as needed for the Principal Investigator and other project staff as needed, c) perform validation of programs and analytical databases to fulfill specifications and ensure integrity of outcomes, d) turn in assignments in a timely manner, meeting appropriate timelines, e) establish and maintain communication with customers to keep them updated on status of their requests.




Essential Duties and Responsibilities:




Mainly responsible for constructing, cleaning, validating, modifying, and analyzing biomedical research data
Query and extract large-scale EHR and MVP data using SQL
Generate reports and visualizations using tools such as R and Python to effectively communicate findings
Collaborate with biostatisticians, clinicians, epidemiologists, data management team and data analysts/programmers to design, create and deliver analytical databases, reports and analyses
Perform validation of programs and analytical databases to fulfill specifications and ensure integrity of outcomes
Establish and maintain communication with customers to keep them updated on status of their requests
Provide statistical expertise towards research projects and conduct statistical analysis using SAS or R
Proactively identify data issues, troubleshoot problems, and implement solutions with minimal supervision
Establish and maintain communication with study team to keep them updated on status of requests
Turn in assignments in a timely manner, meeting appropriate timelines.




QUALIFICATIONS AND ENTRANCE REQUIREMENTS:

Master’s degree in public health domain, statistics, biostatistics or epidemiology, data science, or a related field with 2+ years of statistical programming experience; OR Bachelor’s degree in a related field with 5+ years of programming experience.
Strong programming and analytical skills, with demonstrated proficiency in SQL and at least one statistical programming language (R, Python, or SAS)
Hands-on experience with Microsoft SQL Server, including working with relational datasets, managing and structuring large observational datasets, and writing efficient complex SQL queries for data extraction, creating cohorts, data transformation, and analysis.
Experience in data visualization and reporting using R or Python.
Basic knowledge of study design and research methodology.
Capable of working independently in writing analytic plans, performing analyses and writing reports to facilitate research decision-making.
Demonstrated ability to think algorithmically and approach problems with a strong coding and problem-solving mindset.
Understanding of the healthcare domain, clinical, epidemiologic and/or genomic data, and clinical trials research is a plus.
Natural Language Processing experience is a plus.
Excellent attention to detail, logic, and organizational skills.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.




The employee must be a resident of Massachusetts upon the start date.




Please note we are unable to provide work authorization and/or visa sponsorship.




Work Environment

This job operates in a professional office environment. This role routinely uses standard office equipment such as computers, phones, photocopiers, filing cabinets and fax machines.




Physical Demands

The physical demands described in this job description are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job.




While performing the duties of this job, the employee frequently is required to stand; walk; use hands; and reach with hands and arms.




Selective Service

If you are male application born after December 31, 1959, you must certify that you have registered with the Selective Service System or are exempt from having to do so under the Selective Service Law.




This position requires the ability to occasionally lift office products and supplies, up to 20 pounds.




Position Type/Expected Hours of Work

This is a full-time position, and hours of work and days are Monday through Friday, 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m.




Travel

No travel is required for this position. Optional travel to scientific meetings may occur from time to time.




Work Authorization/Security Clearance

An employee in this position must complete all appropriate background checks at the time of hire and periodic reappointment.




Other Duties

Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities, and activities may change at any time with or without notice.




Equal Opportunity Employer

Boston VA Research Institute provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, transfer, leaves of absences, compensation, and training.




Pay Range

Minimum $70,000.00, Midpoint $80,000, Maximum $90,000.00

Salary is based on related experience, expertise, and internal equity; generally, new hires can expect pay between the minimum and midpoint of the range.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us

RTK Tickets is a dynamic and rapidly expanding company at the intersection of entertainment, technology, and trading. From buzzer-beaters and sold-out stadiums to front-row concerts, we provide a comprehensive platform for buying, selling, and optimizing access to live events — and increasingly, other high-demand assets.




We’re a fast-moving, data-driven brokerage redefining how markets are priced, traded, and optimized across entertainment and emerging asset classes. What began as a two-person idea has evolved into a 20-person team built on passion, precision, and performance.




Now, we’re looking for a Data Analyst to help us build and scale the data infrastructure and predictive models that power how we buy and sell tickets. If you’re excited by real-time markets, complex datasets, and turning raw data into measurable outcomes, you could be a great fit for our team.




What You’ll Do

As a Data Analyst at RTK Tickets, you’ll own the systems and models that drive our pricing decisions. You’ll design and maintain data pipelines that ingest market data from dozens of sources, build predictive models that forecast demand, and work closely with our risk and operations teams to turn insights into actions.

You’ll have end-to-end ownership; from raw data ingestion to production-ready models. Your work will directly influence how we price, buy, and sell across thousands of live events.







Your Day-to-Day

Design, build, and maintain data pipelines that ingest and process transaction and listing data from external marketplaces
Develop and refine predictive models that forecast demand, identify pricing inefficiencies, and optimize offers
Implement data quality frameworks and monitoring systems to ensure fresh, accurate, and reliable data
Build and manage data warehouse pipelines and modeling (ETL/ELT) for large-scale datasets
Collaborate with the risk and operations teams to translate model outputs into actionable decisions
Partner with engineers to integrate pipelines with CI/CD processes and automate data workflows
Present your findings clearly to both technical and non-technical teammates







What You Bring

Required:

1-3 years of experience in data science, data engineering, or a closely related analytical role
Strong Proficiency in Python - including experience with data processing libraries
Solid SQL skills for data analysis, transformation, and pipeline work
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Applied Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, startup-like environment where you take ownership and adapt quickly
Clear communication skills — able to explain complex ideas simply and confidently
Excitement about AI, automation, and cutting-edge technology

Nice to Have:

Exposure to machine learning model deployment or MLOps practices
Interest in live event markets, trading markets, sports analytics, or alternative asset pricing
Experience with LLM-based automation tools for data extraction or enrichment







Why You’ll Love It Here

At RTK Tickets, you’ll gain hands-on experience at the intersection of data, product, and live entertainment. Your pipelines and models will directly influence how we price and trade across thousands of live events every week.

If you want to grow your career at the intersection of data, markets, and live entertainment, we’d love to hear from you.







Interview Process

Our interview process includes several stages designed to assess both technical skills and team fit. Candidates should be prepared to meet with the hiring team for introductory conversations, followed by a Python-based take-home exercise focused on data ingestion, pipeline design, and predictive modeling - mirroring the kind of end-to-end work this role owns. Candidates will walk through their solution with the team before final interviews with the hiring manager and senior leadership.




Compensation & Benefits

Competitive salary with opportunity for performance-based bonuses
Health and Dental insurance
401(k) retirement plan with company matching
Tickets to select live events

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Worldly Partners: Worldly Partners studies and invests in businesses that we believe will compound capital over decades. Our detailed work focuses on understanding the characteristics of companies that produce very long-term returns and the people who build them.




Job Overview: This role involves working closely with the founder of Worldly Partners to transform complex datasets into actionable insights with the goal of driving very long-term performance and risk management.




Key Responsibilities:

Analyze large, complex financial datasets (market data, alternative data, fundamentals) to identify trends, anomalies, and investment opportunities
Collaborate with investment teams to define data needs and translate them into analytical solutions
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to uncover predictive signals and inefficiencies in markets
Build and maintain data pipelines for sourcing, cleaning, and validating structured and unstructured data
Monitor data quality and integrity across multiple sources
Develop dashboards, reports, and visualizations of data for portfolio monitoring
Automate recurring data processes to improve efficiency and scalability
Stay current with new data sources, tools, and technologies relevant to financial markets
In person data collection




Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or related field
2 – 10+ years of experience in data analysis, preferably in finance, asset management, or trading environments
Experience with data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, or similar)
Strong programming skills in Python or SQL, with Java experience preferred
Familiarity with machine learning techniques and model validation practices
Solid understanding of financial markets, instruments, and investment concepts
Experience working with time-series data and large datasets
Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail
Willingness and ability to travel up to 40%




Working Hours:

40 hours per week on site in Needham, MA
Occasional evening or weekend hours may be required depending on business needs
Travel required




Compensation:

$185,000-$225,000
Medical, dental, and vision insurance 100% employer-paid

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
Ascensus is the leading independent technology and service platform powering savings plans across America, providing products and expertise that help nearly 16 million people save for a better today and tomorrow.

Are you a student looking to make extra money this summer while gaining valuable work experience? Ascensus is the leading independent technology and service platform powering savings plans across America, providing products and expertise that help nearly 16 million people save for a better today and tomorrow. Our internship program offers practical corporate work experience that could lead to full-time work after graduation. We are seeking students going into their senior year, graduating in the spring of 2027.

The shift is from 9 am to 5 pm, 40 hours per week, starting in June and ending in August. This is a hybrid position in Newton, MA that pays $20 per hour.

The Data Analyst Intern will support the Retirement Operations team by helping transform data into meaningful insights that inform business decisions. This internship is designed to provide hands‑on experience working with real‑world datasets, analytics tools, and business partners while building foundational skills in data analysis, reporting, and data storytelling. The intern will work closely with experienced analysts and stakeholders and receive mentorship throughout the program.

Key Responsibilities


Assist in collecting, cleaning, and validating data from internal and external sources to ensure data quality and accuracy
Support analysis of datasets to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for operational or business improvement
Help build and maintain dashboards, reports, and visualizations used by leadership and cross‑functional teams
Partner with analysts and business stakeholders to understand data needs and help define metrics and KPIs
Contribute to the development and documentation of data models, data dictionaries, and standard reporting definitions
Perform ad hoc analyses to support business questions and ongoing initiatives
Assist with tracking performance metrics and supporting regular reporting cycles
Collaborate with data engineering, IT, and analytics teams to learn how data pipelines and infrastructure are managed
Follow established data governance, privacy, and security standards


Skills & Qualifications

Required:


Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related field (or recent graduate)
Strong interest in data analysis, analytics, or business intelligence
Exposure to SQL or relational databases through coursework, projects, or internships
Familiarity with Excel (basic to intermediate formulas, pivot tables, data analysis concepts)
Ability to analyze data, think critically, and solve problems
Strong attention to detail and willingness to learn
Ability to communicate findings clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences


Preferred:


Exposure to data visualization tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or Alteryx
Coursework or project experience using Python or R for data analysis
Familiarity with basic data modeling or data warehousing concepts
Interest in financial services, retirement plans, or a highly metrics‑driven industry
Experience working on academic, personal, or team data projects


Core Competencies


Analytical and quantitative thinking
Curiosity and eagerness to learn
Data visualization and storytelling fundamentals
Collaboration and communication skills
Time management and ability to balance multiple priorities
Growth mindset and openness to feedback

Ascensus is the leading independent technology and service platform powering savings plans across America, providing products and expertise that help nearly 16 million people save for a better today and tomorrow.

Are you a student looking to make extra money this summer while gaining valuable work experience? Ascensus is the leading independent technology and service platform powering savings plans across America, providing products and expertise that help nearly 16 million people save for a better today and tomorrow. Our internship program offers practical corporate work experience that could lead to full-time work after graduation. We are seeking students going into their senior year, graduating in the spring of 2027.

The shift is from 9 am to 5 pm, 40 hours per week, starting in June and ending in August. This is a hybrid position in Newton, MA that pays $20 per hour.

The Data Analyst Intern will support the Retirement Operations team by helping transform data into meaningful insights that inform business decisions. This internship is designed to provide hands‑on experience working with real‑world datasets, analytics tools, and business partners while building foundational skills in data analysis, reporting, and data storytelling. The intern will work closely with experienced analysts and stakeholders and receive mentorship throughout the program.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist in collecting, cleaning, and validating data from internal and external sources to ensure data quality and accuracy
  • Support analysis of datasets to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for operational or business improvement
  • Help build and maintain dashboards, reports, and visualizations used by leadership and cross‑functional teams
  • Partner with analysts and business stakeholders to understand data needs and help define metrics and KPIs
  • Contribute to the development and documentation of data models, data dictionaries, and standard reporting definitions
  • Perform ad hoc analyses to support business questions and ongoing initiatives
  • Assist with tracking performance metrics and supporting regular reporting cycles
  • Collaborate with data engineering, IT, and analytics teams to learn how data pipelines and infrastructure are managed
  • Follow established data governance, privacy, and security standards

Skills & Qualifications

Required:

  • Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related field (or recent graduate)
  • Strong interest in data analysis, analytics, or business intelligence
  • Exposure to SQL or relational databases through coursework, projects, or internships
  • Familiarity with Excel (basic to intermediate formulas, pivot tables, data analysis concepts)
  • Ability to analyze data, think critically, and solve problems
  • Strong attention to detail and willingness to learn
  • Ability to communicate findings clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences

Preferred:

  • Exposure to data visualization tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or Alteryx
  • Coursework or project experience using Python or R for data analysis
  • Familiarity with basic data modeling or data warehousing concepts
  • Interest in financial services, retirement plans, or a highly metrics‑driven industry
  • Experience working on academic, personal, or team data projects

Core Competencies

  • Analytical and quantitative thinking
  • Curiosity and eagerness to learn
  • Data visualization and storytelling fundamentals
  • Collaboration and communication skills
  • Time management and ability to balance multiple priorities
  • Growth mindset and openness to feedback

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking to hire a data analyst to join our data team. You will take responsibility for managing our master data set, developing reports, and troubleshooting data issues. To do well in this role you need a very fine eye for detail, experience as a data analyst, and a deep understanding of the popular data analysis tools and databases. Data Analyst Responsibilities: Managing master data, including creation, updates, and deletion. Managing users and user roles. Provide quality assurance of imported data, working with quality assurance analysts if necessary. Commissioning and decommissioning of data sets. Processing confidential data and information according to guidelines. Helping develop reports and analyses. Managing and designing the reporting environment, including data sources, security, and metadata. Supporting the data warehouse in identifying and revising reporting requirements. Supporting initiatives for data integrity and normalization. Assessing tests and implementing new or upgraded software and assisting with strategic decisions on new systems. Generating reports from single or multiple systems. Troubleshooting the reporting database environment and reports. Evaluating changes and updates to source production systems. Training end-users on new reports and dashboards. Providing technical expertise in data storage structures, data mining, and data cleansing. Data Analyst Requirements: Bachelor s degree from an accredited university or college in computer science. Work experience as a data analyst or in a related field. Ability to work with stakeholders to assess potential risks. Ability to analyze existing tools and databases and provide software solution recommendations. Ability to translate business requirements into nontechnical, lay terms. High-level experience in methodologies and processes for managing large-scale databases. Demonstrated experience in handling large data sets and relational databases. Understanding of addressing and metadata standards. High-level written and verbal communication skills.

Title: Lead Data Analyst
Description:
We’re partnered with a fast-scaling healthcare technology company building modern infrastructure that improves healthcare delivery and patient outcomes at scale.




They’re now looking for a Senior Healthcare Data Analyst to help customers unlock the full value of healthcare data while shaping how trusted, high-quality data products are delivered across the platform.




This role sits at the intersection of healthcare analytics, data quality, customer partnership, and modern AI-enabled operations, helping customers understand, trust, and operationalize complex clinical and claims data at scale.




What's on offer




Total cash comp up to $185k
Remote-first environment with strong flexibility
Opportunity to influence how healthcare data is interpreted and trusted at scale
High-impact role within a mission-driven healthcare technology business
Collaborative culture investing in AI adoption and modern ways of working
Exposure to senior leadership and strategic customer initiatives




The Mandate

Partner directly with customers to solve complex healthcare data and platform challenges
Build trust in the platform by explaining data transformations, logic, and quality outcomes
Collaborate with technical stakeholders to develop advanced customisations and solutions
Contribute to the evolution of a best-in-class validation framework across healthcare datasets
Act as an internal subject matter expert across claims, clinical, and operational healthcare data flows
Drive improvements in data reliability, quality, and customer confidence
Apply AI tooling to improve efficiency, organization, and delivery across multiple initiatives
Translate technical complexity into clear business and operational outcomes




What We’re Looking For

5+ years of experience working with healthcare data environments
Strong knowledge of clinical and claims data models and healthcare data ecosystems
Deep understanding of healthcare data standards and feeds including FHIR, HL7, CCD, and full extracts
Familiarity with healthcare terminology including ICD, SNOMED, LOINC, RxNorm, and related frameworks
Experience working with value-based care models and healthcare performance measurement
Strong analytical thinking and structured problem-solving ability
Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts clearly to varied audiences
Experience managing multiple priorities while leveraging AI tools to improve productivity and execution
Genuine interest in adopting AI-enabled workflows and operational practices




Bonus

Advanced SQL capability with experience working across complex datasets and environments
Experience working within health systems, ACOs, CINs, payer organizations, or insurers
Experience working within healthcare technology vendors
Exposure to healthcare data validation, governance, or platform operations environments

Title: Lead Data Analyst
Description:
Do you want to be a part of the clean energy movement? Are you passionate about improving our environment for this generation and those to follow? Are you ready to take on new challenges and collaborate with a future-focused team leading the way into new markets? Join Nexamp! 

This is where you can learn from industry leaders and become one yourself. It's fast-paced, mission-based work that challenges the status quo. Be on the team that's changing the world. 

What we’re looking for: 

We're looking for a Lead Data Analyst to craft insightful and compelling data stories, empower business owners across Nexamp to make more effective data-driven decisions, and help build our self-service data platform. As one of the nation’s largest and longest tenured solar and storage owner operators – and one of the few that is fully vertically integrated – our data is a differentiator in the market and a catalyst for future growth. You’ll be solving uniquely challenging data problems, alongside a strong team, and for a company in which the business model and mission are inseparably intertwined: our solar farms save customers money, help the planet, and are profitable for Nexamp and its investors. 

You will be hybrid out of our Boston office. You will report to the Director, Product Management - Data.

What you’ll do:  

Independently gather requirements for, prioritize, deliver, maintain, and continuously improve reusable data models, dashboards, analyses, A/B tests, and other data assets. Act as the product manager and implementer for your domain’s data offerings. Implement work using advanced SQL and BI tool skills.
Become the subject matter expert for data in your business areas. Identify opportunities for impact, proactively surface insights, support decision-making, and contribute to and influence business strategy and initiatives. Serve as your business stakeholders’ right hand. Support other business areas opportunistically.
Create and maintain comprehensive data documentation and communicate updates to your stakeholders. Write user stories or tickets for data engineering, validate data mapping, conduct QA, and implement data quality monitoring.
Monitor the usage of data assets, increase data literacy, and promote self-service through BI tools among stakeholders. Provide training, guidance, and support as needed.
Work within a centralized data team using agile methodologies. Collaborate with peer analysts through overlapping work, priority shifting, and code reviews to broaden your Nexamp knowledge.
Partner with the data architect, engineers, scientists, and analysts to shape the vision and roadmap for Nexamp’s data platform. Define and track annual OKRs to measure your impact. 

What you’ll bring:

7+ years of experience as a data analyst serving business owners and collaborating with data engineers (experience in the energy/construction industry is a plus, but not required)
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field would be preferred; advanced degree is a plus.
Advanced in SQL and BI tool administration and usage (experience with Databricks and Sigma are a plus, but not required)
Strong track record of independently solving problems.
Commitment to Nexamp's mission and have a passion for solving tomorrow's climate crisis today.
Demonstrated experience in effectively communicating information, ideas, and perspectives with people inside and beyond your organization.   
Experience in showcasing initiative to make improvements to current work, processes, products, and services across the organization. We value accountability and an ownership mentality. 
Ability to ask appropriate questions, analyze data, identify the root causes of problems, and present creative solutions. 
Expertise in building strong internal and external relationships with customers and stakeholders, instilling trust and loyalty across the industry. 
Eagerness to develop a fundamental understanding of how Nexamp operates and then apply that knowledge effectively to inform business decisions.

If you don’t meet 100% of the above qualifications, but see yourself contributing, please apply.

At Nexamp, our mission is to build the future of energy so it is clean, simple, and accessible for all. We are committed to providing a work environment free from discrimination. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate against applicants on the basis of race, ethnicity, religion, sex, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability status, veteran status, or any other basis protected by law. By encouraging a culture where ideas and decisions come from all people, we believe it will help us grow, innovate, and be a part of environmental and social change.

You’ll love working here because:  

Not only will you get to take part in meaningful work and have the chance to change the world alongside innovative, dedicated, and motivated peers, but you will also have access to all the benefits that Nexamp offers! This includes our competitive compensation package; a 401(k) employer-match; health, dental, and vision insurance starting day one; flexible paid time off and holiday PTO; commuter benefits, and cell phone reimbursement. We have headquarters in Boston, MA and Chicago, IL, in addition to growing offices nationwide. We provide healthy snacks, coffee, service days and other volunteer opportunities, company outings, and more!  

Compensation

The reasonably estimated salary for this role at Nexamp ranges from $110,000 - $130,000. In addition to base salary, the competitive compensation package may include, depending on the role, participation in an incentive program linked to performance (for example, annual bonus programs based on individual and company performance, non-annual sales incentive plans, or other non-annual incentive plans). Additionally, you may be eligible to participate in the Company’s stock option plan. Actual base salary may vary based upon, but is not limited to, skills and qualifications, internal equity, performance, and geographic location. 

Nexamp's People team manages all aspects of recruitment and hiring within our organization. We want to inform third-party recruiters, staffing firms, and related agencies that Nexamp does not accept unsolicited resumes. Resumes will only be considered from these entities if a signed agreement is in place and the People team explicitly authorizes external recruiting assistance for a specific position. Any unsolicited resumes received will be deemed the property of Nexamp. We want to emphasize that Nexamp is not liable for any fees associated with unsolicited resumes.

Title: Principal Data Governance Analyst 252416
Description:
Principal, Data Governance

Direct hire

Hybrid remote- Wednesdays onsite in Boston, MA

$130k-140k




We are seeking a Principal, Data Governance to join a growing Enterprise Data & Analytics function. This is a highly visible, strategic role for a senior leader who can help stand up and scale a modern data governance program while partnering closely with both technical and business stakeholders.

This role is ideal for someone who combines technical depth (data, SQL, lineage) with strong communication and leadership skills, and is excited to drive adoption of governance practices across an organization.




🔑 Core Responsibilities

You will play a key role in advancing five foundational pillars of the data governance program:

Operating Model

Develop and refine the data governance operating model, including policies, standards, and best practices

Metadata Management

Drive adoption and optimization of enterprise data catalog tools
Ensure seamless integration with modern data platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks)

Data Quality

Establish data quality standards, rules, and monitoring frameworks
Partner with teams to improve trust and reliability of data assets

Data Stewardship

Lead governance forums (e.g., Data Governance Council)
Enable and support data stewards across the organization

Master Data Management (MDM)

Contribute to long-term strategy and roadmap for MDM and reference data




🛠 Key Duties

Act as a “train-the-trainer”, developing job aids, documentation, and training materials to promote data literacy
Partner with stakeholders to understand business needs and translate them into governance requirements
Read and interpret SQL to validate data lineage and metadata accuracy
Manage metadata workflows, approvals, and cataloging processes




✅ Required Qualifications

7+ years of experience in data governance, metadata management, data quality, or data stewardship
Strong experience with Snowflake (required)
Hands-on experience with at least one data governance tool:
Atlan, Collibra, Alation, or data.world
Deep understanding of:
Data lineage, metadata management, and data cataloging
Data quality frameworks and tools (e.g., Informatica IDQ, Talend, DataGaps)
Experience working within modern data ecosystems (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, BI tools)




💡 Key Skills & Attributes

Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills (this is a highly collaborative role)
Ability to influence across technical and non-technical teams
Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset
Experience working in regulated environments (e.g., familiarity with HIPAA, GDPR, CCPA is a plus)




🎓 Education

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Analytics, Business, Engineering, or related field required
Master’s degree preferred




📍 Work Environment

Hybrid schedule (onsite midweek)




🌟 Why Join?

This is a unique opportunity to help shape and scale a data governance program from the ground up, working at the intersection of strategy, technology, and business enablement.

If you’re looking for a role where you can drive real impact and establish best-in-class data practices, we’d love to connect.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Company Description

WiQo® US, Inc. is the U.S. subsidiary of WiQo®, a leading skincare company from Trieste, Italy, known for its innovative, non-invasive approach to aesthetics. Combining PRX Professional™ treatments and WiQo® personalized skincare, the company provides products that promote skin regeneration and rejuvenation. Founded in 1994 by dermatologist Dr. Rossana Castellana, WiQo® has over 20 years of rigorous safety and efficacy testing. Its skincare solutions, such as PRX Derm Perfexion and PRX-PLUS, are trusted for building confidence through healthier skin.




Role Description

Role Overview

We are looking for a curious, data-driven early-career professional to join as our founding Business Intelligence Analyst. This is a “second-job“ opportunity — we expect you to have 1–2 years of real experience and be ready to own data end-to-end across our organization. You will be the single source of truth for data across HubSpot, Shopify, and QuickBooks, and you will build the dashboards, KPIs, and reports that power our finance, sales, education, and operations teams.




Critically, we want someone who actively uses AI agent tools in their workflow. You should be comfortable leveraging tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Notion AI, or similar AI-assisted platforms to automate repetitive analysis, generate insights faster, and build scalable reporting — not someone who is just curious about AI, but someone already doing it.




AI & Technology Proficiency (Required)

•     Strong, hands-on understanding of AI agent tools (e.g., Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Notion AI, or similar)

•     Ability to use AI to automate data cleaning, report generation, and insight summarization

•     Experience building or adapting AI-assisted workflows for business analysis

•     Comfortable evaluating and adopting new AI tools as the landscape evolves

•     Proficiency in HubSpot, Shopify, and QuickBooks (or eager to master all three quickly)

•     Excel / Google Sheets power user; experience with a BI tool (Looker, Power BI, Tableau, or similar)

•     SQL knowledge is a strong plus; Python or no-code automation tools are a bonus




Data Integrity & Systems Management

•     Own data accuracy and consistency across HubSpot, Shopify, and QuickBooks

•     Build and enforce data validation rules, deduplication standards, and field-mapping protocols

•     Monitor data pipelines and catch discrepancies before they reach reports or leadership

•     Create and maintain a shared data dictionary with clear definitions for all metrics




Finance Support

•     Calculate and track COGS across product lines and time periods

•     Build financial forecasting models and scenario planning tools

•     Develop financial KPIs and dashboards for leadership (margin, revenue targets, burn rate)

•     Support monthly and quarterly close with reconciled data exports from all platforms




Sales Support

•     Design and maintain sales KPI dashboards: pipeline, close rate, deal velocity, quota attainment

•     Analyze customer behavior: purchase frequency, lifetime value, churn signals, segment trends

•     Track regional and territory performance vs. goals; surface over- and under-performing areas

•     Deliver weekly data packages and forecast visibility to reps and sales leadership

•     Identify upsell, cross-sell, and retention opportunities from behavioral and transactional data




Education Support

•     Collect and manage class attendance, enrollment, and completion data across all programs

•     Track participant engagement metrics and class capacity utilization

•     Log and report adverse events related to education programs in compliance with applicable standards

•     Maintain a structured Q&A data repository from classes to identify trends and improve content

•     Generate periodic education reports to support program review and leadership decision-making




Reporting & Dashboards

•     Build and maintain self-serve dashboards for each department: finance, sales, education, operations

Company Description

WiQo® US, Inc. is the U.S. subsidiary of WiQo®, a leading skincare company from Trieste, Italy, known for its innovative, non-invasive approach to aesthetics. Combining PRX Professional™ treatments and WiQo® personalized skincare, the company provides products that promote skin regeneration and rejuvenation. Founded in 1994 by dermatologist Dr. Rossana Castellana, WiQo® has over 20 years of rigorous safety and efficacy testing. Its skincare solutions, such as PRX Derm Perfexion and PRX-PLUS, are trusted for building confidence through healthier skin.


Role Description

Role Overview

We are looking for a curious, data-driven early-career professional to join as our founding Business Intelligence Analyst. This is a “second-job“ opportunity — we expect you to have 1–2 years of real experience and be ready to own data end-to-end across our organization. You will be the single source of truth for data across HubSpot, Shopify, and QuickBooks, and you will build the dashboards, KPIs, and reports that power our finance, sales, education, and operations teams.


Critically, we want someone who actively uses AI agent tools in their workflow. You should be comfortable leveraging tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Notion AI, or similar AI-assisted platforms to automate repetitive analysis, generate insights faster, and build scalable reporting — not someone who is just curious about AI, but someone already doing it.


AI & Technology Proficiency (Required)

•     Strong, hands-on understanding of AI agent tools (e.g., Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Notion AI, or similar)

•     Ability to use AI to automate data cleaning, report generation, and insight summarization

•     Experience building or adapting AI-assisted workflows for business analysis

•     Comfortable evaluating and adopting new AI tools as the landscape evolves

•     Proficiency in HubSpot, Shopify, and QuickBooks (or eager to master all three quickly)

•     Excel / Google Sheets power user; experience with a BI tool (Looker, Power BI, Tableau, or similar)

•     SQL knowledge is a strong plus; Python or no-code automation tools are a bonus


Data Integrity & Systems Management

•     Own data accuracy and consistency across HubSpot, Shopify, and QuickBooks

•     Build and enforce data validation rules, deduplication standards, and field-mapping protocols

•     Monitor data pipelines and catch discrepancies before they reach reports or leadership

•     Create and maintain a shared data dictionary with clear definitions for all metrics


Finance Support

•     Calculate and track COGS across product lines and time periods

•     Build financial forecasting models and scenario planning tools

•     Develop financial KPIs and dashboards for leadership (margin, revenue targets, burn rate)

•     Support monthly and quarterly close with reconciled data exports from all platforms


Sales Support

•     Design and maintain sales KPI dashboards: pipeline, close rate, deal velocity, quota attainment

•     Analyze customer behavior: purchase frequency, lifetime value, churn signals, segment trends

•     Track regional and territory performance vs. goals; surface over- and under-performing areas

•     Deliver weekly data packages and forecast visibility to reps and sales leadership

•     Identify upsell, cross-sell, and retention opportunities from behavioral and transactional data


Education Support

•     Collect and manage class attendance, enrollment, and completion data across all programs

•     Track participant engagement metrics and class capacity utilization

•     Log and report adverse events related to education programs in compliance with applicable standards

•     Maintain a structured Q&A data repository from classes to identify trends and improve content

•     Generate periodic education reports to support program review and leadership decision-making

•     Present findings clearly to non-technical stakeholders including the CEO

Join the Titan Data Transformation team supporting Front Office (ART 1) initiatives focused on customer data. This role is responsible for enabling the successful migration and transformation of customer master data into SAP S/4 HANA, ensuring alignment across sales, distribution, and commercial processes.

Key responsibilities include:


Working in an agile environment with business and technical team to identifying data migration requirements and dependencies.
Responsible for understanding and executing data mapping and data migrations with business and functional analysts.
Work with the business on data clean up
Work with data conversion and data load teams to drive end-to-end migration activities.
Assist in the development of data quality and readiness checks and identify solution changes which could impact checks.
Trouble shoot data extraction, conversion, and load errors
Validate accuracy of migrated data to expected outcomes
Assist with the development of lifecycle processes


Additional Responsibilities:


Support migration and governance of customer master data across sales and distribution processes
Partner with commercial and sales teams to define customer data requirements and resolve data gaps
Validate customer hierarchies, account structures, and sales area data for S/4 readiness
Identify and remediate inconsistencies impacting order processing and customer lifecycle data


Primary Skills/Qualifications:


Master data management and governance experience
5+ years SE manufacturing process knowledge or working in a manufacturing environment which applies data governance principles
3+ years of hands-on experience working with data, data tables and ETL processes
3+ years of hands-on experience working in the SAP environment
Excellent at solving problems and working through complex analytics
Candidate is proactive, collaborative, and persistent
Organized, detail oriented and rigorous
Advanced Excel skills
Intermediate SQL proficiency


Travel Requirement:

This role may require travel up to approximately 50–75% based on project phase and deployment needs.

Passport Requirement:

A valid passport is required as this role may support cross-border collaboration and deployment activities

Title: Healthcare Data Analyst (Clinic Administration)
Description:
The Healthcare Data Analyst will join a collaborative team at Joslin Diabetes Center supporting clinical operations, grounded in patient safety and improvement science to participate in projects that promote a culture of excellence.

The Healthcare Data Analyst will be responsible for acquisition, analysis, interpretation, and reporting of sensitive data to support quality and performance measurement and track improvement efforts. They must be able to work independently and collaboratively to locate and validate data, identify data requirements, and communicate data specifications to team members and leaders. They must be able to develop reporting/analytic solutions, but also be able to draw meaningful conclusions and make recommendations for actions.

The Healthcare Data Analyst will leverage software applications to design solutions and perform detailed data mapping and analysis. They will not only manage data and design new processes, but will be accountable for the timely and accurate production of routine monitoring reports and special projects. This requires experience working with databases, establishing data connections and running queries, aggregating and synthesizing data for business plans, trends, and projections, along with creativity, curiosity, and initiative to evolve the tools and reporting systems to meet the priorities of a dynamic healthcare environment. They will need an understanding of healthcare financial and operational processes, data management, as well as manipulation and interpretation of large databases.

This role is expected to have onsite training for the first few months then transition to a hybrid schedule.

As a health care organization, we have the responsibility to do everything in our power to care for and protect our patients, our colleagues and our communities. Beth Israel Lahey Health requires that all staff be vaccinated against influenza (flu) as a condition of employment.


Responsible for acquiring, validating, and querying data from data repositories and then using available tools to create dashboards, reports, and effectively synthesizing the data for the end user.
Works with members across teams to define problems and clarify objectives; determines data needs and assists in designing collection tools and reports. Evaluates data and reports to assess if reporting meets end user requirements.
Develops executive level presentations including key observations of statistical, financial and qualitative information. Provides consulting support in developing strategic recommendations to aid decision making by senior management.
Stays abreast of healthcare trends and market development as context for analyses, and becomes proficient with the health system's decision support and other systems.
Collaborates with Information Systems and enterprise data analytics team(s) to obtain and maintain sources and mapping tables.
Ensures data integrity and data quality, regularly reviews data for accuracy, identifies trends, and communicates changes.
Other duties as assigned.
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Allied Health or related required. Master's degree in Public Health, Finance, Epidemiology, Biostatistics or equivalent preferred.
2-5 years related work experience.
Strong experience in data management and analytic tools and techniques; Strong experience in report writing, analytics, visualizations, dashboard creation; Strong experience demonstrating analytical, financial, qualitative and quantitative skills.
Advanced technical computer skills as required for technical support specific to functional area and related systems.



The pay range listed for this position is the annual base salary range the organization reasonably and in good faith expects to pay for this position at this time. Actual compensation is determined based on several factors, that may include seniority, education, training, relevant experience, relevant certifications, geography of work location, job responsibilities, or other applicable factors permissible by law.

Title: Healthcare Data Analyst or Senior Data Analyst, Analytics Hub
Description:
Overview

With over 50 years of proven success, ECG, ranked as a Best Midsized Firm to Work For 2025 by Consulting Magazine, is the most experienced healthcare consulting firm in the US. Working exclusively in this space, our people consistently demonstrate their ability to solve challenges for providers and achieve better patient outcomes. Across our eight office locations, we’re seeking individuals who will show the courage to find innovative solutions and make a direct impact on the delivery of healthcare services nationwide.

About ECG

ECG is a national consulting firm that is redefining healthcare together with its clients. We provide a broad range of strategic, financial, operational, and technology-enabled consulting services to the full continuum of care, including hospitals, health systems, medical groups, academic medical centers, children’s hospitals, cancer centers, ambulatory surgery centers, investors, and payers/health plans.

Our work focuses on creating practical, tailored solutions that help clients maximize resources and achieve sustainable results. We value collaboration, integrity, and innovation, and are committed to fostering an inclusive and supportive work environment.

Job Details

Join Our Analytics Hub

Our growing Analytics Hub works with our consulting teams to deliver new insights, streamlined analysis, and world-class intelligence to the healthcare organizations we serve as clients. Combined with our consulting teams, you will be helping physician and executive leadership make informed decisions that can alter the course of their enterprises in areas such as physician alignment, mergers and acquisitions, service line planning, provider compensation, financial performance, transforming care models, patient access, managed care portfolio optimization, digital health expansion, and more. ECG uses a Microsoft Azure and Microsoft 365 environment, with Microsoft Power BI as ECG’s current analytics visualization platform. The goal of the position is to further capitalize on the existing ECG infrastructure, as well as implement new features and technologies. The ideal candidate is open to new challenges, exceptional at multitasking, and proficient at implementing new solutions.

Our detailed plans incorporate qualitative findings with data-driven strategic, operational, and financial considerations that enable organizations to pursue realistic change. Our consultants bridge the gap between strategic thinking and operational implementation with sensible action plans and tactical recommendations. This approach allows us to stretch our clients’ thinking while ensuring that initiatives with the broadest and deepest impact are prioritized and implementable. Our wide-ranging engagements often focus on helping our clients:


Position themselves for value-based care delivery.
Align hospitals and physician organizations.
Strengthen financial performance.
Enhance clinical programs.
Develop strategic partnerships and/or mergers.


Your Opportunity with ECG: Data Analyst or Senior Data Analyst

As a data analyst or senior data analyst, you will support the firm’s client delivery and business development efforts, working with consultants and senior leaders to manage large data sets, aggregate internal and external data, develop scalable models and other analyses, and support data visualization and presentation sets. In addition, the analyst will work directly with our internal support teams (IT, business development, and L&D) and associated vendors for data management and IT solutions and will assist with a range of innovative analytic opportunities. Here, no two days or projects are alike, which means you’ll have a lot to learn and plenty of support to help you succeed.

Your Responsibilities May Include


Perform complex analyses on big data, including cleaning, preparing, and interpreting.
Gather and synthesize data from various sources (e.g., national surveys, proprietary research, client interviews, industries, markets).
Preparing analyses related to hospital inpatient databases and provider claims data.
Build flexible, dynamic, and scalable financial and operational models to forecast trends, preparing scenarios for consulting efforts related to delivery and business development (e.g., strategic planning, operational improvement market assessments).
Apply AI and machine learning techniques to enhance data analysis, automate insights, accelerate output development, and improve decision-making.
Enhance existing models and capabilities built using SQL, primarily, with some use of Python or R in certain models or scenarios.
Identify and execute analyses in response to consultant business intelligence inquiries.
Build relational databases using a Microsoft SQL Server or Fabric.
Communicate market insights to pursuit teams, and support business development efforts.
Configure, maintain, and build flexible reports, queries, and visuals within the Power BI environment and the production and development environments of related databases.
Research, test, and implement solutions in Power BI.
Partner with various consulting units (Strategy & Enterprise Growth, Payer Strategy & Contracting, Health System Performance, Medical Group Optimization) to gather requirements related to data analytics.
Develop, configure, and maintain interfaces to exchange data among Power BI, SQL databases, and other cloud-based applications.
Create and maintain analytical support documentation and operating procedures.
Develop new and innovative methodologies and approaches.
Contribute to internal education and learning opportunities for consultants and operations members for the Analytics Hub and analytics in general, and support education about ECG’s use cases related to analytics in work.
Assist the VP of Data Analytics and AI in developing analytics and data strategy to support data analytics capabilities at the firm.


Qualifications


Strong knowledge of and passion for the healthcare industry, AI, machine learning, and analytics
A bachelor’s degree in either computer science, computer engineering, data analytics, mathematics, or statistics
Prior work experience in healthcare data analytics using SQL and Power BI required (five+ years of experience for senior analyst level; one to three years for analyst)
Prior work experience in healthcare consulting preferred
Experience using Python, R, Fabric, and Tableau a plus
Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
Comfort with manipulating and synthesizing large data sets
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Excellent organizational skills


Job Locations


Boston, Chicago, or Washington, DC
Hybrid work schedule, with a minimum of two days per week in office


Schedule

Full time/exempt

What You Can Expect Of Us

To reward our driven, innovative, and passionate employees, we’ve built a company culture that’s centered on performance. We offer an attractive compensation package, challenging work, and an entrepreneurial environment where you can take ownership of your career—and get out as much as you put in.

The estimated base salary range for this job is $70,000 – $130,000 annually. It represents a good faith estimate of the range that ECG reasonably expects to pay at the time of the job posting. The actual salary paid will vary based on multiple factors, including but not limited to years of experience, special skills, and market changes. This job is eligible to participate in ECG’s annual incentive compensation program, which reflects ECG’s pay-for-performance philosophy. The job is also eligible to participate in ECG’s benefit plans, which include medical, dental, and vision coverage; a 401(k) matching program; unlimited PTO; and other wellness programs.

Apply now and make an impact for years to come.

To begin the recruitment process, please submit your resume via our career site at https://careers.ecgmc.com.

ECG provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to sex, race, color, religion, national origin, citizenship, ancestry, age, disability, pregnancy, medical condition (cancer and genetic characteristics), genetic information, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, marital status, military or veteran status, or any other legally protected characteristic. We participate in E-Verify as part of our onboarding process. Having the permanent legal right to work in the United States is a condition of employment. ECG is not currently able to provide assistance to candidates requiring sponsorship or a visa.

Title: Analyst, Growth Analytics
Description:
Position Overview

Join our Business Analytics team, where data meets creativity to drive smarter media decisions. As the Analyst, you’ll collaborate with internal teams, external partners, and data providers to transform insights into action — actively contributing to optimizing media investments and shaping strategy across digital and traditional channels.

In this role, you’ll dive deep into the evolving world of digital and media ecosystems, gaining experience with broad range of analytics:

Data tracking challenges with cookies and SDKs
Website tagging and measurement strategies
The impact of walled gardens on analytics
TV and out-of-home (OOH) attribution models




You’ll gain hands-on experience with cutting-edge analytics tools and methodologies, helping to build a foundation in media measurement, attribution, and optimization. This opportunity is designed to launch your career in media analytics, connecting you with a brand that’s woven into America’s DNA.




Why This Role Stands Out




This hybrid position blends media analytics with web analytics, offering exposure to multiple specialties across the analytics landscape. You’ll have access to mentorship, professional development, and real-world projects that accelerate your growth. If you’re curious, analytical, and eager to learn, this role opens doors to a dynamic career in data-driven marketing.




Responsibilities

Develop an understanding of client’s industry, business objectives and strategy, media plans, and consumer journey to provide context for analysis and reporting needs
Data Extraction, Transformation & Loading (ETL), Quality & Hygiene
Coordinate with technical teams to ensure accurate and comprehensive web analytics implementation and tracking across web properties.
Provide insight into site visitor behaviour and engagement to identify optimization opportunities across websites to help inform site design changes.
Apply independent critical thinking to analyse vast data sets to uncover media insights (what’s working, what’s not working) to drive strategic decisions.
Participate in brainstorming sessions with communication planners, creative assets, media planners, buyers, clients and other partner agencies
Contribute to the department’s overall capabilities and understanding of media and communications channels
Work with Director and senior analysts to develop report content (Word, Excel, Tableau, Looker, PowerPoint) in support of ongoing deliverables, as well as ad hoc reporting




Required Skills and Experience

Must possess strong interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills
Highly motivated and collaborative team player with strong interpersonal skills
Expertise in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint and Word
Experience working with large datasets to uncover insights that are essential to business optimizations is a plus




Desired Skills and Experience

1+ years of experience in a professional services or corporate environment
Experience using data visualization tools, such as: Excel 365, Tableau or Snowflake preferred
Experience with ad-serving platforms such as Google Campaign Manager is a plus
Experience with web analytics tools such as Google Analytics and/or Adobe Analytics, and/or tag management tools such as DTM/Launch, GTM and Tealium also a plus




Wage and Benefits

We offer a Total Rewards package that includes medical and dental coverage, 401(k) plans, flex spending, life insurance, disability, employee discount program, employee stock purchase program and paid family benefits to support you and your family.

The salary range for this position is posted below. Where an employee or prospective employee is paid within this range will depend on, among other factors, actual ranges for current/former employees in the subject position, market considerations, budgetary considerations, tenure and standing with the Company (applicable to current employees), as well as the employee’s/applicant’s skill set, level of experience, and qualifications.




Employment Transparency

It is our policy to provide equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, ethnicity, gender, age, religion, creed, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, citizenship, genetic information, veteran status, disability, or any other basis prohibited by applicable federal, state, or local law.

Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties, or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities, and activities may change at any time with or without notice.

The employer will make reasonable accommodations in compliance with the American with Disabilities Act of 1990. The job description will be reviewed periodically as duties and responsibilities change with business necessity. Essential and other job functions are subject to modification. Reasonable accommodations may be provided to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

For applicants to jobs in the United States: In compliance with the current Americans with Disabilities Act and state and local laws, if you have a disability and would like to request an accommodation to apply for a position, please email accommodation@mbww.com.

Title: Data Analyst – Front Office - Material Data
Description:
For this U.S. based position, the expected compensation range is $101,600 - $152,400 per year, which includes base pay and short-term incentive.

The compensation range for this full-time position applies to candidates located within the United States. Our salary ranges are determined by reviewing roles of similar responsibility and level. Within the salary range, individual pay is determined by several factors including performance, knowledge, job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Schneider Electric also offers a comprehensive benefits package to support our employees, inclusive of medical (with member reward points), dental, vision, and basic life insurance, Benefit Bucks (credits to apply towards your benefits) flexible work arrangements, paid family leaves, 401(k) + match, well-being and recognition (including service anniversary) programs, 12 holidays per year,15 days of paid time off per year (pro-rated in the first year of employment based on start date), opportunity to purchase company stock (eligibility depends on start date), and military leave benefits.

You must submit an online application to be considered for the position. The Company will accept applications on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.

If you believe this job posting is not compliant with applicable state pay transparency laws in the U.S., please notify the Company as soon as possible upon discovery by completing this form Job Posting Compliance Form.

Join the Titan Data Transformation team supporting Front Office (ART 1) initiatives focused on material data. This role is responsible for enabling the successful migration and transformation of customer master data into SAP S/4 HANA, ensuring alignment across sales, distribution, and commercial processes.

Key responsibilities include:


Working in an agile environment with business and technical team to identifying data migration requirements and dependencies.
Responsible for understanding and executing data mapping and data migrations with business and functional analysts.
Work with the business on data clean up
Work with data conversion and data load teams to drive end-to-end migration activities.
Assist in the development of data quality and readiness checks and identify solution changes which could impact checks.
Trouble shoot data extraction, conversion, and load errors
Validate accuracy of migrated data to expected outcomes
Assist with the development of lifecycle processes


Additional Responsibilities:


Support migration and governance of customer master data across sales and distribution processes
Partner with commercial and sales teams to define customer data requirements and resolve data gaps
Validate customer hierarchies, account structures, and sales area data for S/4 readiness
Identify and remediate inconsistencies impacting order processing and customer lifecycle data


Primary Skills/Qualifications:


Master data management and governance experience
5+ years SE manufacturing process knowledge or working in a manufacturing environment which applies data governance principles
3+ years of hands-on experience working with data, data tables and ETL processes
3+ years of hands-on experience working in the SAP environment
Excellent at solving problems and working through complex analytics
Candidate is proactive, collaborative, and persistent
Organized, detail oriented and rigorous
Advanced Excel skills
Intermediate SQL proficiency


Travel Requirement:

This role may require travel up to approximately 50–75% based on project phase and deployment needs.

Passport Requirement:

A valid passport is required as this role may support cross-border collaboration and deployment activities.

Title: HR Data Analyst, Talent Analytics
Description:
As a global medtech company, we are driven by our Vision of changing the trajectory of lives for a new day and our Mission to create ingenious solutions that ignite patient turnarounds. Our relentless commitment to patients and strong legacy of innovation in healthcare are the foundation of our future. If you're looking for a new chance, a new beginning, a new trajectory, LivaNova is where your talent can truly thrive. Join our talented team members worldwide to become a pioneer of tomorrow—because at LivaNova, we don’t just treat conditions — we aspire to alter the course of lives.

Position Summary

The HR Data Analyst, Talent Analytics will carry out overall strategy for employee data at LivaNova to support the need for data-driven decision-making regarding workforce productivity, engagement and better returns from recruitment and learning investments in the company.

Key Responsibilities


Compile, structure, and analyze data from HRIS, payroll and Finance systems to develop insights and conclusions that help streamline HR processes throughout the company.
Work with the full data analysis pipeline including data cleaning, process documentation, and communicating results via report writing and visualization.
Participate actively in HR Business Intelligence including project intake, visualization tools, quality assurance, and storytelling. Interpret employee data to identify significant differences, relationships, and trends in data, as well as factors that could affect the results of research.
Identify statistical analysis techniques required to deliver insights.
Advance the use of complex analytical techniques and statistical thinking.
Design and implement data models using statistical principles (e.g., regression, correlation) and develop predictive models for attrition, performance, and recruiting demand.
Produce concise, story-driven presentations to communicate analytic insights.
Collect and share information on government labor statistics and external benchmarks.
Continually analyze competitor practices to make appropriate recommendations to upper management.
Partner with HR leaders to design research plans, develop hypotheses, collect data, and run various statistical models.
Help recruiters by providing relevant data, statistics, trends, and patterns to make the recruiting process more efficient. Similar for talent development, total rewards and employee experience (e.g. retention) practices.
Collaborate with subject matter experts across the HR service areas (e.g. Talent Development, Total Rewards and Talent Acquisition), as well as HR leadership, to promote data governance and stewardship, and to improve overall strategic and operational performance and insight.
Work with the HR business partner for organizational development (e.g. organization levels, structure, teams, span of control) and partner with colleagues and customers to achieve meaningful discussions regarding data outputs and feedback on usage activities to improve HR output.
Educate the HR team on analytical findings, general data and data management best practices.
Interact with professional and community groups related to analytics and business intelligence and maintain strong internal and external networks of peers to stay always ahead of the curve.


Essential Knowledge & Experience


Master’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Business Analytics or closely related field
1 year of experience as a Human Resources Analyst or HR Data Analyst
The Master’s degree program must have included courses in Data Mining, Data-Driven Quality Management, Analytical Decision Modeling and provided technical hands-on experience with statistics, data analytics, data visualization (QLIK, Tableau or ggplot2), business intelligence, reporting and software experience with R or Python, a range of data types of various structures and sizes, and an understanding of the differences and use cases for interpretable vs. black box models.


Pay Transparency: A reasonable estimate of the annual base salary for this position is $90,000 - $106,000 + discretionary annual bonus. Pay ranges may vary by location.

Employee Benefits Include


Health benefits – Medical, Dental, Vision
Personal and Vacation Time
Retirement & Savings Plan (401K)
Employee Stock Purchase Plan
Training & Education Assistance
Bonus Referral Program
Service Awards
Employee Recognition Program
Flexible Work Schedules


Welcome to impact. Welcome to innovation. Welcome to your new life.
