Title: Lead Data Analyst (Network)
Description:
About Agero:Wherever drivers go, we’re leading the way. Agero’s mission is to rethink the vehicle ownership experience through a powerful combination of passionate people and data-driven technology, strengthening our clients’ relationships with their customers. As the #1 B2B, white-label provider of digital driver assistance services, we’re pushing the industry in a new direction, taking manual processes, and redefining them as digital, transparent, and connected. This includes: an industry-leading dispatch management platform powered by Swoop; comprehensive accident management services; knowledgeable consumer affairs and connected vehicle capabilities; and a growing marketplace of services, discounts and support enabled by a robust partner ecosystem. The company has over 150 million vehicle coverage points in partnership with leading automobile manufacturers, insurance carriers and many others. Managing one of the largest national networks of service providers, Agero responds to approximately 12 million service events annually. Agero, a member company of The Cross Country Group, is headquartered in Medford, Mass., with operations throughout North America. To learn more, visit https://www.agero.com/.

Note: For our technical positions, we love to get you started in person! You may be required to travel to Medford for your initial onboarding. Don't worry about the logistics - once you're hired, we handle all travel arrangements and expenses for you.

Position Summary

The Lead Data Analyst (Network) is at the forefront of data-driven initiatives for the group, acting as a linchpin between raw data and actionable insights. This position dissects complex datasets, identifies  patterns, and leverages those insights to drive business growth. Collaborates closely with various internal and external stakeholders, helping them understand the data and how this information can shape future strategies.

Responsibilities:

Advanced Analytics: Develop and deploy advanced analytical models to improve our understanding of Network supply and demand, identify operational inefficiencies, and deliver actionable recommendations to leadership
Performance Monitoring: Implement real-time dashboards and reporting to track and assess Network performance against established metrics
Network Strategy Innovation: Challenge the status quo and be willing to propose and test new strategies for increasing competition and engagement within our service provider network
Stakeholder Feedback Analysis: Analyze feedback, ratings, and reviews from multiple sources and stakeholders to gain insights into service quality, customer satisfaction, and provider satisfaction, identifying actionable insights for improvement
Service Provider Insights: Develop operational and performance data to generate actionable insights and recommendations for 3rd party service providers, enabling them to enhance their service delivery, optimize processes, and align more closely with organizational objectives
Capacity Analysis and Modeling: Analyze the capacity of our Network, identifying gaps, weaknesses, and opportunities to improve operational efficiency and service quality, making recommendations for Network recruiting, retention, and optimization
Testing: Leads test and pilots for process changes as a a result of analysis and communicates the results and recommendations to all areas of management
Subject matter expert: Serves as an internal data analytics expert by ensuring consistency in data analysis methodologies and sharing of best practices

Experience and Skills:

BA/BS Degree or equivalent experience. Degree that emphasizes data analysis and communication (e.g., computer science, social sciences, physical sciences, math, engineering, or statistics) preferred
5+ years of business and data analysis experience
Excellent communication and presentation abilities
Strong problem-solving skills and business acumen
Experience with data management, specifically with validating and auditing data and reports from multiple systems
Demonstrated project and task management capability
Past experience with BI, Google Workspace, and CRM Tools
Proficiency in SQL and working with relational databases is required
An analytical professional with attention to detail and high standard of performance excellence

Hiring In:

United States: Arizona, California, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Massachusetts, Michigan, New Hampshire, New York State, New Mexico, North Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia

The anticipated closing date to submit applications for this role is June 20th, 2026. Join our Greenhouse Candidate Portal to track your application status and receive instant alerts for future openings.

The base salary range presented represents the anticipated low and high end salary range for new hires in this position. Your final base salary will be determined based on factors such as work location, experience, job related skills, and relevant training and education. The range listed is just one component of the total compensation package provided by Agero to employees.

National Pay Range: $110,000 USD - $135,000 USD

Life at Agero:

At Agero, you'll find a workplace where your unique perspective is not just welcomed, it's celebrated. We believe that our differences make us stronger, and we're committed to creating an environment where every employee feels a sense of belonging. If you're looking for a company that values your individuality, provides opportunities for growth, and champions open communication, Agero is the place for you. Join our team and help us drive the future of driver assistance, while experiencing a workplace where you can truly thrive.

Benefits Built for Well-being: 

Agero’s innovation is driven by a workforce where all associates feel like they can truly thrive. Agero offers a wide range of benefits to promote well-being, encourage personal development, and ensure financial stability. Our benefits include:

Health and Wellness: Healthcare, dental, vision, disability, life insurance, and mental health benefits for associates and their families.
Financial Security: 401(k) plan with company match and tuition assistance to support your future goals.
Work-Life Balance: Flexible time off, paid sick leave, and ten paid holidays annually.
For Contact Center Roles: Accrual of up to 3 weeks Paid Time Off per year, paid sick leave, and ten paid holidays annually.
Family Support: Parental planning benefits to assist associates through life’s milestones.
Bonus/Incentive Programs

Join Agero and experience a workplace that invests in your success both personally and professionally.

*Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full‑time basis. This position is not eligible for employer visa sponsorship now or in the future.

*It is unlawful in Massachusetts to required or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

Title: Business System Analyst
Description:
Our client is a global technology and consulting firm engaged with a leading asset management organization to modernize and enhance its investment technology platform. This role is embedded directly within the client environment, supporting a high-visibility initiative focused on investment performance, attribution, and portfolio data systems.




Role Overview

We are seeking a Senior Business Analyst with part-time product management responsibilities to own requirements, backlog, and agile delivery for a complex investment platform. The right candidate brings deep asset management domain knowledge, hands-on technical skills, and a track record of driving delivery in fast-moving, cross-functional environments.




Key Responsibilities

Business & Domain Analysis

Lead requirements elicitation sessions with business and operations stakeholders across front, middle, and back-office teams
Translate complex asset management processes into user stories, acceptance criteria, and functional specifications
Map current and future-state workflows, identifying gaps and optimization opportunities

Agile Delivery & Part-Time PM (20–40%)

Facilitate agile ceremonies including sprint planning, stand-ups, retrospectives, and backlog refinement
Own and groom the product backlog in partnership with the Product Owner
Track sprint velocity, manage dependencies, and communicate delivery status to stakeholders and leadership
Coordinate cross-functional delivery across data engineering, analytics, and QA workstreams

Technical & Data Collaboration

Write and maintain SQL queries for data validation, reconciliation, and ad hoc analysis
Build BI visualizations, preferably using Power BI, to support decision-making and executive readouts
Champion AI-augmented practices across the SDLC — requirements drafting, test case generation, automated documentation




Required Qualifications

5+ years as a Business Systems Analyst or equivalent within asset management or financial services
Strong understanding of front, middle, and back office operations — trade lifecycle, portfolio accounting, investment performance, reconciliation, and client reporting
Working knowledge of financial instruments including equities, fixed income, derivatives, alternatives, ETFs, and mutual funds
Demonstrated ability to independently drive requirements gathering, stakeholder workshops, and documentation
Experience facilitating agile ceremonies in a Scrum or Kanban environment
Proficiency in SQL for querying, data validation, and analytical support




Preferred Qualifications

CFA, CIPM, CAIA, or equivalent designation (or active candidacy)
Understanding of investment performance measurement — TWR, MWR, attribution analysis, benchmark construction, and risk measures such as VaR, tracking error, and Sharpe ratio
Experience with Power BI for dashboard development or data visualization
Working knowledge of Python for scripting, data manipulation, or automation
Familiarity with cloud data platforms such as Microsoft Fabric, Azure Synapse, Snowflake, or Databricks
Experience with JIRA, Azure DevOps, or equivalent agile planning tools




What We're Looking For

A self-starter who takes ownership and drives work forward without being asked
Intellectual curiosity about markets, instruments, and the investment management value chain
Strong communication skills bridging technical teams and business stakeholders
Enthusiasm for experimenting with and integrating AI tools into planning, requirements, testing, and delivery
Collaborative mindset with a bias toward clarity, transparency, and continuous improvement

ASSISTANT DIRECTOR, DATA STRATEGY & BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE, Campus Services and Stewardship , to lead the development and execution of a centralized data strategy across a large, complex organization supporting MIT’s built environment; establish enterprise data standards, including common definitions, metrics, and authoritative data sources, and drive their adoption; design and deliver scalable data models, dashboards, and reporting solutions to support decision-making across finance, operations, and capital planning; partner with Finance, Operations, and Business Systems to translate complex needs into governed data products; represent CSS in Institute-wide data governance and ERP initiatives, influencing system design, data architecture, and reporting frameworks; and serve as a strategic advisor to leadership to advance data-driven decision-making and operational effectiveness.

This position has a hybrid schedule and will require at least 2 days on-site.

Title: Analyst
Description:
Jamestown is a design-focused, vertically integrated real estate investment and management firm with a 40+ year track record and a mission to create places that inspire. Since formally launching the Jamestown Green sustainability program in 2012, the firm has embedded environmental, social, and governance (ESG) strategy across its global portfolio — and set a corporate goal to achieve net zero operational carbon by 2050.

The Sustainability Analyst will join a small, high-impact team responsible for executing that strategy across Jamestown's US portfolio. This is a broad role by design: the Analyst will be involved in all major workstreams, from portfolio-wide data collection and ESG reporting to certification management, investor communications, and property-level project coordination. This person will have direct visibility into firm-wide sustainability strategy and regular exposure to senior leadership across Asset and Property Management, Development & Construction, Investor Relations, etc.

We are looking for someone who is a critical thinker, exceptionally organized, able to work and communicate with individuals at various levels in the organization, and is confident owning things that can appear ambiguous. The ideal candidate is comfortable embracing opportunities to demonstrate leadership and ambition alongside their daily responsibilities supporting the administrative functions of the ESG team. While not a requirement, we are also looking for someone with either existing generative AI skills or openness to learning and leverage generative AI. You will see the full picture of how a sustainability program operates at a leading real estate firm, including the strategic decisions, the stakeholder dynamics, and the operational detail that makes it all work.

Essential Job Functions

ESG Data, Reporting, and Compliance


In partnership with our consultants, support Manager of Sustainability in managing the process to collect, maintain, and quality-check environmental performance data (energy, water, waste, GHG) across the US portfolio in coordination with property teams and third-party platforms such as Scaler and ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager
Support preparation of Jamestown's Annual Sustainability and Social Responsibility Report, including data compilation, fact-checking, drafting, and facilitating stakeholder and compliance reviews
Assist with the firm's annual GRESB and UNPRI submissions, including data and evidence gathering, scoring analysis, project management and post submission recapping/debriefing
Prepare ESG performance narratives and fund-level reporting materials in coordination with Asset Management and Investor Relations; maintaining a pulse on ESG initiatives and case studies across the portfolio for appropriate audiences
Own first-draft responses to ESG due diligence questionnaires from institutional investors, coordinating with the team and consultants for review and finalization
Maintain an internal pulse on emerging ESG regulations, building performance standards, and incentive programs relevant to US assets, while drawing on consultant inputs and independent research to prepare concise summary memos and recommendations that translate external influences into portfolio and property-specific initiatives for team review


Property-Level Initiatives


Support Manager of Sustainability in implementation of sustainability pilots and projects at the property-level, tracking progress and learnings
Track and manage certification (ex. LEED, Fitwel) project timelines across the portfolio, coordinating with property teams and external consultants to ensure deadlines and documentation are complete
Execute and maintain regular tenant communication cadences related to sustainability programming and reporting requirements, in coordination with Property Management and consultants


Project & Process Management


Support Manager of Sustainability in maintaining and improving database(s) and tracking systems for portfolio-wide and property-specific sustainability initiatives, certifications, reporting deadlines, and data requests; serve as the team's coordinator of shared documentation, templates, and institutional knowledge so that processes are repeatable and not person-dependent
Support Manager of Sustainability and Director of Innovation & Sustainability in identifying opportunities to build AI-assisted workflows, automations, or templates that reduce manual effort on repeatable tasks; take ownership of building and maintaining those tools for the team
Support Manager of Sustainability as the coordination hub for the Sustainability team, managing day-to-day workflows, tracking open items and deliverables across internal workstreams and external consultants and vendors, and ensuring work is progressing on time and on scope; proactively surface blockers and keep the team moving forward


Experience

MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS REQUIRED


Bachelor's degree. Relevant fields of study may include Environmental Studies, Sustainability, Urban Planning, Real Estate, Finance, Business, Engineering, or related discipline.
2-4 years of relevant experience; backgrounds in real estate, ESG consulting, corporate sustainability, project management, junior Chief of Staff type roles, or a related field are welcomed, but not necessarily required
Any combination of education, training, and experience which provides the required knowledge, skills, and abilities to perform the essential functions of the job


Skills And Abilities


High degree of self-motivation, personal accountability, and collaborative instinct
Genuine curiosity about sustainability and the built environment, and a desire to build deep expertise over time
Confidence engaging with senior stakeholders — you are energized, not intimidated, by a conversation with the CFO
Project management and task management best practices – this role requires owning multiple concurrent workstreams with competing deadlines
Both Microsoft Office and Google Workspace tools
Facility with standard Generative AI tools (ex. Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini)
Ability to manage up, laterally, and with external partners
Strong analytical skills with rigorous attention to detail and accuracy
Clear, professional written and verbal communication skills; ability to translate complex data into coherent narratives for varied audiences


Bonus Skills


ESG reporting frameworks and tools, including GRESB, ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager, and sustainability data platforms such as Scaler
Real estate fundamentals and/or commercial property operations


HOURS

Responsibilities may require an adjusted work schedule, travel, overtime, and evening/weekend hours in order to meet deadlines or attend special events.

Jamestown is a design-focused, vertically integrated real estate investment and management firm with a 40+ year track record and a mission to create places that inspire. Since formally launching the Jamestown Green sustainability program in 2012, the firm has embedded environmental, social, and governance (ESG) strategy across its global portfolio — and set a corporate goal to achieve net zero operational carbon by 2050.

The Sustainability Analyst will join a small, high-impact team responsible for executing that strategy across Jamestown's US portfolio. This is a broad role by design: the Analyst will be involved in all major workstreams, from portfolio-wide data collection and ESG reporting to certification management, investor communications, and property-level project coordination. This person will have direct visibility into firm-wide sustainability strategy and regular exposure to senior leadership across Asset and Property Management, Development & Construction, Investor Relations, etc.

We are looking for someone who is a critical thinker, exceptionally organized, able to work and communicate with individuals at various levels in the organization, and is confident owning things that can appear ambiguous. The ideal candidate is comfortable embracing opportunities to demonstrate leadership and ambition alongside their daily responsibilities supporting the administrative functions of the ESG team. While not a requirement, we are also looking for someone with either existing generative AI skills or openness to learning and leverage generative AI. You will see the full picture of how a sustainability program operates at a leading real estate firm, including the strategic decisions, the stakeholder dynamics, and the operational detail that makes it all work.

Essential Job Functions

ESG Data, Reporting, and Compliance

  • In partnership with our consultants, support Manager of Sustainability in managing the process to collect, maintain, and quality-check environmental performance data (energy, water, waste, GHG) across the US portfolio in coordination with property teams and third-party platforms such as Scaler and ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager
  • Support preparation of Jamestown's Annual Sustainability and Social Responsibility Report, including data compilation, fact-checking, drafting, and facilitating stakeholder and compliance reviews
  • Assist with the firm's annual GRESB and UNPRI submissions, including data and evidence gathering, scoring analysis, project management and post submission recapping/debriefing
  • Prepare ESG performance narratives and fund-level reporting materials in coordination with Asset Management and Investor Relations; maintaining a pulse on ESG initiatives and case studies across the portfolio for appropriate audiences
  • Own first-draft responses to ESG due diligence questionnaires from institutional investors, coordinating with the team and consultants for review and finalization
  • Maintain an internal pulse on emerging ESG regulations, building performance standards, and incentive programs relevant to US assets, while drawing on consultant inputs and independent research to prepare concise summary memos and recommendations that translate external influences into portfolio and property-specific initiatives for team review

Property-Level Initiatives

  • Support Manager of Sustainability in implementation of sustainability pilots and projects at the property-level, tracking progress and learnings
  • Track and manage certification (ex. LEED, Fitwel) project timelines across the portfolio, coordinating with property teams and external consultants to ensure deadlines and documentation are complete
  • Execute and maintain regular tenant communication cadences related to sustainability programming and reporting requirements, in coordination with Property Management and consultants

Project & Process Management

  • Support Manager of Sustainability in maintaining and improving database(s) and tracking systems for portfolio-wide and property-specific sustainability initiatives, certifications, reporting deadlines, and data requests; serve as the team's coordinator of shared documentation, templates, and institutional knowledge so that processes are repeatable and not person-dependent
  • Support Manager of Sustainability and Director of Innovation & Sustainability in identifying opportunities to build AI-assisted workflows, automations, or templates that reduce manual effort on repeatable tasks; take ownership of building and maintaining those tools for the team
  • Support Manager of Sustainability as the coordination hub for the Sustainability team, managing day-to-day workflows, tracking open items and deliverables across internal workstreams and external consultants and vendors, and ensuring work is progressing on time and on scope; proactively surface blockers and keep the team moving forward

Experience

MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS REQUIRED

  • Bachelor's degree. Relevant fields of study may include Environmental Studies, Sustainability, Urban Planning, Real Estate, Finance, Business, Engineering, or related discipline.
  • 2-4 years of relevant experience; backgrounds in real estate, ESG consulting, corporate sustainability, project management, junior Chief of Staff type roles, or a related field are welcomed, but not necessarily required
  • Any combination of education, training, and experience which provides the required knowledge, skills, and abilities to perform the essential functions of the job

Skills And Abilities

  • High degree of self-motivation, personal accountability, and collaborative instinct
  • Genuine curiosity about sustainability and the built environment, and a desire to build deep expertise over time
  • Confidence engaging with senior stakeholders — you are energized, not intimidated, by a conversation with the CFO
  • Project management and task management best practices – this role requires owning multiple concurrent workstreams with competing deadlines
  • Both Microsoft Office and Google Workspace tools
  • Facility with standard Generative AI tools (ex. Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini)
  • Ability to manage up, laterally, and with external partners
  • Strong analytical skills with rigorous attention to detail and accuracy
  • Clear, professional written and verbal communication skills; ability to translate complex data into coherent narratives for varied audiences

Bonus Skills

  • ESG reporting frameworks and tools, including GRESB, ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager, and sustainability data platforms such as Scaler
  • Real estate fundamentals and/or commercial property operations

HOURS

Responsibilities may require an adjusted work schedule, travel, overtime, and evening/weekend hours in order to meet deadlines or attend special events.

Title: Junior Biomedical Data Analyst
Description:
About LifeCanvas Technologies




LifeCanvas Technologies, an MIT spinoff venture, is revolutionizing the way we see and study biological systems. LifeCanvas offers cutting-edge technologies and end-to-end solutions that enable holistic molecular imaging of biological systems, accelerating breakthrough discoveries in science and the development of new therapeutic strategies. As a key partner in this global effort, our computational team works on enabling scientific innovation and directly collaborates with global researchers in bioengineering, neuroscience, and oncology.




Job Summary

We are seeking an ambitious and motivated Junior Biomedical Data Analyst to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience with biomedical data analysis, machine learning, or scientific computing, and a strong interest in building scalable software solutions for both research and industrial applications in a fast-paced start-up environment. In this role, you will support the analysis of large-scale 3D fluorescence microscopy datasets generated by LifeCanvas technologies. You will work closely with our tissue processing, image acquisition, and Contract Research teams to run analyses, validate outputs, troubleshoot issues, and help generate high-quality quantitative results for internal teams and external scientific partners.




Key Responsibilities:

Run, validate, and troubleshoot analyses for Contract Research projects, ensuring accurate results and timely project completion.
Perform quality control on analysis outputs to validate results.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure smooth data acquisition, integration, and downstream analysis.
Communicate analytical findings clearly to internal teams and external scientific partners.
Assist with maintaining and improving components of LifeCanvas image-analysis pipelines for large-scale 3D fluorescence microscopy datasets.
Support the development of data engineering workflows, including database management, ETL processes, and handling of large imaging datasets.




Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in computer science, bioengineering, computational biology, electrical engineering, or a relevant field.
Experience with image processing for biological and/or biomedical applications
Experience using Python for data science, scientific computing, automation, and image analysis.
Strong problem-solving skills with a self-motivated and detail-oriented mindset and the ability to work in a team environment.
Adaptable and proactive in a fast-paced environment, comfortable working across multiple concurrent projects and evolving requirements.
Excellent communication skills and the ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams.




Preferred Qualifications:

Familiarity with light microscopy, neuroscience, and immunohistochemistry
Familiarity with best practices for software engineering.
Exposure to machine learning or deep learning frameworks such as PyTorch or TensorFlow.
Experience in working with data engineering/analysis pipelines.




Benefits:

Competitive salary.
Full insurance package for full-time employees.
Flexible time off.
401(k) benefits.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
ZoomInfo is where careers accelerate. We move fast, think boldly, and empower you to do the best work of your life. You’ll be surrounded by teammates who care deeply, challenge each other, and celebrate wins. With tools that amplify your impact and a culture that backs your ambition, you won’t just contribute. You’ll make things happen–fast.

ZoomInfo is seeking a Senior Data Analyst to drive our customer insights and competitive intelligence strategy, leading analyses that shape how we evolve our data products to meet customer needs and maintain market leadership.

In this role, you'll become the expert on customer experience with our data product and ZoomInfo's competitive landscape. You'll design rigorous analyses (understanding complex problems, forming hypotheses, testing with data, and delivering well-backed recommendations) that translate customer feedback, market trends, and competitive movements into strategic priorities. This isn't about building dashboards or SQL reports. This is about strategic analysis that drives decision-making and ensures our data products continuously evolve to power GTM and agentic workflows for sales and marketing professionals.

As a trusted advisor to Product, Engineering, and Data Leadership, you'll progressively own more of the customer insights strategy and lead larger strategic initiatives. This is a role with a significant growth runway for someone who wants to become the go-to expert on customer-driven data strategy and competitive positioning.

Who You Are

Strategic Thinker with Analytical Depth: You understand how to translate customer data and market intelligence into actionable strategy. You've worked with customer analytics, product intelligence, or competitive analysis. Maybe you've shaped product strategy, identified market opportunities, or driven data-driven prioritization. You don't just execute—you have informed opinions on what matters, and you're comfortable saying "here's what we should do and why" based on your analytical understanding and business judgment.

Growth-Oriented Problem Solver: You're excited to build deep expertise in customer analytics and competitive intelligence and grow into leading strategic initiatives. You're equally comfortable designing quantitative analyses and conducting qualitative research, connecting data patterns to business impact. You thrive in evolving environments where priorities shift and problems aren't always well-defined, maintaining momentum and quality even when the path forward isn't perfectly clear.

Clear Communicator: You can build compelling narratives that drive decisions, whether through presentations, written reports, or demos. You're skilled at distilling complex analyses, defending recommendations, and translating technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders.

What You'll Do

In your first 12-18 months, your primary focus will be building a customer feedback framework that drives prioritization and establishing yourself as the customer insights expert. You'll work with team members who have context on our data products and systems, learning the landscape while bringing fresh analytical perspectives and strategic depth. As you gain mastery, you'll increasingly own customer insights strategy and lead strategic data improvement initiatives.

Build Deep Customer & Product Intelligence Expertise


Analyze customer behavior patterns, feedback data, and usage metrics to understand how customers experience our data and where opportunities for improvement exist in coverage, quality, and depth
Master ZoomInfo's competitive landscape: who our competitors are, what data they offer (company, person, and intelligence signals), how they're evolving, and where differentiation opportunities exist
Develop standardized frameworks for evaluating competitive threats such as data models backed by multiple sources that provide cadenced competitive intelligence
Create clear intelligence briefs, customer insights reports, and strategic recommendations that inform data product roadmaps


Drive Strategic Prioritization Through Analysis & Insight


Build and maintain a customer feedback framework leveraging internal tools that track customer platform usage, unstructured data from customer feedback sources like call recordings, and other data assets to establish yourself as the go-to expert, especially for technical stakeholders with less customer and competitive knowledge
Influence prioritization conversations about data product roadmaps by bringing customer insights, competitive intelligence, and informed opinions on strategic direction
Translate customer pain points and emerging competitive trends into concrete data improvement opportunities and strategic recommendations


Lead Complex, Forward-Looking Strategic Initiatives


Identify and lead strategic opportunities for future-looking core data initiatives based on customer needs and competitive gaps—such as new data sources, expanded attributes, improved coverage, or new intelligence signals. These analyses may range from week-long investigations to month-long strategic projects
Combine customer analytics, competitive research, understanding of ZoomInfo's data pipelines, and business judgment to solve complex problems and drive buy-in for strategic initiatives
Collaborate with Engineering and Product to assess technical feasibility and shape solution approaches


Build Partnerships & Institutional Knowledge


Develop strong working relationships with Product, Engineering, Data Leadership, and fellow data analysts
Conduct impact analyses that quantify how core data product improvements affect coverage, accuracy, and depth, validating whether strategic initiatives deliver intended customer or competitive outcomes
Document your learning, approaches, and insights so customer and competitive knowledge is shared across the team
Serve as a strategic resource, helping bridge immediate customer or competitive needs with long-term data capabilities


Required

What You'll Bring


Bachelor's degree in Business, Economics, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or related quantitative field
5+ years of experience in data analytics, customer analytics, product analytics or related strategic roles (these backgrounds are interchangeable)
Experience conducting customer analytics, market research, or competitive analysis that informed product or business strategy
Ability to design and execute rigorous analyses, solving ambiguous, multi-faceted business problems that required figuring out the approach and delivering well-backed recommendations that drive decisions
Strong programming skills in Python and SQL for data analysis and manipulation. You may use AI tools to accelerate coding, but should be capable of building technical proof-of-concepts or experimental tools independently
Demonstrated ability to work effectively with Product and/or Engineering teams, translating between analytical findings and strategic business needs
Excellent communication skills: able to build compelling narratives and explain strategic recommendations to diverse audiences
Self-directed with strong ownership mentality: you drive your work forward and know when to seek input


Strongly Preferred


Experience with B2B data products, business intelligence platforms, or data-as-a-product companies
Experience with customer analytics, product analytics, or usage data analysis
Background in competitive intelligence or market research
Knowledge of GTM workflows, sales intelligence tools, or B2B go-to-market strategies
Experience creating strategic frameworks or conducting impact assessments
Track record of leading or co-leading cross-functional strategic initiatives


This Role Is Not a Fit If

You're looking to focus primarily on building dashboards or data engineering architecture, creating reports, or tracking ongoing metrics. While you'll conduct strategic analyses and need general knowledge of ZoomInfo's data pipelines to inform your recommendations, the core mission is forward-looking strategic analysis focused on customer insights and competitive intelligence, not operational reporting or production engineering.

This Role Is a Strong Fit If

You want to become an expert on customer-driven data strategy and competitive positioning and grow into leading strategic initiatives. You're excited to build deep analytical expertise, contribute informed opinions to prioritization decisions, and progressively take on more ownership as you master the domain. You enjoy solving problems where the answer isn't obvious and seeing your analysis directly shape product direction.

Actual compensation offered will be based on factors such as the candidate’s work location, qualifications, skills, experience and/or training. Your recruiter can share more information about the specific salary range for your desired work location during the hiring process. We want our employees and their families to thrive.

In addition to comprehensive benefits we offer holistic mind, body and lifestyle programs designed for overall well-being. Learn more about ZoomInfo benefits here.

Below is the US base salary for this position. Additional compensation such as Bonus, Commission, Equity and other benefits may also apply.

$102,200—$160,600 USD

About Us

ZoomInfo (NASDAQ: GTM) is the Go-To-Market Intelligence Platform that empowers businesses to grow faster with AI-ready insights, trusted data, and advanced automation. Its solutions provide more than 35,000 companies worldwide with a complete view of their customers, making every seller their best seller.

ZoomInfo is committed to protecting your privacy when you apply for jobs with us. Please review our Job Applicant Privacy Notice for more details on how we handle your personal information.

ZoomInfo may use a software-based assessment as part of the recruitment process. More information about this tool, including the results of the most recent bias audit, is available here.

ZoomInfo is proud to be an equal opportunity employer, hiring based on qualifications, merit, and business needs, and does not discriminate based on protected status. We welcome all applicants and are committed to providing equal employment opportunities regardless of sex, race, age, color, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, disability status, religion, protected military or veteran status, medical condition, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We also consider qualified candidates with criminal histories in accordance with legal requirements.

For Massachusetts Applicants: It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability. ZoomInfo does not administer lie detector tests to applicants in any location.

Title: Intern | ALS - Data Modernization and Analysis
Description:
ENABLE your future through light.

Excelitas is a global technology leader with more than 7,500 employees, focused on delivering market-driven solutions to fulfill the illumination, optical, detection and imaging needs of OEMs and end-users across the biomedical, semiconductor, industrial, consumer products, scientific, security, defense and aerospace sectors.

ENGAGE with us today and make your contribution to the future! Join the team that leading technology companies turn to for cutting-edge photonic innovation. At Excelitas Technologies you are how we EXCEL.




Overview

Excelitas Technologies, a leader in photonics and frequency tunable laser solutions, is seeking an intern to support data modernization efforts within our wafer fabrication operations. This 8‑week, on‑site internship offers hands‑on experience with manufacturing data processing, code migration, and basic image analysis. An extension may be available based on performance and project needs.

Responsibilities

Clean, standardize, and prepare wafer‑fab data for database upload.
Support data validation and documentation of data sources and formats.
Assist in converting legacy code (ex., VB6 to C#.NET), including code translation and functional checks.
Perform basic image feature analysis (e.g., particle counting) and label data for future ML or AI workflows.

Preferred Qualifications

Pursuing a degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Applied Physics, or related fields.
Exposure to C#, Python, SQL or LabVIEW.
Interest in image analysis or feature extraction.

What You’ll Gain

Exposure to real wafer‑fab workflows in a photonics‑focused company.
Hands‑on experience with data modernization and legacy code migration.
Mentorship from experienced engineering staff.

Compensation:

Pay Range: $22hr - $27hr Depending on Experience




This position requires the use of information which is subject to the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR)

Visa sponsorship is not available for any position at Excelitas

Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer

Minorities/Females/Disability/Veteran/Gender Identity/Sexual Orientation

Excelitas is seeking leaders and innovators to join our global team!

Visit: https://jobs.excelitas.com/

Title: Data Analyst (Marketing Analytics, Bangkok Based, Relocation provided)
Description:
About Agoda

At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.

Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.

No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.

This role is based in Bangkok, Thailand, and is open to candidates worldwide. We offer a generous relocation package to ensure a smooth transition to living and working in Bangkok.

Get to Know our Team:

The Performance Marketing Team of Agoda is a world leader in online marketing. This department is highly data-driven and focused on developing at-scale marketing programs that improve the lifetime value of Agoda customers through measurable marketing programs and channels. The team is a blend of the best analysts, marketing strategists, and data scientists in the world. 2–3 years of experience

Strong English communication
Solid business acumen
Strong statistical and analytical skills
Proficient in Excel and hands-on with data tools (SAS, R, SPSS, Python, VBA, SQL, Tableau)
Experience designing and analyzing A/B tests
Works independently on daily analytics tasks and provides business recommendations


It’s Great if you Have:


Experience in digital marketing
Academic research experience

Title: Performance Analyst
Description:
Role Description & Responsibilities:

The Performance Analyst serves as the analytical engine of the North America Business Planning team. Working side-by-side with our Industry Business Planners, you will turn data into insight, transform spreadsheets into decisions, and help shape the strategies that drive revenue growth and market share gains across our industry portfolio. Rather than owning a single industry vertical, you will work horizontally partnering with every Industry Business Planner on the team to deliver the market analyses, financial models, and competitive intelligence that fuel their go-to-market (GTM) plans.

This role is purpose-built for early-career professionals who are eager to learn and excited to apply modern tools — including AI, machine learning, generative AI, and AI-assisted coding — to real business problems. You will sharpen your strategic thinking, build a foundation in B2B enterprise software strategy, and develop the analytical, technical, and storytelling skills that power senior planning roles. Enterprise software experience is helpful but not required; curiosity, analytical horsepower, and a builder’s mindset are.


Partner with Industry Business Planners across all verticals — including Automotive, Industrial Equipment, Aerospace & Defense, High-Tech, and others — to provide analytical support, market research, and financial modeling
Build and maintain macroeconomic, market, and competitive analyses that inform 1- and 3-year strategic plans across the territory
Support the annual sales planning exercise: territory analysis, account scoring, target setting, capacity modeling, and revenue plan build-out across all sales distribution channels
Develop financial models and tools to support revenue planning, performance analysis, and other items to improve insights and business decisions
Design and maintain dashboards, automations, and analytical tools that scale the productivity of the Business Planning team
Apply AI, machine learning, and generative AI tools to accelerate research, automate repetitive workflows, and surface insights at greater speed and scale
Use AI-assisted coding (Python, SQL, scripting) to prototype analytical tools, automations, and data workflows — even if you are still learning to code, AI copilots will be your collaborator from day one
Conduct ongoing competitor intelligence: monitor competitor moves, win/loss patterns, pricing dynamics, and market positioning; package findings into clear narratives for Sales and leadership
Support the identification of priority accounts and OEM/supply-chain ecosystem networks that anchor our growth ambitions
Synthesize macroeconomic, industry, and account-level data into briefings, decks, and recommendations for Sales Directors, Industry Business Planners, and senior leadership
Contribute to the feedback loop with global Market Intelligence and Industry teams, surfacing North America–specific dynamics and trends
Proactively propose enhancements to our planning processes, methods, and content — fresh ideas welcome


Qualifications:


Bachelor’s degree in a related field (Business, Economics, Engineering, Finance, Data Analytics, Statistics, or similar)
5–8 years of experience in business analysis, strategy, finance, market research, sales operations, or a related analytical role preferred
Master’s degree or MBA is a plus, but not required
Demonstrated interest in AI, machine learning, analytics, or modern data tooling — through coursework, side projects, certifications, or prior work — is highly valued


Inclusion statement

In order to provide equal employment and advancement opportunities to all individuals, employment decisions at 3DS are based on merit, qualifications and abilities. 3DS is committed to a policy of non-discrimination and equal opportunity for all employees and qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth or medical or common conditions related to pregnancy or childbirth), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, familial status, national origin, ancestry, age (40 and above), disability, veteran status, military service, application for military service, genetic information, receipt of free medical care, or any other characteristic protected under applicable law. 3DS will make reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with known disabilities, in accordance with applicable law. Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with applicable state laws and local ordinances. We are committed to fair employment practices and will evaluate all candidates based on their qualifications, regardless of past arrest or conviction history.

Compensation & Benefits

Dassault Systèmes offers an excellent salary with potential for bonus, commensurate with experience. Benefits include a choice of plans providing comprehensive coverage for medical, dental, vision care for employee & dependents as well as employee life, short & long term disability, tuition reimbursement, immediate 401K enrollment, 401K match (50 cents on the dollar, up to the first 8% of your eligible compensation that you contribute based on match eligibility criteria), flexible time off policy, and 10 paid holidays.

Salary Pay Transparency

Compensation for the role will be commensurate with experience. The total expected compensation range will be between $103000 and $120000, representing the base salary (or annualized salary based on estimated hourly compensation) and target bonus.

Title: Senior Associate - Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

We are currently seeking a senior associate data analyst to help us collect, transform and extract meaning from data for the Global Technology Services (GTS) Control Tower team at State Street. The position performs a wide range of activities with experts from GTS, business intelligence, business analysis, data engineering, process engineering, governance, and data analysts to collaboratively solve a wide array of complex technical problems.

About The GTS Control Tower Team

The GTS Control Tower team is an exciting and growing area of State Street with unique insight into the whole spectrum of our data, metrics, and dashboards at State Street. This name change reflects the work we are actively doing across GTS: integrating dependency management, business analysis, process design, and governance to transform data into action and keep remediation efforts flying smoothly.

 

The GTS Control Tower Mission

Our core focus is on transforming data into actionable insights. Our goal is to enable GTS Leadership to accelerate progress against key strategic initiatives across GTS and to drive results.  

Core Responsibilities


Report & Dashboard Development: Design, develop, and maintain high‑quality, accessible interactive Power BI dashboards and reports that clearly communicate key performance indicators (KPIs) and business insights.
Data Modeling & Transformation: Build and optimize semantic data models, including relationships, calculated columns, and measures using DAX. Perform data extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) using Python and Power Query (M).
Data Source Integration: Connect, consolidate, and manage data from multiple sources such as SQL databases, Azure services, Excel, and other enterprise systems into reliable, well‑structured datasets.
Performance Optimization: Tune data models and DAX calculations to ensure optimal performance, scalability, and user experience.
Security & Platform Administration: Implement and maintain row‑level security (RLS), manage Power BI workspaces and apps, and support capacity and governance best practices within the Power BI Service.
Stakeholder Collaboration: Partner with business stakeholders to gather requirements, translate business needs into analytical solutions, and deliver actionable, decision‑ready insights.
Documentation & Standards: Create and maintain clear documentation for data models, transformation logic, report definitions, and operational procedures to support transparency and long‑term maintainability.



What We Value


Willingness to learn and develop new skills, embracing challenges and continuous improvement
Strong work ethic, focus on achieving goals, and delivering measurable results
Desire for creative thinking, problem-solving, and proposing new ideas to improve processes or products
People who thrive in a collaborative team environment



Requirements


An online portfolio with examples with nonconfidential data that demonstrates your analysis skills
Some experience with Microsoft Power BI, DAX, Power Query, M, ETL, Python, PySpark, SQL
A Bachelor's degree in a data analytics, data science, computer science or equivalent experience



Salary Range

$52,000 - $91,000 Annual

The range quoted above applies to the role in the primary location specified. If the candidate would ultimately work outside of the primary location above, the applicable range could differ.

Employees are eligible to participate in State Street’s comprehensive benefits program, which includes: our retirement savings plan (401K) with company match; insurance coverage including basic life, medical, dental, vision, long-term disability, and other optional additional coverages; paid-time off including vacation, sick leave, short term disability, and family care responsibilities; access to our Employee Assistance Program; incentive compensation including eligibility for annual performance-based awards (excluding certain sales roles subject to sales incentive plans); and, eligibility for certain tax advantaged savings plans.

For a full overview, visit https://hrportal.ehr.com/statestreet/Home.

About State Street

Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success.

We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future.

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law.

Read our CEO Statement

Job Application Disclosure

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

Title: Technical Associate I, FSAS
Description:
Posting Description

TECHNICAL ASSOCIATE, FSAS, MIT Sloan FSAS , will help create an extensive ETL [Extract, Transform, Load] pipeline to process numerous datasets. The individual will identify and vet data sources; develop pipelines to robustly extract, process, load, and validate data; develop data products such as violation risk scores and product classification; and performing and write up research on these data products. Data sources are in multiple languages and include both structured and unstructured data. Working as part of the Data Science subgroup, the Technical Associate I will collaborate closely with interdisciplinary teams to ensure data quality, consistency, and usability across the project. The role will also involve conducting exploratory analyses, supporting model development, and contributing to written research outputs, including technical documentation and reports. This position offers an opportunity to contribute to high-impact research at the intersection of data science, food safety, and public health, while gaining experience with complex, real-world datasets and advanced analytical methodologies.

A full job description is available at https://web.mit.edu/sloan-hr/jobs/TechnicalAssociateIFSAS.pdf

Job Requirements

REQUIRED : Bachelor’s degree in a relevant scientific field (computer science, engineering, mathematics); a minimum of two years of specialized experience with qualitative data analysis, research methods, social sciences (which may include experience gained as an undergraduate); familiarity using Python and SQL; familiarity using Github; ability to work both independently and as a member of a team; attention to detail; and familiarity with basic statistical techniques (e.g. hypothesis testing, simple linear models). PREFERRED : Experience working with databases (MSSQL); experience working with Docker; experience working with Selenium; experience working with machine learning (TensorFlow); and an interest in food safety and security issues.

This is a one-year term position with full benefits.

Title: BI Analyst
Description:
We are seeking a highly analytical and technically proficient Data Analyst to support a small number of high-value enterprise accounts. This role is focused on owning the integrity, accuracy, and clarity of performance data across complex, multi-channel marketing programs.

Working closely alongside an Enterprise Account Director or Strategy Director, you will act as the primary analytical partner, ensuring performance is measurable, insights are actionable, and attribution frameworks are sound. This role centers on building a comprehensive understanding of each client’s business, measurement framework, and the factors influencing performance outcomes.

This position plays a critical cross-functional role between Strategy, Paid Media, Client Services, and Analytics.

What You'll Do

Data Analysis & Business Intelligence


Analyze multi-channel data to identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities tied to key business outcomes, including revenue, CAC, LTV, and MER.


Measurement & Attribution Ownership


Own the end-to-end measurement framework across assigned enterprise accounts
Ensure accurate conversion tracking across platforms, including Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn, TikTok, and CRM environments
Validate attribution logic and identify gaps in tracking, reporting, or data flow
Partner with Strategy to align measurement to business KPIs (revenue, pipeline, LTV, CAC)


Tag Management & Technical Implementation


Lead implementation and ongoing optimization of tracking via Google Tag Manager
Configure and maintain complex tag setups, including custom events, triggers, and variables
Support server-side tagging and advanced tracking environments where applicable
Collaborate with developers to ensure accurate data layer implementation


Data Quality, QA & Governance


Own QA processes across tracking, tagging, and reporting outputs
Maintain clean, consistent event taxonomies and UTM structures
Proactively identify discrepancies in platform vs. source-of-truth reporting
Ensure compliance with privacy frameworks and consent management tools


Performance Analysis & Insight


Translate raw data into clear, actionable insights tied to business outcomes
Support deep-dive analyses on performance shifts, channel contribution, and funnel efficiency
Partner with the Strategy Director to inform testing roadmaps and investment decisions
Build or support dashboards that provide clarity into performance across channels


Cross-Functional Partnership


Act as the analytical counterpart to the Strategy Director / Enterprise AD
Support campaign launches with tracking validation and measurement readiness
Collaborate with Paid Media, Client Services, and Analytics teams to ensure alignment
Serve as a key voice in diagnosing performance issues and identifying opportunities


What You'll Bring


6+ years of experience in ad operations, digital analytics, or conversion tracking.
Advanced experience with Google Tag Manager and GA4 with hands-on implementation experience.
Deep understanding of multi-touch attribution and cross-channel measurement
Strong understanding of browser-based tracking, cookies, and attribution principles.
Experience using debugging tools like Google Tag Assistant, Chrome DevTools, and Meta Pixel Helper.
Exceptional attention to detail and documentation skills.
Familiarity with server-side tagging environments and advanced GTM configurations.
Experience with CRM integrations (e.g. HubSpot, Salesforce) and offline conversion tracking
Familiarity with consent management tools and privacy-compliant tracking practices
Ability to write or modify JavaScript for custom tagging or enhanced eCommerce tracking.
Understanding of data layer strategies and dynamic event tracking.


Benefits and Perks We Provide


Remote Workplace. You have the option to work at our office in Boston or remotely in one of these states: AZ, CA, CO, CT, FL, ID, IL, KY, ME, MD, MA, MI, MO, NV, NH, NJ, NY, NC, SC, OH, OK, PA, RI, TN, TX, UT, VT, VA.
Paid Time Off. Receive generous paid vacation benefits that increase each year you’re with us, 14 Company Holidays, and Summer Fridays from Memorial Day through Labor Day.
401K + Match. 401K plan with 4% Safe Harbor employer match after one year of employment and financial literacy services provided through our retirement provider.
Life Insurance Benefit. No-cost coverage to ensure peace of mind for your family.
Short Term Disability Benefit. We've got you covered if you need to be OOO with an illness or injury that keeps you out of work.
Healthcare. Choose from several competitive healthcare plans for you and your family with 80% employer coverage for all medical and dental premiums, 100% employer coverage for vision, and the option to participate in the FSA program.
Commuter Benefits. Allocate pre-tax funds towards your commute to save up to 40% per year in transportation expenses.
Continuing Education. Access to monthly team-led webinars, exclusive 829-cohort based learning, digital course platforms, and funding opportunities to attend national conferences and events.


What We Believe

At 829 Studios, we boldly embrace diversity, inclusivity, and equal opportunity. Our goal: a team rich in backgrounds, perspectives, and talents. Why? Because inclusivity powers our brilliance and client success. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer that is committed to equal employment opportunities regardless of race, religion, creed, color, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, national origin, disability, mental illness, age, ancestry, genetic information, military service, or any other status protected under applicable federal, state, or local laws.

Who We Are

829 Studios is a Boston-based integrated digital marketing agency with a mission to propel organizations to unlock their full potential and accomplish their most ambitious goals. We harness the power of logic with magic – a balance of data-driven strategy and brand-focused creative. We partner with diverse organizations across various industries and our client list encompasses venture-backed start-ups, publicly traded companies, non-profits, and more. By committing not only to our growth as a company, but the development of our employees and teams’, we have created a collaborative environment that fosters ingenuity and promotes diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging values within our community. 829 has been recognized as one of the nation’s top agencies by the Inc. 5000 Fastest-Growing Privately Held Companies, Adweek 100 Fastest-Growing Digital Agencies, Boston Business Journal's Fast 50 (Massachusetts' 50 Fastest-Growing Private Companies), HubSpot's Top Digital Agencies list, and a we are and employee-certified Great Place to Work.

Salary Range: $80,000 - $90,000

We determine compensation based on experience, demonstrated skills, and current market conditions at the time of hire to ensure fairness and consistency across roles. Actual compensation will depend on experience and may be adjusted based on skills, qualifications, and alignment with the scope and impact of the role.

Title: IT Associate Data Analyst
Description:
Eversource will not offer immigration-related sponsorship for this position.  Applicants who require immigration sponsorship—either now or in the future—should not apply.  This includes, but is not limited to, direct company sponsorship, listing Eversource as the employer of record on immigration documents, or any work authorization that requires company involvement or documentation (e.g., H-1B, OPT, STEM OPT, CPT, TN, J-1, O-1, etc.).


The IT Associate Data Analyst partners with Customer-facing business teams and IT delivery teams to unlock business insights through trusted, well-mapped, and well-understood data. Serving as a liaison between the business and IT, this role develops a strong working knowledge of Eversource Customer data ecosystem (Customer data products, Enterprise Data Platform (EDP) availability, and how data supports day-to-day business processes) and works closely with business partners to anticipate needs and translate business questions into clear data requirements, mapping logic, and testable acceptance criteria. The analyst is hands-on with data performing analysis, documenting data mappings/relationships and business rules, executing data testing and reconciliation from source through EDP and downstream consumption, and technically investigating data issues to drive resolution with engineering and application teams. The role also collaborates with Domain, Solution, and Enterprise IT Architects and delivery teams to define functional and technical requirements, evaluate workflow/process improvements, explore conceptual solution design options, and play a key role throughout the application lifecycle for small and large development efforts.

Essential Functions: 

Partner with business and technology stakeholders to understand objectives, processes, and decision needs; translate them into clear analytical and data requirements.

Elicit, document, and manage functional requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria aligned to data products and Enterprise Data Platform (EDP) strategy.
Develop and maintain source-to-target data mappings, business rules, and data relationship/lineage documentation; ensure end-to-end traceability from requirements through business signoff.
Execute data validation and reconciliation (source-to-target, transformation logic, completeness, exception analysis) and support UAT/defect triage and regression checks as needed.
Investigate and triage data issues; isolate root cause and coordinate resolution with architects, data engineers, application owners, and delivery teams.
Lead and facilitate business/IT meetings; conduct workflow/process analysis, identify improvement opportunities, and support solution design, development, and implementation.
Support agile delivery by preparing/refining backlogs (epics/features/stories) and partnering with product owners and teams on prioritization, sprint planning, and grooming.
Develop business cases, estimates, and value tracking to support investment decisions; present as needed to governance groups (e.g., PMO/Finance/BPC).
Create and maintain enablement materials (definitions, dataset usage guidance, FAQs, job aids/training) to promote consistent, responsible self-service use of Domain data.
Support vendor/procurement activities (RFP/RFI) and contribute to statements of work when applicable.





Technical Knowledge/Skill/Education/Licenses/Certifications:

Technical Knowledge/Skill: 

Enable business users to find, understand, and use Customer data responsibly by documenting data definitions, metric logic, and dataset guidance (e.g., FAQs/common issues).

Elicit and document data/analytics requirements (user stories, acceptance criteria) and translate business questions into data definitions and testable outcomes.
Perform hands-on data analysis (profiling, trends/outliers, reconciliation) to support insights and troubleshoot data quality issues.
Create and validate source-to-target mappings, business rules, and lineage/relationship documentation (Source → EDP → downstream consumption).
Execute data validation and testing (source-to-target checks, transformation logic verification, completeness/exception analysis) and support UAT/defect triage.
Use SQL (or equivalent querying) and Excel to validate data, reconcile results, and produce repeatable analysis.
Understand data flows and integration concepts (pipelines, latency, transformations) and how upstream changes impact downstream reporting.
Triage data issues with technical evidence, isolate likely root cause (source anomaly vs. mapping/transformation vs. timing/integration), and coordinate resolution with engineering and application teams.
Communicate findings clearly to technical and non-technical audiences; facilitate discussions to align on definitions, assumptions, and next steps.
Comfortable working in agile delivery teams and using common tools (e.g., Jira/Azure DevOps) to manage requirements, testing artifacts, and issues.
Maintain clear, organized documentation and follow data/IT standards and controls as applicable (definitions, mappings, test evidence, issue notes).

Communicate findings clearly to technical and non-technical audiences; facilitate discussions to align on definitions, assumptions, and next steps.
Comfortable working in agile delivery teams and using common tools (e.g., Jira/Azure DevOps) to manage requirements, testing artifacts, and issues.
Maintain clear, organized documentation and follow data/IT standards and controls as applicable (definitions, mappings, test evidence, issue notes).


     Education:    

Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience.                       


    Experience:  

Zero (0) to three (3) years demonstrated application of Business Analysis skills.

Prior experience in the utility industry is a plus.                        




Licenses & Certifications:  

IIBA Entry Certificate in Business Analysis (ECBA)
IIBA Certification of Competency in Business Analysis (CCBA)
IIBA Certified Business Analyst Professional (CBAP)
IIBA Agile Analysis Certification (AAC)
IQBBA Certified Foundation Level Business Analyst (CFLBA)
IREB Certified Professional for Requirements Engineering (CPRE)
PMI Professional in Business Analysis (PBA)
SAFe POPM



Working Conditions:

Must be available to work emergency restoration assignment as required.
Must be available to travel between MA/CT/NH as necessary.



Mental Aspects:

Work includes researching, analyzing, writing and public speaking. 
This role works in an environment that often requires the performance of multiple simultaneous activities, where multiple competing deadlines need to be met and work is performed under pressure while involving significant business commitments and results. 
Occasional overtime may be needed.





Competencies:

Build trusting relationships

Manage and develop people

Foster teamwork and cross-functional collaboration

Lead change

Communicate strategic vision

Create an engaged workforce

Focus on the customer

Take ownership & accountability






Compensation and Benefits:


Eversource offers a competitive total rewards program. Check out our careers site for an overview of our benefits programs. Salary is commensurate with your experience. This position is eligible for a potential incentive. The annual salary range for this position is:
$81,000.00-$90,000.00





Worker Type:
Regular





Number of Openings:
1





Emergency Response:


Responding to emergency situations to meet customers’ needs is part of every employee’s role. If employed, you will be given an Emergency Restoration assignment.  This means you may be called to assist during an emergency outside of your normal responsibilities, work hours and location. 






EEO Statement:


Eversource Energy is an Equal Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employer.  All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, religion, disability status, or protected veteran status.

Title: Senior Associate - Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

We are currently seeking a senior associate data analyst to help us collect, transform and extract meaning from data for the Global Technology Services (GTS) Control Tower team at State Street. The position performs a wide range of activities with experts from GTS, business intelligence, business analysis, data engineering, process engineering, governance, and data analysts to collaboratively solve a wide array of complex technical problems.

About The GTS Control Tower Team

The GTS Control Tower team is an exciting and growing area of State Street with unique insight into the whole spectrum of our data, metrics, and dashboards at State Street. This name change reflects the work we are actively doing across GTS: integrating dependency management, business analysis, process design, and governance to transform data into action and keep remediation efforts flying smoothly.

 

The GTS Control Tower Mission

Our core focus is on transforming data into actionable insights. Our goal is to enable GTS Leadership to accelerate progress against key strategic initiatives across GTS and to drive results.  

Core Responsibilities


Report & Dashboard Development: Design, develop, and maintain high‑quality, accessible interactive Power BI dashboards and reports that clearly communicate key performance indicators (KPIs) and business insights.
Data Modeling & Transformation: Build and optimize semantic data models, including relationships, calculated columns, and measures using DAX. Perform data extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) using Python and Power Query (M).
Data Source Integration: Connect, consolidate, and manage data from multiple sources such as SQL databases, Azure services, Excel, and other enterprise systems into reliable, well‑structured datasets.
Performance Optimization: Tune data models and DAX calculations to ensure optimal performance, scalability, and user experience.
Security & Platform Administration: Implement and maintain row‑level security (RLS), manage Power BI workspaces and apps, and support capacity and governance best practices within the Power BI Service.
Stakeholder Collaboration: Partner with business stakeholders to gather requirements, translate business needs into analytical solutions, and deliver actionable, decision‑ready insights.
Documentation & Standards: Create and maintain clear documentation for data models, transformation logic, report definitions, and operational procedures to support transparency and long‑term maintainability.



What We Value


Willingness to learn and develop new skills, embracing challenges and continuous improvement
Strong work ethic, focus on achieving goals, and delivering measurable results
Desire for creative thinking, problem-solving, and proposing new ideas to improve processes or products
People who thrive in a collaborative team environment



Requirements


An online portfolio with examples with nonconfidential data that demonstrates your analysis skills
Some experience with Microsoft Power BI, DAX, Power Query, M, ETL, Python, PySpark, SQL
A Bachelor's degree in a data analytics, data science, computer science or equivalent experience



Salary Range

$52,000 - $91,000 Annual

The range quoted above applies to the role in the primary location specified. If the candidate would ultimately work outside of the primary location above, the applicable range could differ.

Employees are eligible to participate in State Street’s comprehensive benefits program, which includes: our retirement savings plan (401K) with company match; insurance coverage including basic life, medical, dental, vision, long-term disability, and other optional additional coverages; paid-time off including vacation, sick leave, short term disability, and family care responsibilities; access to our Employee Assistance Program; incentive compensation including eligibility for annual performance-based awards (excluding certain sales roles subject to sales incentive plans); and, eligibility for certain tax advantaged savings plans.

For a full overview, visit https://hrportal.ehr.com/statestreet/Home.

About State Street

Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success.

We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future.

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law.

Discover more information on jobs at StateStreet.com/careers

Read our CEO Statement

Job Application Disclosure

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.
